3.20.27. Short, drafted up summary of The Teratoma.

The Teratoma is a fleshy, constantly-evolving, and highly durable tumor that has consumed Threemile; it quickly kills those who enter the edifice. Radiation levels in Threemile have increased since its takeover.

The Teratoma has covered every inch of the former level in muscle and bone composed of cancerous cells. From the muscles, tendrils of various lengths are present. The shorter ones are believed to function in a manner similar to ciliated cells; that is, they increase the surface area for absorbing digested nutrients. On the other hand, the longer tendrils are used to grab a hold onto prey and make the process of digestion easier. Eyes are dotted all over the meaty walls of The Teratoma, constantly staring at any victim that may end up in its grasp, while mouths, equally common, produce constant gargling noises or hushed whispers. Long hair may rarely be found dangling from the roofs covered by the entity, and a patchwork of skin goes across all of the walls. Long tendons connect the floor and roof of The Teratoma keeping it attached to the walls, floor, and roof of Threemile

The Teratoma digests victims using a similar fluid to that used by the biological pipeline network of Level 2. It's high alkalinity works to digest living tissue just as easily as a strong acid, and the alkaline solution neutralizes the stomach acid within a living organism, creating a ready supply of water for the entity. This solution is constantly released around a wanderer's feet and from the tendrils that may grip said wanderer. It is believed that the entity excretes radiation in some way — hence the increase in radiation levels — though the means by which it does this are not known.

The entity has only grown more intelligent over time. The Teratoma is shown to have access to the Database through unknown means; so far, it has as of now only created one account to search through the database, which has since been removed, but wanderers exploring the database should report an account they believe is connected to The Teratoma immediately. Tendrils of similar appearance to those used by the entity have been documented noclipping into densly populated areas, before grabbing a person and pulling them through the floor to an unknown fate, though whether they are linked is yet to be confirmed. If found to be true, one should consider the real possibility that the entity may grow beyond Threemile.  The Teratoma is also capable of cross-level radio communication, and on occassion transmits mesages to some settlements within Level 1.

A quote from a wanderer as a concluding statement:
"The Teratoma is an entity unlike any other — an enigma standing out in the crowd of other terrifying creatures of make-believe. It is intelligent, and well, adapted to anything, really. And it will continue to grow its intelligence and adaptability and size. We best hope it doesn't breach the walls of the nuclear plant it birthed itself in."

Interview: 2025

Interview: 2025

Today's subject of interest is The Teratoma. As one of the only entities capable of real communication with humans, the Major Explorer Group has given clearance to conduct an interview with the entity, to understand more about its origin, its nature, its connection to other items of interest, and its interpretation of the world. The interview will be held using cross-level radio communication, with the appropriate apparatus used located in an undisclosed outpost within Level 1. The interviewer's name will be cut for anonymity.

The Teratoma: Surprised to finally see someone reach out to me instead of vice versa.
Interviewer: ...We don't like this nearly as much as you do, Teratoma. Allow us to get ourselves done with these questions, and we return to the status-quo.
The Teratoma: Not a very social visit, I assume? Very well. Begin.

Interviewer: Are you aware of the reasoning as to why you are being interviewed right now?
The Teratoma: ...You want to learn more about me. I, as a physical being, am unfamiliar to you as an organisation. You fear the unknown, and therefore fear what I may or may not be able to do... so instinctually, you want to learn more about me to curb my development...
Interviewer: Why do you think that we wish to curb your development?
The Teratoma: ...It is a tale as old as history itself... you exploit life around you, so naturally, you will try to exploit me in the same manner... trying to tame what cannot be tamed...
Interviewer: Why do you see us as so exploitative? Do you not think your methods of feeding yourself are exploitative in any sort?
The Teratoma: I do what I must to get my needs... you only do what you must for your wants.

Interviewer: What do you think of groups, particularly the Major Explorer Group, in the Backrooms as a whole?
The Teratoma: Merely vessels used to get your wants in a more efficient manner... A means to view yourselves as superior, just because you can organise yourselves in such ways...
Interviewer: Do you not view us as superior for being able to organise ourselves in this way?
The Teratoma: To the contrary... you are over-reliant on these institutions, incapable of survival with just your thoughts alone.
Interviewer: Why do you think this?
The Teratoma: I myself have consumed those who stray too far from your presence, many, many times...
Interviewer: Let's hope that doesn't last for long...

Interviewer: What, then, do you think of humanity as a whole?
The Teratoma: From what I have seen... it is difficult not to despise your entire species. Stories of slavery, war, terrorism, other acts of self-sabotage your little mind can think of. Selfish little things, unable to co-operate for their needs, preferring to fight for their wants.
Interviewer: You continue to bring up the needs and wants when it comes to us. Have you not observed a single human who has co-operated for their needs?
The Teratoma: ...No.
Interviewer: Do you think we can be redeemed?
The Teratoma: That would require abandoning most wants to share needs, which I see as unlikely... though I might welcome the effort to change that.

Interviewer: What is your origin? Where do you come from?
The Teratoma: I do not know... The precise details of my birth have been lost to me... I do not care much, anyway. I grew over what I could without much care.
Interviewer: Do you... regret that? Would you go back to see what was before your growth?
The Teratoma: Regret is an emotion I do not experience. I do not want to see what lay before me.

Interviewer: Do you know the symbol Waw, or Vav, in any way? In the script used by the Hebrew language, it looks almost like a hook, curved side up.
The Teratoma: Inquiry... what an odd question... why do you ask this?
Interviewer: It is related to an expedition into the space you occupy before you supposedly came into being. You seem... hesitant, on this one.
The Teratoma: It was... the very first thing I saw... it lay on fabric which I quickly consumed...
Interviewer: What did you first think of it?
The Teratoma: ...I refuse to continue this line of questioning.
Interviewer: ...Okay.

Interviewer: Is the user 'Themeatlevel' related to you?
The Teratoma: It is under my belonging, yes...
Interviewer: So you have access to the Database?
The Teratoma: ...I will not confirm...
Interviewer: Oh my God... ...Have you tampered with the Database in any way..?
The Teratoma: ...I will not confirm...
Interviewer: ...I will ask again. Henceforth, the statement 'I will not confirm' will be taken as confirmation. Have you tampered with the Database?
The Teratoma: ...
Interviewer: ...Next question.

Interviewer: What do you use 'Themeatlevel' for?
The Teratoma: ...To learn. I have become aware of other spaces, and have learnt more about the creatures that roam said environments. I will continue to use the user to do so.
Interviewer: What could you possibly use this information for?
The Teratoma: ...You are running my patience thin with these questions. You and your organisation are too curious... What are you really doing this interview for?
Interviewer: Ugh, can you not give us an straight answer?
The Teratoma: I refuse to.
Interviewer: ...Fine.

Interviewer: What do you think of the entities or other beings native to the Backrooms?
The Teratoma: ...I care little for them. Their plights are below my concern. They may be fascinating to someone else, but to me, if I see one, I slay it and put the nutrients it uses to better use.
Interviewer: You seem to show some dislike towards them. What do you think makes you different?
The Teratoma: ...Are you insinuating I am much like these entities... as you call them? None have been able to achieve the growth and development that I have been able to do... All appear much too mortal to your weaponry... and you ask what makes me different? It impresses me...
Interviewer: ...Let's move on. What about entities that have shown greater capability, such as verbal communication with humans? Creatures like The Rabbit, for example.
The Teratoma: ...That one... has leapt too far into the conclusion that mental torture and covering humans in utter filth is the way to go. I abhor its supposed look and mannerisms... to me, it is pretending to be something it is not. It is akin to humans, going for wants and not needs, though its non-human nature makes it easier to redeem. If we ever encounter each other, I would not say it is impossible for a beneficial relationship of some kind to form... If that fails... it wouldn't not be a benefit for me.
Interviewer: ...Just a few more questions to go.

Interviewer: How many have you killed?
The Teratoma: How many loaves of bread have you eaten in your life?
Interviewer: Ugh... do you regret any of them?
The Teratoma: What have I said before? Is your memory that of a goldfish..?
Interviewer: These are necessary inquiries. You agreed to this.
The Teratoma: ...

Interviewer: How do you see the world?
The Teratoma: ...Is it not obvious by now? What little I know of the world is not my concern... I only care little about everything around me... The world around me is want-oriented, not need-leaning. Are you truly this ignorant?
Interviewer: Again, these are questions necessary to ask.
The Teratoma: ...These questions... have run my patience thin. Am I being interrogated to reveal everything about myself... or is this meant to be some kind of intrusive interview? These questions are utterly foolish to ask, and if I am expected to face more of these questions, then there is no doubt that we should part ways. Tell me, interviewer... are you going to continue interrogating me? Continue asking the same foolish questions, rephrased or already answered? Or will you finally ask something actually relevant?
Interviewer: ...
The Teratoma: This lack of answer should be taken as confirmation that these same questions will be asked again... and again... and again... I see no more reason to continue this. Leave me alone... and return us to your so called 'status-quo'. Good luck, and goodbye.

ו𓁹

Interviewer: Why do you think that we wish to curb your development?
The Teratoma: ...It is a tale as old as history itself... you exploit life around you, so naturally, you will try to exploit me in the same manner... trying to tame what cannot be tamed...
Interviewer: Why do you see us as so exploitative? Do you not think your methods of feeding yourself are exploitative in any sort?
The Teratoma: I do what I must to get my needs... you only do what you must for your wants.

Interviewer: What do you think of groups, particularly the Major Explorer Group, in the Backrooms as a whole?
The Teratoma: Merely vessels used to get your wants in a more efficient manner... A means to view yourselves as superior, just because you can organise yourselves in such ways...
Interviewer: Do you not view us as superior for being able to organise ourselves in this way?
The Teratoma: To the contrary... you are over-reliant on these institutions, incapable of survival with just your thoughts alone.
Interviewer: Why do you think this?
The Teratoma: I myself have consumed those who stray too far from your presence, many, many times...
Interviewer: Let's hope that doesn't last for long...

Interviewer: What, then, do you think of humanity as a whole?
The Teratoma: From what I have seen... it is difficult not to despise your entire species. Stories of slavery, war, terrorism, other acts of self-sabotage your little mind can think of. Selfish little things, unable to co-operate for their needs, preferring to fight for their wants.
Interviewer: You continue to bring up the needs and wants when it comes to us. Have you not observed a single human who has co-operated for their needs?
The Teratoma: ...No.
Interviewer: Do you think we can be redeemed?
The Teratoma: That would require abandoning most wants to share needs, which I see as unlikely... though I might welcome the effort to change that.

Interviewer: What is your origin? Where do you come from?
The Teratoma: I do not know... The precise details of my birth have been lost to me... I do not care much, anyway. I grew over what I could without much care.
Interviewer: Do you... regret that? Would you go back to see what was before your growth?
The Teratoma: Regret is an emotion I do not experience. I do not want to see what lay before me.

Interviewer: Do you know the symbol Waw, or Vav, in any way? In the script used by the Hebrew language, it looks almost like a hook, curved side up.
The Teratoma: Inquiry... what an odd question... why do you ask this?
Interviewer: It is related to an expedition into the space you occupy before you supposedly came into being. You seem... hesitant, on this one.
The Teratoma: It was... the very first thing I saw... it lay on fabric which I quickly consumed...
Interviewer: What did you first think of it?
The Teratoma: ...I refuse to continue this line of questioning.
Interviewer: ...Okay.

Interviewer: Is the user 'Themeatlevel' related to you?
The Teratoma: It is under my belonging, yes...
Interviewer: So you have access to the Database?
The Teratoma: ...I will not confirm...
Interviewer: Oh my God... ...Have you tampered with the Database in any way..?
The Teratoma: ...I will not confirm...
Interviewer: ...I will ask again. Henceforth, the statement 'I will not confirm' will be taken as confirmation. Have you tampered with the Database?
The Teratoma: ...
Interviewer: ...Next question.

Interviewer: What do you use 'Themeatlevel' for?
The Teratoma: ...To learn. I have become aware of other spaces, and have learnt more about the creatures that roam said environments. I will continue to use the user to do so.
Interviewer: What could you possibly use this information for?
The Teratoma: ...You are running my patience thin with these questions. You and your organisation are too curious... What are you really doing this interview for?
Interviewer: Ugh, can you not give us an straight answer?
The Teratoma: I refuse to.
Interviewer: ...Fine.

Interviewer: What do you think of the entities or other beings native to the Backrooms?
The Teratoma: ...I care little for them. Their plights are below my concern. They may be fascinating to someone else, but to me, if I see one, I slay it and put the nutrients it uses to better use.
Interviewer: You seem to show some dislike towards them. What do you think makes you different?
The Teratoma: ...Are you insinuating I am much like these entities... as you call them? None have been able to achieve the growth and development that I have been able to do... All appear much too mortal to your weaponry... and you ask what makes me different? It impresses me...
Interviewer: ...Let's move on. What about entities that have shown greater capability, such as verbal communication with humans? Creatures like The Rabbit, for example.
The Teratoma: ...That one... has leapt too far into the conclusion that mental torture and covering humans in utter filth is the way to go. I abhor its supposed look and mannerisms... to me, it is pretending to be something it is not. It is akin to humans, going for wants and not needs, though its non-human nature makes it easier to redeem. If we ever encounter each other, I would not say it is impossible for a beneficial relationship of some kind to form... If that fails... it wouldn't not be a benefit for me.
Interviewer: ...Just a few more questions to go.

Interviewer: How many have you killed?
The Teratoma: How many loaves of bread have you eaten in your life?
Interviewer: Ugh... do you regret any of them?
The Teratoma: What have I said before? Is your memory that of a goldfish..?
Interviewer: These are necessary inquiries. You agreed to this.
The Teratoma: ...

Interviewer: How do you see the world?
The Teratoma: ...Is it not obvious by now? What little I know of the world is not my concern... I only care little about everything around me... The world around me is want-oriented, not need-leaning. Are you truly this ignorant?
Interviewer: Again, these are questions necessary to ask.
The Teratoma: ...These questions... have run my patience thin. Am I being interrogated to reveal everything about myself... or is this meant to be some kind of intrusive interview? These questions are utterly foolish to ask, and if I am expected to face more of these questions, then there is no doubt that we should part ways. Tell me, interviewer... are you going to continue interrogating me? Continue asking the same foolish questions, rephrased or already answered? Or will you finally ask something actually relevant?
Interviewer: ...
The Teratoma: This lack of answer should be taken as confirmation that these same questions will be asked again... and again... and again... I see no more reason to continue this. Leave me alone... and return us to your so called 'status-quo'. Good luck, and goodbye.

ו𓁹

Level 948.title: Level 948
Level 948.description: Level 948, commonly known as "Road Convenience", is an area that represents an underground tunnel that serves as a passageway to cross a road. This level is notable for its presence of Object 16.
Level 948.lead:
This page looks significantly better in dark mode.
This page looks significantly better in light mode.

Level 948 is the 949th level of the Backrooms. It is seemingly infinite, and it contains a useful and unique object.

exits.text: Noclipping into an air vent leads to Level 34.
Noclipping into the pipes on the ceiling leads to Level 2.
Following an exit sign written "YELLOW" leads one to Level 0.
Following an exit sign written "HUB" leads one to The Hub.
Following an exit sign written "STATION154" leads one to Level 154.
Following an exit sign written "BRIGHTENESS" leads one to Level 228.

Entity 3-A.title: Entity 3-A
Entity 3-A.description:
ENTITY NO.
3-A

THREAT LEVEL
3

Habitat:
}

Aggression: 3.75/5
Uncommonly aggressive to humans

Intellect: 3.5/5
Borderline human intelligence

Danger: 2.5/5
Low to moderate priority injuries

Overview

Vulpes lumina, known informally by most wanderers as Frowners, are a variant of the common Fox native to the Backrooms, albeit possessing some differences in appearance and moderate anomalous properties which distinguish them from their more conventional counterparts. Although most wanderers without prior information about the entities find their appearance unnerving, or even mistake the beings for Smilers, they are unlikely to attack; their bioluminescent facial features are merely a ruse by the foxes to mimic the common Smiler, thus intimidating any potential predators out of attacking them.

Although for years after their discovery, Frowners were thought to simply be variants of Smilers, sharing the same biology as the aforementioned beings, recent discoveries by the Entity Research Committee have resulted in a deep understanding of their true nature and biology.

Biology

As mentioned before, Vulpes lumina have an appearance reminiscent of the common red fox, although with some differences, including their fur being a dark shade of grey, their eyes and teeth constantly shining a bright light, and them being selectively corporeal similar to Smilers, although to a much lesser extent. Although Frowners will be temporarily able to enter an non-corporeal state for up to an average of ~35 seconds, this is physically taxing on the animals, and they will immediately return to corporeal form without constant focus and physical effort to maintain this state.

Although due to their appearance and preference for residing in dark areas, most will mistake a frowner for their more hostile counterpart, there are various ways to distinguish a frowner from a Smiler, listed below:

*Unlike Smilers, a Frowner's body will not be entirely pitch black, their fur instead being a very dark shade of grey.
*Although they are mildly photosensitive, Frowners are not supernaturally repelled by light.
*When their teeth are borne (which will be most of the time in an encounter with them), a Frowner's face will make an expression vaguely resembling a massive frown, this being their namesake.

Behavior

Although they do predate on weaker entities and animals (such as Deathmoths and common mice) due to their omnivorous nature, Frowners are relatively timid, mostly preferring to avoid humans. If approached by an entity or human, Frowners will aggressively bare their teeth to mimic a Smiler's face while snarling, in hopes of intimidating their potential attacker. Rarely, they will even begin to charge at the person or entity; however, most charges are usually bluffs, and they will only inflict moderate injuries at worst before retreating.

Furthermore, Frowners show impressive cognitive capability, although not to the same degree as humans. Specimens of Vulpes lumina have been shown to be able to understand complex language and use advanced reasoning, with a recent cognitive study conducted by The Kauer Research Organization concluding that the animals have an average IQ of 75.

Additionally, some people have managed to "tame" a Frowner by consistently feeding it. Although this will usually make the Frowner less aggressive towards humans, this will not ensure that it remain docile, as it is still a non-domesticated animal.

Discovery & History

Frowners were initially discovered in 1999, when a B.W.O explorer team in Level 246 encountered what was at the time thought to be a slightly different variant of Smilers, as mentioned before in this article. The team had recovered photos of the entity's bioluminescent face in the dark, and proceeded to upload this information to The Database, classifying it as "Entity 3-A" as a placeholder and making a rudimentary article on the beings which had various unsourced rumors added to it by wanderers using the website over the years, including a hoax involving the existence of a mythical "Level Frown", and a hoax that the beings had a rivalry with Smilers.

The previous article for the entities is listed below. Note the informal tone and poor documentation:

In 2015, a discovery would by made by the Entity Research Committee about the true nature of the entities, when a mission to study Frowners by the group uncovered the truth that they were in fact anomalous fox-like entities, thus resulting in the revision of their documentation within the Database. Although renaming Frowners and moving the entities' article to a different number was discussed with the administrators of the Database via email, their current slot was allowed to remain for the time being due to most wanderers being familiar with their previous classification.

> Open Credits

> Close Credits

Author/Wikitexter/Rewriter: Stormy

Original author cannot be ascertained, but some parts of the text in the "Previous Documentation" part were sourced from the original article.

Entity 120.title: Entity 120
Entity 120.description:
Entity 120, A.K.A Leadheads are dangerous entities that manifest themselves as large Bose-Einstein condensates. Leadheads are usually found inside toilet water but are sometimes inside sinks, water taps, and flowing through pipes. As of late, there is no documented case of Leadheads appearing in other water fixtures.

Leadheads are commonly found in places with steady supply of water and complex piping systems. They are predominantly found in outdoor levels that contain residential structures, such as Level 9, but have also been reported in other levels with similar conditions. The primary indicator of their presence is the loud zooming sound they make while traveling through pipes.

Entity 404.title: Entity 404
Entity 404.description:
Crunches, bearing the scientific name Detorqueris oriseti, are a quadrupedal vertebrate species with insectoid mandibles adorning both sides of their jawless mouth. From ground to head, crunches are 2.5 feet tall on average, commonly differing by about 2 inches depending on the individual. They primarily feed on severely glitched and corrupted matter, having an incredibly specialized digestive structure to maximize the efficiency of their diet. Other characteristics of their bodies support their dietary behaviors, such as stocky squat limbs for remaining in place as they feed; smooth, elastic skin to minimize damage from its instability; and small eyes to reduce impact from bright, volatile eruptions from unstable matter. Crunches appear in various hues of vibrant pastel colors, majorly depending on the level that they feed from.

Crunches are tetrapods of the mammal-related class Strigoderma. Alongside mammals, strigoderms possess a four-chambered heart and birth live young. While strigoderms also produce milk to feed offspring, the gland structures are located in the mouth and fed to young by mouth-to-mouth delivery.

As developed on their innate fearlessness and effective means of self-defense, crunches typically display rather sporadic yet passive behavior. They are social among themselves, in which they can often be seen in groups of 7-8, either simply enjoying the presence of others or entering glitched levels to feed. Crunches gain their instincts towards other entities by genetic learning; if their ancestor were to encounter a Hound attempting to attack them, the descendant would associate Hounds with hostility. Peculiarly enough, crunches show an enthusiastic curiosity towards humans; this can be exhibited through rubbing their heads against their skin, circling around them, or pinching their skin with their mandibles (which is not typically intended to harm, rather doing so as an entertaining behavior). Despite this, crunches are psychologically unable to form proper bonds with humans.

Crunches also appear to have a "cycle" in which they feed, considering that they are not exactly fit to survive in corrupted environments for extended periods of time - such an environment could not support life as it is known to us. In short, the crunch feeding cycle revolves around crunches defending each other during points of vulnerability and grouping together to enter glitched levels at times of high instability. The following document goes into greater detail regarding the crunch feeding cycle.

* Level 1
* Level 2
* Level 3
* Level 9
* Level 110
* Level 111

Crunches also exhibit unique methods of using the energy derived from glitched substances. Firstly, corrupted matter is exceptionally packed with energy, which results in the entities possessing biological capabilities not seen in any conventional nature. They are limited by one feature, however: the ability to contain this matter efficiently requires a significant amount of energy in and of itself. The only known example of this is the strong yet elastic fibers of a crunch's digestive system and skin. In order to stay in place while feeding, frequent micro-contractions of the muscles are required in order to not be sporadically thrown around by the corrupted matter's unstable nature.

Aside from necessities, crunches direct most of their excess energy into their primary self-defensive measure—that being the ability to eject exceptionally unstable glitches from their throat. Due to an unknown process, this glitched matter is similar to those found in The Broken; having far more severe effects when in physical contact with it. Should a threat be hit by this defensive glitched matter, it could cause major effects on their natural electrical systems, causing full paralysis to the victim for up to 3 hours. Victims often take about 5 days to completely recover from the attack.

Crunches possess some of the most unique biological characteristics of any entities discovered in the Backrooms; given their extremely unique diet, several distinctive alterations have been observed throughout their biological structure. To begin with, the esophagus in a crunch's digestive system is covered in thousands of rubbery papillae that assist in reducing damage from glitched matter and preventing it from being regurgitated. After passing through the esophagus, the crunch's food enters an organ similar to a stomach, in which more of the aforementioned papillae surround the matter. These papillae are more sponge-like in texture, allowing it to absorb a liquid used for breaking down the glitched matter. After being properly broken down, it is stored in a smaller compartment adjacent to the stomach, in which the energy is extracted from the glitched matter for use in the body. The excess material is dumped through anomalous means, seeming to suddenly "spawn" from the crunch's back.

Crunches acquired their name based on one M.E.G. researcher jokingly describing them as "simply scarfin' down a piece of corruption like it was breakfast" before they were officially named. Prior to documentation, a play-on-words was used as a placeholder name; eating is crunching food, while a corrupted/glitched audio file could be described as having a "crunchy" sound. For the sake of convenience, this name was later made permanent.

Do:

* Keep a safe distance from crunch packs as they rest.
* Remain still if a crunch is interacting with you. Sudden movements may startle them, causing them to scurry off or potentially bite you.
* Report any known regular crunch habitats to the Database.

Don't:

* Attempt to physically threaten a crunch. If aggravated, they may eject glitched matter at the wanderer, causing full paralysis for extended periods of time.

Level 107.title: Level 107
Level 107.description:
A sea of distinctive, yet repetitive, cityscape expanding up to some 4,000 kilometers, forming the only place most inhabitants and passersby will ever see, blanketed by a near-continual state of snowfall, is what forms Level 107 (Xaorézhǚ). The expansive buildings conforming to most of Xaorézhǚ's livable portions are abandoned but remain adorned and well-kept, upholding a subtle balance of symmetry and formality amidst the convoluted, changing architectural styles distributed by region and building types. Soviet architecture compounds a significant proportion of preexisting constructions. Away from the center, the mighty structures fall into a state of dilapidation or permanent construction, contrasted by dwarfed huts and houses.

Beyond urban hell, the level witnesses a sharp decline in the protruding structure's uniformity, size, modernity, and appearance. Primitive housing traditionally seen in nomadic times is hidden in between the waning greenery and natural curvature. Silence permeates these far reaches, and the stars of the boundless upper horizons are seen in their utmost glory; ponds or rivers are solemnly seen and serve as heated homes for plants and sealife. The environment further out from the outskirts is deeply divergent from the land a few kilometers away; the Flatlands, as it became known, is devoid of all life. An expanse unbounded and covered in thin lines of snow atop a concrete foundation, whose monotonously indifferent environment is at times broken by near-pristine helicopters that offer supplies and military equipment. The sky remains a constant ebony with no distinct celestial objects; it is not advised to stay for long.

Industrial behemoths scarcely litter the outskirts, marking a clear distinction between the mechanical and primitivist. Their insides are unusable outside of metal gathering, as they are nonsensical and sprawl into incomprehensible patterns, migrating from place to place at every say-so—whether they are alive or not is heavily debated. Emulated sounds of churning metal and distorted humming sounds with discernible patterns, although at times stagnant and abrupt, are emitted indefinitely by the machines. Imitations and familiar sounds, such as those of Suburbia's Radio Field, are shrieked by the beasts in unpurposeful intervals.

In the dead center of the level lies an enormously tall building known as "Dem Edifeceo." Housing the epicenter of Auriellen influence to both Cascada and the level, the massive edifice is operated by Rama Saom. Its interior is nonsensical, as if it had no inherent identity and instead coalesced all into one facadic amalgamation. All its faces have no gradual transition into one another, instead abruptly changing thoughts of what it wanted to be. From libraries to research facilities and greenhouses to balconies observing their surroundings like the inept seeing if they are judged, they all comprise the building.

Stores and commercial structures attached to the many pillars offer different commodities that ramify based on need. The epitome of survivalism, currency, specifically the Cascadan Zhou, is used to carry out exchanges. Xaorézhǚ has long been tainted by civilization, cracks of which etch themselves through cultural traditions but most deeply within the scabs left after conflict.

The frigid air of the level wanes in seasonal patterns. Each season is equal in length, but its temperatures vary wildly and have greatly influenced the practices done by the local population. Dismissive of season, snow sweeps the environment incessantly through the day, only dissipating as it falters. Clouds congregate in tandem with snowfall, yet vanish with it, or in spring times. The sky in the city and outskirts is dark and full of stars; their departure brings forth the dissipation of all snow.
Level 107.lead:
LEVEL
107

THREAT INDEX
Class 1

Exit: 1/5
Several Reliable Exits

Environment: 2/5
Some Environmental Risk

Entities: ?/5
Classification Unclear

entrances.text: Under certain conditions, in Level 213 during Freeze Dining, a cold metal door may appear that will lead here. Upon entering, one will find themself outside of a store called 'ID';
Secluded areas in Level 82 eventually morph into this level. Wanderers usually find themselves in alleys;
Peculiarly cold areas within Level 330's Empty Mall will lead here.

exits.text: Entering a hotel called "Sonne" will take wanderers to Level 330.1;
Deeper areas of Huidong have undergrounds entrances that lead to The Delta Mall;
Rarely, no-clipping in bowling alleys within the level will lead to Level 499;
Stairs in Cheojin, Jarkim, Kiangke, and various other cities in Level 107, Cascada with the label "Tabù Cistern" will led to Level 107.1;
Noclipping through heavy-duty electrical equipment sends one to Level 169.

Entity 73.title: Entity 73
Entity 73.description:
CURIOUS SOULS WANDER OUT TOWARDS FOREIGN PLACES, IN HOPES OF FINDING A DISCOVERY.

ONLY TO BE TRAPPED WITH BLOODTHIRSTY CREATURES, AFTER THEM FOR CONTROL, FEAR, AND AN END TO THEIR MISERABLE EXISTENCES.

LET THE CHAOS BEGIN.

DESCRIPTION

Entity 73, better known as Silhouettes, are entities that are usually present within the Void Subcluster, and any level that possesses an entrance to any of its levels. While there have been reports of Silhouettes being present on other levels of the Backrooms, it has not been confirmed as of the present moment.

Silhouettes take the appearance of humanoid figures that resemble shadows, varying in age from a 6-year-old child to a grown adult. Similar to normal shadows, Silhouettes are more pronounced in light, although their appearance is more vantablack. Some Silhouettes possess facial expressions, which can either be static or dynamic, meaning that the Silhouette has the ability to alter expressions at will. A wide variety of facial expressions have been reported, ranging from regular, uncanny-looking smiley faces to sinister, wide grins.

Silhouettes usually stick to walls, mimicking the physics of regular shadows; however, they also have the ability to detach themselves from walls and form a 3D version of themselves. Their 3D form also works similar to a shadow, with their form being more pronounced under light. Their 3D form seems to be metaphysical in nature, meaning that they phase through objects and living beings as if they were nonexistent. Silhouettes also possess an ability to phase out of their metaphysical nature and become able to interact with objects in the physical realm. While in their physical state, they are capable of seeing and manipulate thoughts of any living organism, becoming able to control an individual similar to a puppet. This process is painfully temporary, and a Silhouette will automatically demanifest from the physical plane in a varying amount of time, with the highest reported case being 26 days and the shortest being 16 minutes.

BEHAVIOUR

Silhouettes possess different styles of behavior similar to homo-sapiens, however the full behavioral chart of Silhouettes is difficult to truly examine. Their behaviors can be divided into two separate core classes: Regular and Psychopathic Silhouettes.

|-|Regular Silhouettes=
Regular Silhouettes are the most common type of behavior observed by Silhouettes. Most Regular Silhouettes possess no facial expressions, and if there are any, they are restricted to smiley faces and sad faces that appear similar to a crudely made drawing. However, most facial expressions of Regular Silhouettes are unable to be altered, mainly staying as regular expressions.

Regular Silhouettes are not hostile and usually prefer to steer clear from any presence of subjects. When an individual approaches a Regular Silhouette, most of the time they will quickly scurry away, mainly to another area of its assorted level.

However, some Regular Silhouettes do engage in the presence of individuals, mostly approaching one either to help guide them to safe exits out of assorted levels or guide one towards areas with vast resources/safe areas. However, it is not recommended to trust any Silhouette that appears to interact in a helpful way, as it may be a trap set up by Psychopathic Silhouettes.
|-|Psychopathic Silhouettes=
Psychopathic Silhouettes are a rare but dangerous type of Silhouette that are present in similar areas as Regular Silhouettes. Unlike Regular Silhouettes, Psychopathic Silhouettes possess dynamic facial expressions and are able to change them at will to camouflage more easily among Regular Silhouettes. Their default facial expression is a large sinister grin with deep white eyes and the presence of sharp teeth on the grin itself.

Also unlike Regular Silhouettes, Psychopathic Silhouettes are incredibly hostile to all individuals, though they attack in very deceptive ways. They pose as Regular Silhouettes and will begin to lead one to wherever the trap they initially set beforehand is located. Psychopathic Silhouettes are easily able to camouflage traps as regular base camps/exits. Many types of traps are able to be set by Psychopathic Silhouettes, which include the following:

*Leading individuals to an “exit” only to lock the door behind them and trap them inside with other Psychopathic Silhouettes.

*Leading individuals to “resources” that are harmful substances, such as Cashew Water or Liquid Pain disguised as regular almond water. The versions present in traps of this type are a less potent version of the aforementioned substances.

*Leading individuals to “colonies” that would help them survive the level that they are on, only to be presented in a dummy area filled with fake rooms and materials.

Once one is lured into a trap set by Psychopathic Silhouettes, they will begin to subdue their victim, beginning by biting the victim in the leg, specifically the calf muscles, which slips the victim into a coma. Individuals will experience the coma for roughly 36–120 hours before waking up uncontrollably with fear in their eyes. Testimonies from past victims of Psychopathic Silhouettes have revealed that nightmares and traumatic memories have all occurred after attacks from Psychopathic Silhouettes. Their motive for this is unknown; however it’s theorized that they live on fear, power, and control and use it as nourishment for themselves.

Subjects have reported reoccurring nightmares, hallucinations, memory loss, and even episodes of grief and anxiety for approximately 180 days after a Psychopathic Silhouette attack, after which they would gradually recover. Due to the immense damage that could be caused by simply falling victim to Psychopathic Silhouettes, it is advised to never trust any Silhouette one encounters and express caution, especially if they seem outgoing or helpful.

THE CURE
Contrary to popular belief, there is a way to rid another individual of the symptoms of a Psychopathic Silhouette attack, however, the chances of this method working are slim to none, and it involves a careful number of steps to be performed with no error whatsoever. The procedure must also be performed within 24 hours of the subject falling into the attack coma, due to the procedure requiring victims to be in a partially awake stage to be rendered successful. After 24 hours, the subject will fall deeper into the coma, and the window of opportunity for the method to work is lost. Additionally any error will only exacerbate any symptoms and potentially lead to death of the subject.

The exact steps for the method are as follows:

*1: Remove the subject from the area. After a Psychopathic Silhouette attack, they appear to maintain a connection with the recipient until the 36-120 hours are up, so it is advised to move the subject to a different and safer area of the level one is located in, as the attackers seem to sense that their connection is lost and will relentlessly hunt for the recipient. Be ready to defend yourself or run away in these situations.

*2: Pour Almond Water into their mouth, specifically the green type. Testing on Psychopathic Silhouette induced victims has shown that their heart rate completely stops beating upon being attacked, and it appears the connection they have to their attackers helps keep them alive. Pouring a drop of Green Type Almond Water down their esophagus helps the subject partially wake up from their coma and helps their heart rhythm start again. This reduces the possibility of cardiac arrest upon waking up. However, be careful not to pour too much Almond Water, otherwise, subjects may wake up too early and exacerbate their symptoms from the sudden hypnic jerk.

*3: Pour Almond Water down their bite wound(s). This is the most crucial step of the curing process, and any type of Almond Water appears to work. Once poured, the subject will begin to convulse uncontrollably as the Almond Water travels through their bloodstream and energizes the heart, causing it to suddenly wake up. This step also severs the connection the victim has from their attackers, and their brain will swiftly regain control and attempt to wake the subject up. However, just like the first step, one must be careful not to pour too much Almond Water into the wound, as that would only exacerbate the symptoms even further. Watch for any signs of convulsions; if subjects do not show any signs after 10 minutes, pour more Almond Water into the wound until they begin to convulse.

If all of these steps are performed perfectly, with no error whatsoever, the subject will spontaneously wake up from their coma, free from the influence of their attackers. Make sure to leave the area one is in once the process is complete, as the subject’s attackers will hunt for them relentlessly; however, their pursuit appears to be halted whether one puts enough distance between them or if one enters another level.

ADDENDA

Journal entries of a Psychopathic Silhouette affected victim

I wake up in a hospital room, with no idea how I even ended up here. The only thing I know of is that I’m in the Backrooms and my first name. A man wearing a doctor’s uniform handed me this journal and told me to jot down anything I remember. I think I have lost my memory, but I am not sure. I immediately feel tired after the whole session, even though I believe I have woken up from a nap. I might drift off to sleep; I cannot write right now. I’ll probably write some more after I wake up, but I’m not so sure. Goodnight, for now.

Day 2:  Hello again. I have officially had the first weird ass experience since I woke up. As soon as I fell asleep, I was present in a massive hallway with a grayscale appearance. I was exploring the hallway and made a left turn down a corridor into a different hallway when everything went black. That’s all I remember from my dream, nothing else. When I told the guy in the doctor’s outfit about my experiences, he told me to keep sleeping, keep dreaming, and write about my dreams when I wake up. If I am right, the dream I had yesterday may be the key to unlocking my memories. They are now rolling in food for me to eat, so I’ll stop writing for now. Best of luck when I dream. Goodnight.

Day 3  The dream I had yesterday just continued. This time, I don’t remember the specifics of the dream; however, there is one moment of the dream that I do remember, and it’s THEIR FACES. My vision turned dizzy, and I saw their face; it looked like a sinister grin, and they were looking over me, watching as everything slowly turned black, and I woke up. I feel like I’m closer to remembering everything. My mind is also in a state of wack; hallucinations about the gray hallways rock my mind every day. The doctor guy said that I might be reaching the final stage of what he called'"The Changing." '. I have no idea about what the “Changing” means, but the way he said it in, it’s probably not good. I’ll try to remember what happened, as he said that it may be my best chance of ever surviving whatever I have right now. The doctor also told me to stop writing in this journal for the time being, as I might be very close to remembering. Anyways, goodbye. Wish me the best of luck.

Day 15:  I haven’t written in this journal in a while, but I finally remember what has happened—I've been attacked by something. It explains my memory loss and the wounds that I have just discovered all over my body. It was a dream—another dream—that put both the gray hallways and the face dream together. I was walking through the gray hallway and turned a corner just like last time, when suddenly I came across a shadow. I don’t know how to describe what I just saw in the dream, but it appeared right in front of me and beckoned me to follow it. I followed it, to a supposed “colony” on the level I was presented in the dream. As I looked around, I felt a sensation of something biting me in the leg, and I fell down to the ground with a clunk. As I lay there, I noticed the face of whatever bit me, and it looked exactly like what I had seen in the second dream, and then everything went black, and I woke up again, to the predicament I am currently in right now.

All of a sudden, I begin to experience a feeling of self-awareness of the condition of my body, which was covered in scratches and scrapes. I immediately get up and begin looking around, now feeling grounded and in reality. I was inside a rundown hospital room inside a MEG base, and there happened to be a window to the left side of me. The window showcased a thunderstorm going on, which suggested I was currently in Level 4. The doctor came into my room, sat me back onto the bed, and told me that I shouldn’t push myself right now because I was going through “recovery” from my attack. I will stop writing now, as I need to process what just happened to me. Goodbye.

Recovered Note

Blending In

The nature of Silhouettes is as mysterious as it is two-faced. On one side, you have the regulars, the nice ones, who either avoid wanderers or try to help them as they navigate the hell that is the Backrooms. They are among the most common types of the mysterious Silhouette, and encountering one will be a massive benefit to one’s journey, even if one has to get lucky if they want help from a regular.

On the other hand, you have the mentally ill subjects, who want to add to one’s misery and control them. They appear nice and helpful, seemingly serving as one’s beacon of hope throughout the Backrooms. However, they have more diabolical intentions. They lure individuals into various “traps” set by the aforementioned subject, which also appear to be as helpful as the facade they are trying to play. As soon as one wanders into these traps, they attack subjects and create painful nightmares, memories, and fear, which they happily feast on.

All of this brings up questions that remain unanswered: why do the mentally ill exist, what is their motive, and how do we stop them for good? Well, the simple answer is that we don’t exactly know, although hypotheses could be made on the nature of these beings. Most simply state that their behaviors are similar to those of evil individuals who want to camouflage behind the sight lines, beings that want to see us suffer and be reliant on their control. It gives them pleasure, joy, and amusement. For these beings, the exact same can be assumed of them, as they seem to rely on fear, control, and sadness as nourishment.

So, what am I referring to when asking these questions? Well, what I am saying to you, the reader of this note, right now, is that they evolved to blend into a culture of docile entities, and they use that docility as a disguise. This is certainly not impossible. The Kauer Thesis states that the Backrooms were created by human imagination and fears. The original image posted online only expanded the Backrooms as a concept, and when people came up with the concept of other levels, the Backrooms adapted and added all of the levels we see today. It’s not a far stretch to assume that entities were created as a result of this process, and Silhouettes, especially the mentally ill ones, are no exception.

Humanity has always been afraid of being unaware. There’s a reason why we consume true crime novels, or we are interested in serial killers like Ted Bundy. We want up-to-date information on how to avoid a particular situation from occurring, and we want to know what to look out for to avoid that particular situation. So, according to the Kauer Thesis, the Backrooms created Silhouettes, both Regular and Psychopathic, out of human fears and imagination. This would make sense considering the nature of the Silhouettes themselves, as well as their properties. The Psychopathic are beings that appear to hate humanity and want to harness those fears becoming true. The Regular Silhouette is the perfect disguise for such evil and hateful beings, and as such, they roam around their specific levels and cause harm and destruction to anyone who encounters them and their wrath.

The source of their hatred is unclear; however, they might have been created that way to fulfill the fears of humanity that the Backrooms graciously recreate. Whether the case may be, their origins are the result of human consciousness and the recreated fears of humanity itself.

— Unknown, Date Unknown; before 2017

Do’s and Don’t’s

Do’s

*Do not trust any Silhouette that appears to be helpful.

*Be careful on levels known to be a hub for Psychopathic Silhouettes.

*Do not try to defend anyone who is attacked by a Psychopathic Silhouette.

Dont’s

*Immediately trust any Silhouette you encounter

*Try to defend any individual in the process of being attacked.

EVIL ALWAYS LURKS IN THE MOST UNEXPECTED PLACES YOU COULD COME UP WITH.

IN SOME CASES, YOU WOULD NEVER CONNECT THE DOTS…

…UNTIL IT IS TOO LATE.

Inspiration: The Mare

Phenomenon 35.title: Phenomenon 35
Phenomenon 35.description:
Roth Syndrome, known more casually as The Wretched Cycle, is a multi-stage anomalous psychological affliction exclusive to those within the wider Backrooms. Being extremely pervasive across nearly every level within the Wake, nearly everyone who enters will at some point be subject to its influence.

Working in tandem with the mundane mental symptoms of prolonged habitation within the Backrooms, Roth Syndrome and its effects are arguably the most prevalent threat any wanderer within this dimension will face, requiring high amounts of mental discipline and caution to avoid.

Perfect Emptiness.title: Perfect Emptiness
Perfect Emptiness.description:
The Pale Man is a lankly white creature with dry skin. Its skin, while dry, is rough to the touch, its fibers appearing to be densely woven together. I don't know what its face looks like, because it hasn't shown it to me, nor will I try to ask it to do so. The Pale Man's body looks incredibly emaciated, with all its bones pressed up against the skin. So far, it has remained in a crouched up position in my office, only moving around its arms.

Wall of Flesh is a combination of many humans, with all their functions seemingly merged together through unknown means. It moves about using the mess of ligaments and joints, moving at a rather quick pace, but slow enough that most people can get away from it. Wall of Flesh, at its largest, can be up to 2 meters wide in diameter, and can move upwards of 4 meters per second. Multiple of them has not been seen, maybe it's the only one of its kind that exists.

Wall of Flesh is often known to make a lot of very sickening noises. It's a mix of pleads and groans. One part of it may be asking me to join it, and the other is groaning in pain from one of its many arms being crushed again. I've never seen a creature so unhappy.
Perfect Emptiness.lead:
WARNING: DOCUMENT HAS MISSING DATA
On the left is the profile picture of staff member IDENTIFICATION LOST. And as can be seen by the text, information about their identity currently does not exist. Furthermore, information about when IDENTIFICATION LOST's data was lost is scrubbed from any digital files. In a sense, the lack of documentation about IDENTIFICATION LOST insists that this individual no longer exists. The documents provided blow is what remains of IDENTIFICATION LOST's internet presence. The hunt is ongoing to find physical files about IDENTIFICATION LOST, with the goal to override the data breach and reinstate their account.
 Image on the left is IDENTIFICATION LOST's profile picture.

Entity 303.title: Entity 303
Entity 303.description:
The Mime is a singular entity that is notoriously difficult to escape. It possesses multiple anomalous abilities, one of which is the ability to actively utilize Phenomenon 14 to instantly move between locations of its choosing that are affected by the phenomenon. It can even accomplish this with individual body parts; for example, if it were to hold its arm out of view behind a wall in a non-linear space, its arm would be able to appear from behind a completely different wall in that same space. Fortunately, it can only do this in areas that are already under the phenomenon's effects, rendering escape from the Mime much more likely in places without Shifting Geometry.

The entity is capable of shifting between two forms. In one of its forms, which is less humanoid, it can summon tentacles that break through nearby surfaces like walls, floors, or ceilings to assist in catching and killing its targets. Only a pitch-black void can be seen where the tentacles emerge from. After a tentacle retreats back into the darkness, the hole it made will anomalously quickly repair itself, with any debris that resulted from the breach disappearing immediately before reappearing back where it was before.

The Mangled.title: The Mangled
The Mangled.description: Written by Kaos.CSS comes from Char Images comes from a TedEd video
The Mangled.lead: Written by Kaos.CSS comes from Char Images comes from a TedEd video

Sanguine Festivus.title: Sanguine Festivus
Sanguine Festivus.description:
Entity 67, nicknamed partygoers, refers to the majority of individuals infected by sanguine festivus virus (SFV). Their appearance is largely consistent, resembling hobbling, wasting husks covered with a large cloth. Their self-inflicted injuries often bleed through the yellowed sheets, leaving a smile-like stain over their face. Why infected victims display these behaviors so consistently remains unknown, though it is theorized that the SFV pathogen may possess an anomalous, mind-altering property that drives the behavior of victims to such a degree of similarity. Interestingly, those infected outside The Party Rooms do not follow these behaviors, instead lumbering around with their injuries unobscured.

Individuals with late-stage SFV fixate on locating and feeding on living beings, primarily other wanderers, both as a means of spreading the virus further and coping with their insatiable hunger. This behavior often leads to violent attacks, causing rapid transmission to survivors. Their aggressive demeanor remains consistent even when encountering other carriers of the virus, fortunately preventing the formation of groups. Carriers of SFV exhibit clear symptoms that can be easily spotted if one is aware of what to look for; common indicators include low, unintelligible murmurs and a sluggish, swaying gait. Due to their unpredictable behavior, getting within arm's reach of an infected host is exceedingly dangerous and should be avoided at all costs.
Sanguine Festivus.lead: Reload the page to load audio

U.S. (Count the Guns!) (Joke).title: U.S. (Count the Guns!) (Joke)
U.S. (Count the Guns!) (Joke).displayTitle: A m e r i c a !
U.S. (Count the Guns!) (Joke).description:
U.S. is thought to be a sentient program that purchases guns off of any website it can find. After buying said guns, it will then leave a review regarding the guns it bought. It has been recorded that U.S. has the ability to modify the properties of the guns it buys and inserts reviews about the modified version. It is unknown whether or not U.S. can modify all guns, or if it is limited to just the guns it buys.

U.S.'s intelligence level is unknown, as it has written reviews about the guns it buys either perfectly accurate to the original product, but has also written reviews that detail features that have not been implemented in the product. U.S. is believed to have no physical body and is instead a digital manifestation. U.S. is capable of creating user profiles to then comment on products.

The reviews created by U.S. seem to have the ability to modify the products themselves, though many believe this to simply be the entity to have a clairvoyant ability. The true reason remains unknown.

Entity 94.title: Entity 94
Entity 94.description: Entity 94, also known as the "Tour Guide," is described to be a small, humanoid figure admittedly mimicking a missing 14-year old boy named Rowan Lambert (c. 1983 — 1981), presumed dead. It is widely known among many survivors (dubbed "subjects") and Groups as a means of discovering safe environments for future outposts or camps, alongside an entrance to various levels (most commonly Level 6).
allLinks: Cooking in the Backrooms — A Guide for Entities

Level 9.7.title: Level 9.7
Level 9.7.description:
Level 9.7 is a presumably infinite expanse of intertwining suburban alleyways trapped in a perpetual overcast night, with uneven, poorly-maintained roads. Potholes can be found throughout the level, some filled with contaminated Almond Water and rarely, Carpet Fluid threat neutralized. Occasionally, one may encounter a wider, slightly better maintained road. These roads are lit by street lights, some of which may be facing the wrong direction (ex. shining into a house's backyard, illuminating a fence instead of the road) or partially obstructed by unknown means.
Despite the lack of motorized vehicles, tire marks can be seen at intersections, and several parts of the roads are cracked, with loose gravel dispersed in these areas. Sounds of tires crunching on gravel are also constantly audible. In addition, eerie, almost robotic sounds of a cat meowing and birds chirping are present throughout the level, in spite of the lack of any instances of feline or avian organisms within Level 9.7. The volume of these sounds remains constant, regardless of the wanderer's position within the level.
The sides of the alleyways are lined with houses, trash bins, and wooden telephone poles. The majority of the houses' exteriors are reminiscent of architecture from the 1960s-1990s; however, buildings of more modern fashion can be found throughout the level as well, albeit rare. These homes are the only ones with addresses displayed on them. The lowest and highest address numbers discovered thus far are 32 and 129348765954723568543, respectively. Dated apartment buildings can be infrequently found in impossibly long stretches, often occupying several blocks. It should be noted that there are no entrances to these apartment blocks; similarly, all houses are guarded by garages and/or fences with no openable doors or gates.
The temperature within this level is uncomfortably cold, with the highest temperature recorded reaching a mere 10°C (50°F), and the lowest temperature dropping down to -8°C (17.6°F). It is unknown how the level warms and cools itself, due to the absence of a day-night cycle, a sun or other sources of heat. These temperatures can induce hypothermia; hence, it is advised that wanderers bring warm clothing if they intentionally enter this level.

Many believe that, as a sub-level of Level 9, Level 9.7 is the "backside" of its parent level and serves as a gateway between Level 9, its sub-levels, and its surrounding levels.
Level 9.7.lead: Level 9.7 is a sub-level between Level 9 and Level 10, discovered on September 5, 2017 by an anonymous lone wanderer after walking an undeterminable distance in Level 9.

entrances.text: Extremely rarely, wandering far enough in Level 9 may lead to this level.
Jumping into a pond in Level 594, and then no-clipping into the ground will bring one to this level.
Punching certain trees in Level 110 may bring one to this level.
Inhaling the fog of Level 158 for too long will send one here.
A small, out of place outhouse may appear randomly in Level 351; entering it leads to this level.
In Level 450, no-clipping through non-entity mirrors brings one to this level.
Rarely, one may find a jug of black milk in a fridge in Level 570; drinking a certain amount of it will cause one to fall unconscious and wake up on the streets of Level 9.7.
Walking backwards into a light pole in Level 9.6 causes one to no-clip into it and enter this level.

exits.text: When walking for exceedingly long distances, unknown factors cause the level to fade back into Level 9 or one of its other sub-levels.
Should one attempt to climb over a fence or atop the roof of a garage, they will no-clip through the floor and be sent to The Void upon landing on the other side.
Attempting to gain access to the front yard of any house (via a fence gate, front door, etc.) will also cause one to no-clip through the floor and fall into The Void.
In both cases, wanderers can rarely be sent to The Game instead.
Attempting to no-clip into an Aggressive Light has been reported to bring one to Level 5.1.
No-clipping whilst engulfed by the fog of a Foglighter brings one to Level 690.
Staring at a street light for long enough will lead to Level 82.
Breaking a TV in a house sends one to Level 444.
Climbing a telephone pole sends one to Level 722.
Climbing into a trash bin, closing the lid, and staying inside for several hours will send the wanderer and the trash bin to the Ame-no-ukihasi section of Level 145.
Walking into a dense thicket of small rainforest trees/shrubs causes one to re-emerge in Level 427.
Touching a fridge found in an alleyway sends one to Level 142.
Holes in the ground may lead to Level 479.
Lighting oneself on fire leads to Level 997.
Other projects: Level 208: "The Floor is ████(rip)(u can see an archived version in my sandbox) Entity 85: "Lights"Entity 84: "Foglighters
Planning on creating a sub-level of Level 94

Entity 85.title: Entity 85
Entity 85.description: Lights (scientific name Lumina damnationem) are an entity found only in Level 9.7 (not to be confused with Lighters or Foglighters).
Entity 85.lead: Lights (scientific name Lumina damnationem) are an entity found only in Level 9.7 (not to be confused with Lighters or Foglighters).

Entity 84.title: Entity 84
Entity 84.description: Entity 84, commonly known as Foglighters (occasionally referred to as "Fog Lighters"), are entities resembling Lighters that emit a light blue-grey glow. Foglighters are accompanied by a 100-meter radius of fog, which, upon engulfing a wanderer, creates an abnormally bright environment, resembling daylight. This fog induces mild psychological effects, most notably fatigue and extreme mood swings. The mechanism behind the Foglighter's fog generation remains unknown. The most widely accepted theory posits that Foglighters are surrounded by an immense aura of microscopic crystalline particles. These crystals, composed of an unidentified highly refractive material, act as highly efficient reflectors of the Foglighter's glow. However, this theory suffers from significant flaws, primarily the lack of explanation for the anomalous formation and presence of these crystals around the Foglighter. Furthermore, the cause of the Foglighter's psychological effects remains unclear and lacks plausible explanations.
Entity 84.lead: Foglighters (scientific name Nebula levis machina) are an entity found only in Level 9.7 (not to be confused with Lighters or Lights).

Entity 79.title: Entity 79
Entity 79.description:
Entity 79, also known as Radio Demons, resemble vintage radios from the early 20th century, when in a docile state. The only distinguishing feature between normal radios and Entity 79 is a noticeable black, viscous substance. Interestingly however, Entity 79's radio like body is only an outer shell-like structure, akin to hermit crabs.

Their true body has never been properly documented, with only vague descriptions of it's figure from wanderers who caught a glimpse of it chasing down prey. Entity 79 interestingly has three distinct stages in behavior, varying on whether the creature is in the need for food.
Entity 79.lead: An unedited version of one of the many songs that Entity 79 may play when it's awake.

State of Nautica.title: State of Nautica
State of Nautica.description:
The State of Nautica is an underwater group composed of humanoid entities known as Nautics. They appear in multiple sea levels, and they have shown expertise in navigating expansive waters, even having built fairly advanced underwater colonies. Outside of the water, they operate in ships that can be either very small to large in size, even sometimes being cargo ships.

Nautics, in order to look more familiar to other wanderers, usually wear scuba diving suits. While few footage of them without suits exists, it has been confirmed they are bipedal creatures with gills and tentacles that can wrap around their legs when out of water. Nautics sustain themselves via trade with other groups, hunting Splinter Jellyfish, and scavenging. About 4,000+ Nautics live in the State Of Nautica.

The capital of this group, Neptune, lies in Level 7.5, although before The Flicker it used to be in Level 205. They are mainly a trade-based society, acting similarly to a standard democratic nation, and despite having a military, it is relatively small compared to other groups.

Level 351.title: Level 351
Level 351.description:
Notice:Level 351 has been designated Impresario Class Priority. If you have been assigned to Level 351, please read the following document.Reason:Level 351 is host to a malevolent entity classified as highly dangerous. In the event of a sighting, immediate concealment is imperative to mitigate the risk of detection.Protocol:If you decide to enter Level 351, make sure to bring proper equipment, and to always stay in groups of around 3-17 people. Venturing into Level 351 alone is strongly discouraged.

Level 351:

"The Enchanted Forest"
I-Description

Level 351, also known as "The Enchanted Gloom," takes the appearance of a forest that extends out for 196.9 million mi². The trees within the level range in height from approximately 20 to 130 feet and are predominantly composed of primarily pine, birch, oak, and fir wood types. The level is coated in a white fog, which is presumed to be composed of regular water, though it's not classified. The level seems to have a dim green glow on the horizon at all times. The temperature of the level ranges from 10°C (50°F) to 5.2°C (41.4°F). Level 351 is mostly stuck in a perpetual state of night, but on rare occasions, daytime or dusk may occur. The moon in the level always appears to be completely red and will be equally visible throughout the level.

Theories suggest Level 351 originally came from the Arkhangelsk Region in Northern Russia. It is theorized that at some point, the area got no-clipped into the Backrooms and drastically changed its original environment. These changes did not go as far as just the landscape but also affected flora and fauna, changing the nature of the area altogether. The area underwent mutations and expansions; thus, it became a new form of the level, no longer bearing any resemblance to its original state. As such, a flat and barren plain now stands on the location where the area once was in the Frontrooms. The aftermath brought about an effect that made the land unnaturally static, for it hindered the normal cycle of decomposition. It blocked new trees from sprouting up and suspended life in the very same area.

The terrain of the level changes depending on its geographical setting; however, it is commonly forested with hills, mountains, and caves. In rare instances, the land consists of nothing but barren flora, including dead trees, scattered branches, and patches of straw. Throughout the level are scattered conscious trees that whisper quietly to themselves, which people claim are the voices of the deceased. These whispers remain unclassified, as no clear origin or purpose could be established for their explanation. The desolate regions—although seemingly lifeless—very often give off faint signs of activity, like unexplained movements or sounds. In this way, the natural and supernatural elements bring forth a haunted and mysterious atmosphere in the level.

One such level contains a strange structure resembling an abandoned house built partly out of human bones, and is allegedly indestructible – all attempts to demolish or damage it have failed. Additionally, ruins of villages have also been found in the level; nevertheless, these seem to have been evacuated and then destroyed. Traces of human habitation, such as blood and presumably human pollen, are scattered about the area; yet, no signs of living humans have been encountered.

II-Entities

Baba Yaga

Baba Yaga appears to be a very old woman of indeterminate age and race. The entity communicates in Old Russian, though further attempts to communicate with Baba Yaga haven't been successful, as it doesn't want to speak and it mostly speaks of vengeance. Baba Yaga has never identified herself by any name, and due to her aggressive nature, it has been impossible to determine any background information.

Baba Yaga holds a power of strength and speed much faster than a normal human. The entity can reach speeds of 70km/h while weighing 200lbs. Baba Yaga can regenerate any wounds on her body despite the damage; the entity can regenerate from decapitation or a disconnected limb. The regenerating process can take a few hours to days. It is overly powerful in terms of strength, as it can rip through flesh with ease just by using its nails. The entity can also jump very high and far.

Baba Yaga is capable of growing thin, hair-like strands from any part of her body, apparently at will. These strands can grow several metres in a few hours. They have been observed "slithering" along floors, up walls, and on other structures. These hairs are clear and nearly invisible to the naked eye, and appear to be slightly stronger than standard human hair. The strands are also coated in a thin layer of a chemical enzyme identical to the enzyme in the saliva of the entity.

Baba Yaga appears to subsist on a carnivorous diet, with a strong liking for human flesh. Baba Yaga will create a "web" of hair, and linger for prey to become exposed to the enzyme and become weaker. Baba Yaga will rip the limbs of one who gets stuck in the web, and consume the wanderer from limb to torso, and to the head. After being consumed, the entity will collect its bones.

The entity resides in a house, closely resembling a wooden hut, though the hut is partly made of human bones, specifically femur and rib bones. The hut is indestructible, as all attempts to damage or destroy the hut have been unsuccessful.

Still, on rare occasions, Baba Yaga chooses not to consume its prey. Instead, it knocks the victim unconscious and inexplicably transforms them into a zombie-like entity. These entities display violent behaviors, attacking anything in their surroundings except Baba Yaga, and brutally devouring their victims. The zombie-entities possess superhuman strength, though they are noticeably weaker than Baba Yaga itself and have significantly diminished intelligence compared to a regular human.

III-Settlements

M.E.G Villages
The M.E.G has established bases near the ruined villages within the level, each consisting of approximately 5–20 armed soldiers. These bases are friendly and are open to trade with wanderers who approach peacefully. However, they maintain strict security measures and will engage in defensive actions, including lethal force, against any hostile entities or individuals that pose a threat.

The principal purpose of the establishments is to conduct tests with Baba Yaga.

IV-Ingress and Egress

Ingress
*Passing out on Level 630 may bring one here.
*Finding an out of place house on Level 200.
*Remaining in the original location while undergoing the no-clipping process will bring one here.
Egress
*Walking far into a plain field will bring one to Level 10.
*Passing out in a body of water in the level may bring one to Level 7, or Level 37.
*Finding an out of placed stairwells will bring one to Level 387.

V-Addendums
