V-Addendums

Entity 54.title: Entity 54
Entity 54.description:
Entity 54, otherwise known as Crumbs, are common entities found throughout most of the Backrooms. Their discovery dates back to September 2011.

Description
Crumbs, also referred to by their scientific name Parvum cinis (meaning "little ash"), are hidden entities, of about one to two centimeters in diameter. They are found within cracks, holes, and corners across most levels of the Backrooms, more specifically, dark or humid levels. Crumbs are entities considered hard to document. Due to their size, and speed, they often go unnoticed by most lifeforms. Crumbs are usually considered counterparts to ants in the Frontrooms, due to their similar appearance and behavior, though, both contain striking differences.

Despite their aggressiveness and various defense mechanisms, Crumbs are generally easy to kill. An accidental step or kick will crush a Crumb to death. Containing an instance is also relatively easy, while being quick, a Crumb can be contained in the same way an ant or a bee can, a large box will imprison it. It is impossible for them to climb, or jump, therefore, exiting the box will be near impossible. Another method of killing a Crumb is by sinking them in water. While splashing them in water will not damage them, they will dissolve into water if surrounded by it.

Crumbs are very well-used around the Backrooms. They can be used as food for Wimpworms, and their body can be used as some sort of poison. Crumb pieces are used as a poisonous seasoning for some foods around the Backrooms, they are also canned as "handheld poison". If one was able to remove the poison of a Crumb's entire body without destroying it, Crumbs could be very appreciated foods, due to how many nutrients can be found within them.
Behavior
Crumbs are much alike to ordinary ants in behavior, most of them are part of or build colonies, with hierarchies, and roles. Workers must go out and search for nourishment, while one queen produces children. While similar, Crumbs have a different organization to that of ants. Workers are divided into army, care, and food-searching, and they all work independently from any other instance of the entity. They will often explore levels alone, ignoring the dangers of the location. The internal structure and location of a colony are also very different. Unlike their Frontrooms counterparts, Crumbs prefer to establish colonies within dark, humid, and/or hidden places, like, for example, inside a couch. Their colonies, unlike ant ones, are more unpredictable, certain spaces receive random roles, without any organization or long-term planning.

Crumbs also differ from ants due to their behavior, Crumbs are more aggressive and super-protective. They attack any non-Crumb lifeform on sight. They will bravely guard a colony until their last breath. Despite working together, Crumbs are not friendly to each other and can fight during everyday tasks. This extends to younglings and even sometimes to the queen. Typically, fights between younglings do not cause issues. In contrast, if a Crumb fights the queen, and successfully defeats her, that Crumb takes on the role of queen, while the former queen will either die from stress and hunger or become a worker.
BiologyAs aforementioned, Crumbs are very small, invertebrate entities that take the form of bread crumbs, but the type of crumb may vary depending on region. Some examples of other crumb-esque forms these entities can take are popcorn crumbs, snack crumbs, etc. Due to being small and weak, these creatures have developed a variety of defense measures. For example, it can produce a loud squeak to scare predators and retains a poisonous bite, that can kill a human in mere hours. In addition, Crumbs have small legs, which allow them to run at high speed. Crumbs are described to have adhesive substances that cover their bodies, which they can use for defense, and hiding. Strangely, the entity's body appears to reach full adhesiveness during its death. It is possible that this is caused by stress. In a desire for defense, the entity produces more of this sticky substance, in hopes of surviving.

These entities are considered omnivorous creatures. They will eat anything small enough to fit their mouths. A small rock would and will likely be eaten by a Crumb at some point. These entities do not conventionally drink, instead, they absorb rain or puddle water, which they find anywhere. An instance can last weeks without food or water, as it can store a bit of the nutrients within itself. However, in emergencies, the entity may eat small pieces of itself to survive. If a specimen dies from hunger or dehydration, it will dissolve into a mass of crumbs and the adhesive substance it is covered in. The mixture of both these substances, after some days, will result in a toxic compound. While this compound will not kill, it may cause fairly common diseases, like fever.
DiscoveryCrumbs were discovered in Level 386, by an anonymous explorer. Images of the entity were sent to Back-Graph for revision. After weeks of analysis, it was determined that these were newly discovered entities, which the Back-Graph community named "Bread Crumbs", the name would later be simplified to Crumbs. They would be categorized as Entity 54 with the scientific name Parvum cinis.
Do's and Dont's

They are found within cracks, holes, and corners across most levels of the Backrooms, more specifically, dark or humid levels. Crumbs are entities considered hard to document. Due to their size, and speed, they often go unnoticed by most lifeforms. Crumbs are usually considered counterparts to ants in the Frontrooms, due to their similar appearance and behavior, though, both contain striking differences.

Despite their aggressiveness and various defense mechanisms, Crumbs are generally easy to kill. An accidental step or kick will crush a Crumb to death. Containing an instance is also relatively easy, while being quick, a Crumb can be contained in the same way an ant or a bee can, a large box will imprison it. It is impossible for them to climb, or jump, therefore, exiting the box will be near impossible. Another method of killing a Crumb is by sinking them in water. While splashing them in water will not damage them, they will dissolve into water if surrounded by it.

Crumbs are very well-used around the Backrooms. They can be used as food for Wimpworms, and their body can be used as some sort of poison. Crumb pieces are used as a poisonous seasoning for some foods around the Backrooms, they are also canned as "handheld poison". If one was able to remove the poison of a Crumb's entire body without destroying it, Crumbs could be very appreciated foods, due to how many nutrients can be found within them.
Behavior
Crumbs are much alike to ordinary ants in behavior, most of them are part of or build colonies, with hierarchies, and roles. Workers must go out and search for nourishment, while one queen produces children. While similar, Crumbs have a different organization to that of ants. Workers are divided into army, care, and food-searching, and they all work independently from any other instance of the entity. They will often explore levels alone, ignoring the dangers of the location. The internal structure and location of a colony are also very different. Unlike their Frontrooms counterparts, Crumbs prefer to establish colonies within dark, humid, and/or hidden places, like, for example, inside a couch. Their colonies, unlike ant ones, are more unpredictable, certain spaces receive random roles, without any organization or long-term planning.

Crumbs also differ from ants due to their behavior, Crumbs are more aggressive and super-protective. They attack any non-Crumb lifeform on sight. They will bravely guard a colony until their last breath. Despite working together, Crumbs are not friendly to each other and can fight during everyday tasks. This extends to younglings and even sometimes to the queen. Typically, fights between younglings do not cause issues. In contrast, if a Crumb fights the queen, and successfully defeats her, that Crumb takes on the role of queen, while the former queen will either die from stress and hunger or become a worker.
BiologyAs aforementioned, Crumbs are very small, invertebrate entities that take the form of bread crumbs, but the type of crumb may vary depending on region. Some examples of other crumb-esque forms these entities can take are popcorn crumbs, snack crumbs, etc. Due to being small and weak, these creatures have developed a variety of defense measures. For example, it can produce a loud squeak to scare predators and retains a poisonous bite, that can kill a human in mere hours. In addition, Crumbs have small legs, which allow them to run at high speed. Crumbs are described to have adhesive substances that cover their bodies, which they can use for defense, and hiding. Strangely, the entity's body appears to reach full adhesiveness during its death. It is possible that this is caused by stress. In a desire for defense, the entity produces more of this sticky substance, in hopes of surviving.

These entities are considered omnivorous creatures. They will eat anything small enough to fit their mouths. A small rock would and will likely be eaten by a Crumb at some point. These entities do not conventionally drink, instead, they absorb rain or puddle water, which they find anywhere. An instance can last weeks without food or water, as it can store a bit of the nutrients within itself. However, in emergencies, the entity may eat small pieces of itself to survive. If a specimen dies from hunger or dehydration, it will dissolve into a mass of crumbs and the adhesive substance it is covered in. The mixture of both these substances, after some days, will result in a toxic compound. While this compound will not kill, it may cause fairly common diseases, like fever.
DiscoveryCrumbs were discovered in Level 386, by an anonymous explorer. Images of the entity were sent to Back-Graph for revision. After weeks of analysis, it was determined that these were newly discovered entities, which the Back-Graph community named "Bread Crumbs", the name would later be simplified to Crumbs. They would be categorized as Entity 54 with the scientific name Parvum cinis.
Do's and Dont's

Entity 101.title: Entity 101
Entity 101.description:
Old Birds are the second-to-last surviving species of the genus Novolavem, just behind the Novis Noctua. Usually measuring 8 feet 4, Old Birds are bird-like, specifically owl-styled, beings characterized by their bipedalism, high intelligence, and their complex language. Old Birds were predicted to become the dominant species of the Backrooms due to their unique Backrooms-centric evolution, weren't it be for human settlement in the Backrooms.

Old Birds are pseudo-humanoid, as previously stated, bird-like beings with their bodies being covered in feathers, which are longer along the arms, resembling wings in spite of their inability to fly, and having a short, squarish tail. They weigh 100 to 130 kg, though exceptional instances can reach up to 158.8 kg. The feathers of adult and young males are dark colored, with some having white primaries and white tails; females are more grey and white in color. The head and hands of Old Birds are bare, with their skin being a pure white; the skin in the head of Old Birds is considerably more sturdy than other parts, and it has been described as "bone-like."

Old Birds have continuously been nomadic hunter-gatherers since their discovery, with permanent residence having only been reported as late as 2003. Human-Old Bird interactions have kickstarted a period of continuous (and ongoing) technological change and cultural revolution for the species, with assumptions of behavioral assimilations being made as Old Birds strive to reach the "greatness of human civilization".

The Novis Noctua, or simply Noctua, are rarely-sighted fifteen-feet-tall creatures similar to a dragon. Covered in feathers of dark red and brown, with a pale-colored mask similar to their still-alive relatives, the Old Birds, on their face; two small horns sit atop the entity's head, circulated by a halo. They have two massive wings and split tails, though their ability to fly is disputed, as they have never been seen doing such. They have the ability to vocally communicate, thus allowing the transliteration and translation of the Noekali script.

Currently there only exist ten confirmed living Novis Noctuans remaining. What exactly occurred to other members of the species is unknown, though it is speculated that they most likely starved to death. The ten remaining members of the species have survived due to evolving to survive extended periods without food or through collaborations with the Old Birds. All remaining population of the species are forming members of the designated "Ten Sols Council;" they act as the guiding lights of almost every group of Old Birds and thus have been seen as "gods" by them. Additionally, it has been noted that the largest communities of Old Birds are led by members of the council.

Each member of the Ten Sols Council, known as Sols or Dawnbreakers, has a sector of specialization designated under the "Tenets of Society and Survival." The council also details, allows, and spreads practices, technologies, and anything of significant help or importance to Old Bird society; furthermore, they mandate actions regarding justice and law, diplomacy, and everything else. Interactions between Old Birds and Noctuas are common, as individual Old Birds are allowed to meet the council with the intent to suggest actions mainly on both internal and foreign matters and the implementation of things (specified in the official Database documentation). Interactions with humans remain purely for diplomatic purpose, their most recent wide appearance was during the ratification of the Solsium Defense Accord.

Noctuas are believed to live up to 20,000 years old or are potentially immortal.

The Novis Noctua, or simply Noctua, are rarely-sighted fifteen-feet-tall creatures similar to a dragon. Covered in feathers of dark red and brown, with a pale-colored mask similar to their still-alive relatives, the Old Birds, on their face; two small horns sit atop the entity's head, circulated by a halo. They have two massive wings and split tails, though their ability to fly is disputed, as they have never been seen doing such. They have the ability to vocally communicate, thus allowing the transliteration and translation of the Noekali script.

Currently there only exist ten confirmed living Novis Noctuans remaining. What exactly occurred to other members of the species is unknown, though it is speculated that they most likely starved to death. The ten remaining members of the species have survived due to evolving to survive extended periods without food or through collaborations with the Old Birds. All remaining population of the species are forming members of the designated "Ten Sols Council;" they act as the guiding lights of almost every group of Old Birds and thus have been seen as "gods" by them. Additionally, it has been noted that the largest communities of Old Birds are led by members of the council.

Each member of the Ten Sols Council, known as Sols or Dawnbreakers, has a sector of specialization designated under the "Tenets of Society and Survival." The council also details, allows, and spreads practices, technologies, and anything of significant help or importance to Old Bird society; furthermore, they mandate actions regarding justice and law, diplomacy, and everything else. Interactions between Old Birds and Noctuas are common, as individual Old Birds are allowed to meet the council with the intent to suggest actions mainly on both internal and foreign matters and the implementation of things (specified in the official Database documentation). Interactions with humans remain purely for diplomatic purpose, their most recent wide appearance was during the ratification of the Solsium Defense Accord.

Noctuas are believed to live up to 20,000 years old or are potentially immortal.

Entity 57.title: Entity 57
Entity 57.description:
Chatterers are helpful entities that are commonly found throughout the entirety of the Backrooms. In appearance, they look exactly like the chatter telephone toy manufactured in the Frontrooms.  , although they seem larger by a few inches. Similarly to their Frontrooms counterpart, Chatterers hold 4 blue wheels, which they use to travel throughout levels. Additionally, they all include a white car-like structure, used for attaching other pieces of the entity. Every Chatterer holds a red telephone on top of them, along with a rotary dial.

Slight decorations, such as colored stars, are often found within the entirety of a Chatterer. Furthermore, signs of deterioration can also be noted. These often include small cracks and varying levels of weathering in the paint.

Chatterers will approach a wanderer as soon as they see one. Just as the wanderer notices the  presence of a Chatterer, it will start ringing its telephone and rolling its eyes very quickly, most likely suggesting the wanderer to answer the telephone. If the wanderer does answer the telephone, they will feel an intensive urge to follow the Chatterer. Doing so will, surprisingly, lead to the exit of whichever level they are currently within. Wanderers that have rejected the assistance of Chatterers have reported seeing said entity stalking them for a few days, however, no harm had  been committed towards them.

Chatterers have been proven to scare off a large variety of dangerous entities, such as Smilers, Slashers, and Stalkers. Furthermore, it has been proven that Chatterers often use this ability to protect wanderers in mortal danger , even going far enough to protect numerous outposts during the Flicker.

While not helping wanderers, Chatterers spend their time collecting bottles of almond water and pushing them individually until they create piles of said object. It is unknown why they do this, but it is widely theorized Chatterers create these groups of Almond Water for wanderers in need of resources. Unfortunately, Chatterers do not seem to differentiate Cashew Water from Almond Water, often creating mixed piles of said objects.

In some rare cases, Chatterers have been confirmed to be able to communicate with wanderers, knowing the basics of a variety of languages. Numerous tests have been done in regards to their ability to talk, however, most Chatterers refused to speak during the interview. Nonetheless, a few communicated with their respective interviewers, revealing many facts about the entity. Throughout these interviews, it has been confirmed that Chatterers seemingly spawn with their ability to talk, as well as with their knowledge of the layout of various Euclidean levels.

Chatterers can be found throughout the majority of the Backrooms, usually within levels of class 3 or higher. Their main nest can be found within level 157. Holes and burrows have been discovered in the deeper areas of the level, carved within the walls and floors. Numerous observations taken by the outposts within level 157 have confirmed that Chatterers are more likely to spawn in these burrows than any other location throughout the Backrooms.

Further inspections and autopsies on Chatterers have proven that they have a brain similar to a human's, including areas that work similar to the cerebrum and the cerebellum. Although further investigation is ongoing, it is widely presumed this brain allows Chatterers to perform certain vital functions, such as thinking. Furthermore, small tubes seem to connect a Chatterer´s brain to its legs. Deeper research has been done, revealing that these tubes hold large quantities of neurons, allowing the Chatterer to control its wheels, therefore being able to move.

Additionally, a Chatterer´s telephone is connected to its brain via a small, helical spring. Unlike the rest of the Chatterer, this spring is extremely mysterious, holding many anomalous properties that somehow make the entity be able to speak.

FEAR.title: FEAR
FEAR.description:
The Apocalypse Wiki

Introduction
doomsday

/ˈduːmzdeɪ/
noun

the last day of the world's existence.

(in religious belief) the day of the Last Judgement.

a time or event of crisis or great danger.

In most religions, it is believed that great calamities beckon the coming of doomsday, with the calls of trumpets resounding from the skies as a warning. Alas, nobody could have foreseen this.
May 22nd, 1960. A catastrophic, record-breaking earthquake of magnitude 9.6 occurred in Valdivia, Chile, followed by destructive tsunamis across the Pacific, with a disastrous number of casualties and collateral damage involved. Not long after, a mysterious area dubbed the Null Zone appears in the region of Santiago, presumably involved with the abnormal magnitude of the disaster.
By the year 1961, various occurrences had been reported in the newspapers, civilians apparently encountering dreadful abominations and even capturing several on camera. As years passed by, the number of sightings steadily increased, as the numerous in sizes and variety entities began to grow progressively hostile towards the population, some subtly infecting one’s immune system, some bluntly razing villages to the ground, staining the earth with blood. The world began to grow chaotic, as countries fell into anarchy and disarray, the era now dubbed the Apocalypse Period.

The origins of those abominations are unknown, however, they are believed to be linked to the unusual magnitude of the Chilean earthquake and to the sudden creation of the Null Zone (being abnormal by itself). They are believed to number in the thousands, or perhaps even millions, of such entities roaming the earth now. Listed below are the various categories of entities:

Entity Categories

Anima

Anima were the first of the creatures to be discovered and the first to appear, named after the word "animal" due to their animal-like characteristics. The mere size of an Anima can startle any person, some reaching up to 40 meters in height; either the bare simplicity or complexity of its appearance can create disgust and fear. In most images of it, it emits a sense of stripped familiarity; usually having 4 limbs in its perfect shape that resemble an animal, and necks that can even reach for the skies. From the videos provided, Animas' moves are cautious and calculated, their behaviors are animalistic. They know when to hide, and when to strike. They bring fear, but they seem not to attack others unless agitated. Most people who encounter an Anima usually survive, albeit severely traumatized after. Anima attack with fear.

Although most of the Anima match the description above, do note that there are exceptions. There have been reports on flying Anima or Anima that have more than 4 limbs. Some Anima even have wings or tentacles. The first ever discovery and photo of it was taken in 1961. The photographer is presumed dead.

Bio

Bio were the second of the creatures to be discovered, although we do not know the exact date of when these creatures appeared. They are named "Bio", due to their virus-like characteristics. Bio is a group of mainly 5 viruses (as of now) which quickly spread slowly throughout the world in a few years since its discovery. For the first 5 months, the viruses were not identified as they just caused minor symptoms akin to a cold, and could be eradicated with over-the-counter medicine. Then, one of the viruses was identified as one of the Bio, and it started to mutate. Symptoms were becoming more extreme: first, it was diarrhea or loss of taste, but symptoms like platelet suppression, altered mental status, and random loss of consciousness started appearing. The amount of viruses made doctors suspect that already existing viruses and ignorance caused the virus to be free to spread. By the end of the 7 months, the virus stopped mutating and now had its foundation symptoms: organ failures, altered brain structures, shortness of breath, and severe immune deficiency, taking 2 weeks for its victims to die.

In the second year of the viruses' lifespan, the viruses became less infectious but killed even faster, along with painful or uncomfortable symptoms. Attempts to identify its transmission methods had failed due to its unknown infecting methods. Pathologists couldn't study the virus, as it barely left a trace except within people. It was not found through water sources, air, or insects, but only in human blood, which with the virus is dangerous enough to handle despite safety equipment. We have the most information on these creatures by far due to their straightforward attack methods.

The virus was first discovered in 1965.

Naturus

Naturus are the least documented creatures so far and the third to be discovered. Named after the word "nature", due to its habit of blending with nature. It appears only if one is alone and its appearance usually camouflages with their surroundings. For example, in a forest, a Naturus' appearance would resemble a thorn/root-looking entity. Their sizes range from 10 to 50 meters. The entity's only attack is a mental property where one starts to feel immense fear 50 meters around it, which can cause a consistent release of adrenaline. If you stay more than 15 minutes around a Naturus, adrenaline levels can become so high it causes effects like severe stress or other negative emotions, and prolonged effects of the 'flight or fight' reaction (which causes rapid heart rate, shaking, high blood pressure, and dizziness). Additionally, while within 50 meters of a Naturus, sound seems more muffled or quiet.

A Naturus is usually stationary and stares in the direction of the nearest observer. However, if one attempts to hurt it, the Naturus will start to move towards them and emit unintelligible and loud noises. The Naturus then will follow them until they are close to another person. The Naturus was first discovered in 1970.

Humanus

Humanus are the most common creatures, being the creatures that have been encountered the most. They were the fourth type of creature to be discovered. It first appeared in 1971 when an expedition group trying to find a Naturus got ambushed by it. As the name suggests, they appear to be humanoid figures, often with either distorted facial features or none at all. They are around a towering 60 meters in height, taking on a pale grey-ish color. Sometimes, along with its skeletal and slender limbs, some might also have small, albeit long, tentacles in the back, and extra arms. Humanus has commonly been observed in high areas of mountain ridges, cold flatlands, and rarely within isolated lakes. Humanus emit a mild volume ringing noise akin to tinnitus, and is theorised to emit this noise as a form of echolocation. Additionally, Humanus has been observed to be stealthy and fast when one is not aware of its presence, using the environment to its advantage at all times. They are completely passive, but in some cases, where one isn't careful, a Humanus may accidentally step on someone, crushing them almost instantly.

Miscellaneous

Miscellaneous creatures are exceptionally rare creatures and the fifth type of creature to be discovered. Miscellaneous creatures can take on a variety of different forms, but share no similarities with any of the creatures stated above, therefore receiving its own category. Miscellaneous creatures are the smallest and the most nimble. As stated before, they take on many different forms, for example, a shadow that can shift to any practical form or a human hand that is typically used to attack its victims when they aggravate it. Miscellaneous creatures generally appear in small, enclosed, and dark spaces. They mainly target mentally ill people, and the same creature can be in multiple places at once. Once one of these miscellaneous creatures manifests, a prevalent mental effect causes its victims to stay within its vicinity until it disappears. The creature can only follow a person for 1 hour before disappearing. Miscellaneous creatures are also one of the least dangerous creatures; the only physical harm they can cause is the already mentioned attacking when aggravated, albeit the only observed effect from it is that it can blind, deafen, or remove one's taste for up to 5 months. The creature has additionally been observed within episodes of sleep paralysis, trapping individuals within the episode for a longer time (1 to 2 hours longer). They were first discovered in 1975.

Amalgamations

Amalgamations are fairly uncommon, but very dangerous creatures. They were the sixth type of creature to be discovered. Their appearances are usually very messy and cannot be described easily. Often, amalgamations are capable of flight and can reach up to 40 meters, with either missing or extra facial features and mutated limbs. If one encounters this creature, their best choice is to run, as amalgamations can stun anyone within at least a 100-meter radius, meant to disorientate its victims. People have reported losing more energy near it, hearing more voices or unsettling noises, which could cause overstimulation. Amalgamations can also cause random rashes, gashes, or wounds subtly without touching their victim, and can produce large amounts of carbon dioxide. Amalgamations are attracted to any isolated structure or expansive flatlands with no structures present, and can quickly destroy entire buildings and stuff within them; notably, Amalgamations' form is generally go-through, but can switch between different states of matter. They move extremely fast when not observed by anyone, and disappear in around 50 days. They were first discovered in 1976.

Mixed

Mixed creatures are the last creatures to be discovered currently and are relatively rare ones, additionally. As the name suggests, mixed creatures share a combination of different properties from other creatures stated above. They are generally very massive, with their size ranging from 400 meters to 1 kilometer. It is theorized that they hide within the clouds not to catch anyone's attention. When in the middle of urban areas, mixed creatures drop v substances that explode upon impact, akin to bombs, into buildings. They can also change the climate around them and cause different natural disasters, like tornadoes, tsunamis, etc. Additionally, these tentacles make extremely loud static-like noise, 40 meters away, measuring up to 120 dB. If there are any sudden formations of big, extreme, tornado-like clouds over urban areas, it's usually a sign of a mixed creature's presence. The creature moves moderately fast, at 70kph, and dies out when it leaves its targeted area. People have made attempts to examine it further, though attempts have failed; flying a plane into the clouds causes it to impossibly bounce back in the opposite direction. In one of the experiments, not only did the plane completely disappear, but all the people within the plane were supernaturally teleported back to the ground floor.

Comment Section?

ש๏เ๔.

ђєɭɭ๏ קє๏קɭє! ฬє ђ๏קє๔ ץ๏ย єภן๏ץє๔ Շђє รђ๏ฬ! ๒ยՇ ภ๏ฬ เՇ'ร Շเ๓є Շ๏ Շคкє ๒คςк ฬђคՇ เร ๏ยгร.

ש๏เ๔.

รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔รקгєค๔ Շђє ฬ๏г๔ ๏Ŧ ש๏เ๔

ש๏เ๔.
ﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץєﻮ๏๏๔๒ץє

Symptoms were becoming more extreme: first, it was diarrhea or loss of taste, but symptoms like platelet suppression, altered mental status, and random loss of consciousness started appearing. The amount of viruses made doctors suspect that already existing viruses and ignorance caused the virus to be free to spread. By the end of the 7 months, the virus stopped mutating and now had its foundation symptoms: organ failures, altered brain structures, shortness of breath, and severe immune deficiency, taking 2 weeks for its victims to die.

In the second year of the viruses' lifespan, the viruses became less infectious but killed even faster, along with painful or uncomfortable symptoms. Attempts to identify its transmission methods had failed due to its unknown infecting methods. Pathologists couldn't study the virus, as it barely left a trace except within people. It was not found through water sources, air, or insects, but only in human blood, which with the virus is dangerous enough to handle despite safety equipment. We have the most information on these creatures by far due to their straightforward attack methods.

The virus was first discovered in 1965.

Naturus

Naturus are the least documented creatures so far and the third to be discovered. Named after the word "nature", due to its habit of blending with nature. It appears only if one is alone and its appearance usually camouflages with their surroundings. For example, in a forest, a Naturus' appearance would resemble a thorn/root-looking entity. Their sizes range from 10 to 50 meters. The entity's only attack is a mental property where one starts to feel immense fear 50 meters around it, which can cause a consistent release of adrenaline. If you stay more than 15 minutes around a Naturus, adrenaline levels can become so high it causes effects like severe stress or other negative emotions, and prolonged effects of the 'flight or fight' reaction (which causes rapid heart rate, shaking, high blood pressure, and dizziness). Additionally, while within 50 meters of a Naturus, sound seems more muffled or quiet.

A Naturus is usually stationary and stares in the direction of the nearest observer. However, if one attempts to hurt it, the Naturus will start to move towards them and emit unintelligible and loud noises. The Naturus then will follow them until they are close to another person. The Naturus was first discovered in 1970.

Humanus

Humanus are the most common creatures, being the creatures that have been encountered the most. They were the fourth type of creature to be discovered. It first appeared in 1971 when an expedition group trying to find a Naturus got ambushed by it. As the name suggests, they appear to be humanoid figures, often with either distorted facial features or none at all. They are around a towering 60 meters in height, taking on a pale grey-ish color. Sometimes, along with its skeletal and slender limbs, some might also have small, albeit long, tentacles in the back, and extra arms. Humanus has commonly been observed in high areas of mountain ridges, cold flatlands, and rarely within isolated lakes. Humanus emit a mild volume ringing noise akin to tinnitus, and is theorised to emit this noise as a form of echolocation. Additionally, Humanus has been observed to be stealthy and fast when one is not aware of its presence, using the environment to its advantage at all times. They are completely passive, but in some cases, where one isn't careful, a Humanus may accidentally step on someone, crushing them almost instantly.

Miscellaneous

Miscellaneous creatures are exceptionally rare creatures and the fifth type of creature to be discovered. Miscellaneous creatures can take on a variety of different forms, but share no similarities with any of the creatures stated above, therefore receiving its own category. Miscellaneous creatures are the smallest and the most nimble. As stated before, they take on many different forms, for example, a shadow that can shift to any practical form or a human hand that is typically used to attack its victims when they aggravate it. Miscellaneous creatures generally appear in small, enclosed, and dark spaces. They mainly target mentally ill people, and the same creature can be in multiple places at once. Once one of these miscellaneous creatures manifests, a prevalent mental effect causes its victims to stay within its vicinity until it disappears. The creature can only follow a person for 1 hour before disappearing. Miscellaneous creatures are also one of the least dangerous creatures; the only physical harm they can cause is the already mentioned attacking when aggravated, albeit the only observed effect from it is that it can blind, deafen, or remove one's taste for up to 5 months. The creature has additionally been observed within episodes of sleep paralysis, trapping individuals within the episode for a longer time (1 to 2 hours longer). They were first discovered in 1975.

Amalgamations

Amalgamations are fairly uncommon, but very dangerous creatures. They were the sixth type of creature to be discovered. Their appearances are usually very messy and cannot be described easily. Often, amalgamations are capable of flight and can reach up to 40 meters, with either missing or extra facial features and mutated limbs. If one encounters this creature, their best choice is to run, as amalgamations can stun anyone within at least a 100-meter radius, meant to disorientate its victims. People have reported losing more energy near it, hearing more voices or unsettling noises, which could cause overstimulation. Amalgamations can also cause random rashes, gashes, or wounds subtly without touching their victim, and can produce large amounts of carbon dioxide. Amalgamations are attracted to any isolated structure or expansive flatlands with no structures present, and can quickly destroy entire buildings and stuff within them; notably, Amalgamations' form is generally go-through, but can switch between different states of matter. They move extremely fast when not observed by anyone, and disappear in around 50 days. They were first discovered in 1976.

Mixed

Mixed creatures are the last creatures to be discovered currently and are relatively rare ones, additionally. As the name suggests, mixed creatures share a combination of different properties from other creatures stated above. They are generally very massive, with their size ranging from 400 meters to 1 kilometer. It is theorized that they hide within the clouds not to catch anyone's attention. When in the middle of urban areas, mixed creatures drop v substances that explode upon impact, akin to bombs, into buildings. They can also change the climate around them and cause different natural disasters, like tornadoes, tsunamis, etc. Additionally, these tentacles make extremely loud static-like noise, 40 meters away, measuring up to 120 dB. If there are any sudden formations of big, extreme, tornado-like clouds over urban areas, it's usually a sign of a mixed creature's presence. The creature moves moderately fast, at 70kph, and dies out when it leaves its targeted area. People have made attempts to examine it further, though attempts have failed; flying a plane into the clouds causes it to impossibly bounce back in the opposite direction. In one of the experiments, not only did the plane completely disappear, but all the people within the plane were supernaturally teleported back to the ground floor.

Comment Section?

Entity 98.title: Entity 98
Entity 98.description: The Tinkerer is a large quadruped standing at roughly 7ft tall on all fours. It has a large eerily hominid looking torso, with canine hind legs, a reptilian head reminiscent of a raptor-like head, and finally and most notably two large spike-like bones acting as forelegs. All mammalian parts of the body are cover in multiple layers of thick blue and white fur, this would indicate a more artic habitat but as of now M.E.G has not found any Tinkerers in said environments.
Entity 98.lead:
ENTITY NO.XCVIII
THREAT LEVEL

3

Habitat: Common

Residential Levels (4, 9, 11, etc.)

Aggression: 3/5

Aggressive under certain circumstances

Intellect: 4/5

human level intelligence

Danger: 4/5

Life-threatening if provoked

Blood Eagles.title: Blood Eagles
Blood Eagles.description:
BL00D EAGLES

THE AG0NIZING BLISS Y0U MUST NEVER KN0W 0F

THEY BEGAN AT MY LOWER WINGS, THOSE WHICH CLOAKED MY LEGS

CANDLES WERE HELD BENEATH THEM AS THEY TURNED ASHEN GREY

A SCORCHING HEAT PULSED THROUGH MY BONES AS THE FLAMES DANCED

AND THEY WERE WRAPPED AROUND MY LEGS ONCE MORE TO CRACKLE FOR ETERNITY

ABROGATING ME OF SECURITY.

THEY MOVED ONTO MY CENTRAL WINGS, THOSE WHICH SOARED TO THE SKIES

A SICKLE HAD SHEARED THEM AWAY FROM MY BODY, CRIMSON FLUID SEEPING

TO REPLACE THEM, THEY HAD CARVED OUT MY BACK TO THE RIBCAGE

AND THEY WERE BROKEN OUTWARDS, COUNTLESS DAGGERS AGAINST MY SPINE

ABROGATING ME OF DIVINITY.

THEY MOVED ONTO MY UPPER WINGS, THOSE WHICH CONCEALED MY GAZE

THEY WERE SET ASIDE AS MY LEFT EYE WAS TORN OUT, PLACED INTO MY CHEST

BUT THEY WERE NOT AFFECTED AS MY OTHER TWO PAIRS OF WINGS

SO I COULD DRY MY EYE OF TEARS AS UNENDING PAIN CLOUDED ALL I KNEW

ABROGATING ME OF TOLERANCE.

Blood eagles are anomalous entities in the Backrooms with characteristics akin to that of seraphim from the Christian Bible. Their name originates from the torture method of the same moniker which involves breaking the victim's ribs outwards to resemble wings.

  ∾ DESCRIPTION

Blood eagles take the form of humanoid figures adorned with a pair of feathered, silky wings obscuring their face, along with other subtle wing-like structures surrounding their back and waist. Their legs are absent, though they primarily move via levitation of unknown means. Their physical conditions are emaciated, with many of their larger bones being visibly outlined through the skin. Numerous injuries of high variety are present along their bodies, which can fluctuate from small bruises and cuts to large aching scars. All instances of blood eagles share the presence of one notable mutilated section; that being a set of spiny rib-like growths protruding from their backs. With areas often fractured or broken off, the back ribs of a blood eagle are oppressively sensitive to pain responses.

The center of blood eagle's chests are torn open, the cavity spanning a majority of their torso. Serrated ribs surround a location where a heart would typically be placed (despite no notable internal organs being seen in blood eagles thus far). Many of the ribs do not connect to the spine; rather joining the spread of bony growths aforementioned on blood eagles' backs. In its center instead lies a singular eyeball with its optical nerves clinging to nearby flesh. Though the nerves are not attached to the brain of a blood eagle, the eye is entirely functional and used as its main sensory organ.

  ∾ BEHAVIOR

When not occupied with other events, blood eagles meander around levels with seemingly no purpose, merely passing through environments and observing the area. Blood eagles do not require nutrition, hydration, or sleep — albeit are able to rest for short periods to slightly negate pain responses. Encounters with predatory entities often result in the blood eagle in question fleeing, though may use tools or a warning screech to ward off the threat.

The warning screech of a blood eagle.

The life span of blood eagles is divided into two distinct stages; the pre-molting phase and the post-molting phase. The pre-molt appearance is more human-like (including features such as legs and more humanoid hands), albeit with far more severe injuries such as flayed skin and disfigured limbs. While it is unknown where pre-molt blood eagles appear from, they are exceptionally susceptible to the environment. The main goal of a blood eagle in this phase is to seek a viable place to begin its molting process — preferring dark secluded areas of low temperatures. When the conditions are acceptable, the blood eagle will curl into fetal position, slowly growing a dense layer of flesh over its body. Veins extend from the chrysalis and root themselves into the environment, suspending itself above the ground as rings of flesh extrude from the main body. These rings are embedded with several eyes surrounding the exterior which scout the area for potential threats. Wanderers in the eyes' line of sight will experience extreme anxiety and paranoia-based mental effects, primarily serving as the blood eagle's self-defensive measure as it develops into its post-molting phase. The molting state typically lasts 2-3 months.

  ∾ BIOLOGY

As aforementioned, blood eagles typically defend themselves from threats via fleeing or emitting a warning screech. In extreme cases, however, they can spread the wings obscuring their face and expose their right eye to the attacker. The facial eye of a blood eagle has been described as bloodshot and withering, with a prominent pitch-black pupil that absorbs all light. All matter in the facial eye's range of vision will be afflicted with a phenomenon known as "fuzzing" which gradually morphs the matter into a black, ashy material. Matter affected by the blood eagle's eye will possess a crackly quality to its texture akin to television static. The ashy substance may also disperse into the air, causing difficulty breathing in afflicted areas. However, revealing the facial eye is extremely agonizing for blood eagles; such to the point that it cannot be exposed for longer than a few seconds.

Blood eagles have a mutually symbiotic relationship with blood marrow involving the unique interactions between their bone-like structures. When a blood eagle encounters a Blood Marrow's cocoon, it may press its back ribs against the cocoon's outer shell. By doing this, a special process begins in which a thin cyan fluid in transported from the Blood Marrow to the body of the blood eagle. The liquid, under the unofficial name of marrow sap, seems to relieve physical restrictions caused by pain in blood eagles. Additionally, the reaction time and alertness of the consumer is boosted. While marrow sap does function identically in humans, it is extremely potent and may cause seizures or allergic responses upon consuming merely 10-20 milliliters. In certain circumstances, drinking marrow sap may also lead to a gradual decease of the individual over several days.

  ∾ ADDENDUM

"A BL00DSH0T GAZE 0F DESPERATI0N"

THE HEART OF A BROKEN SOUL HOLDS UNYIELDING BENEVOLENCE AT ITS CORE

It was something ricocheting in my mind. Something soaked in its own scarlet blood. Where the eagle's skeletal ailerons pulsed in stress, my heart winced with regret. Where the cinder dawn of what were once wings seemed to ignite once more, the cloaks of comfort unveiled themselves around me. I was exposed and vulnerable. Defenseless against sanity's tightening grasp that was a storm of needles into my skull. Blood began to drip again; I followed the trail like a lamb to slaughter. The cleaver that would strike my neck and depart it with each vertebra was not so merciless, however — rather, it was the glimmer of gold and pearl awaiting at heaven's gates. Somewhere I'd like to be. Somewhere a little closer to being truly free.

Level 83.1.title: Level 83.1
Level 83.1.description:
Level 83.1, commonly known as Hills of Hibiscus, represents an expansive terrain characterized by undulating hills adorned predominantly with vibrant hibiscus-colored grass and various flowers, including lotuses and fluffblooms. The level maintains a consistent state during sunset, signifying that the sky will continuously display an orange tint irrespective of the waiting duration. Throughout the level, a faint floral scent permeates the atmosphere, detectable from any location.

The sky displays a spectrum of colors, including blue, pink, crimson, and occasionally white, with intermittent clouds gradually moving across the horizon. Waterways in various shades of pink can be observed throughout the valleys. The temperature remains consistently temperate. The pathways at Level 83.1 do not adhere to a uniform route; rather, they diverge into waterways or ascend toward the skies, separating from the hills.

Certain regions within Level 83.1 demonstrate elevations that surpass their surroundings by as much as 1200 meters, consequently hindering the visibility of the wanderer. The shadows are represented with a minimal level of graphical fidelity, reminiscent of those typically found in video games, despite lacking association with any specific game. Situated behind several hills, there exists what appears to be a valley, currently designated as a sub-section referred to as The Hibiscus Valley.
Level 83.1.lead:
This page looks the best in dark mode.

Level 83.1 is a sublevel between Level 83 and Level 84 in The Backrooms.

entrances.text: Wandering into a specific area in Level 83 could lead one to The Hibiscus Valley in Level 83.1.
Noclipping through a vibrant flower patch in Level 10 may lead to Level 83.1.
Falling asleep near a pink sunset-like glow in Level 11 has been known to transport wanderers here.
It is possible to get here from Level 504, though the method of egress from there is unknown.
Having a karma rating between 150 and 199 in Level 995 could lead one to this level.

exits.text: Swimming underwater and allowing the current to carry you may transport you to Level 37.
Climbing the Blooming Canopy tree and resting under its glowing petals may lead to Level 100.
Smelling purple flowers in The Hibiscus Valley can make you fall asleep and wake up in Level 626.2.

Wandering into a certain section resembling a specific level could lead one into the exact level. An example of this is one getting randomly sent to Level 118 while passing through the Canopy Pathway area.

Entity 105.title: Entity 105
Entity 105.description:
"What do you wish for, child?"

First discovered in Level 83, Floraduc Alvearium, also known as Entity 105, is a large collection of plants all bounded together at the root system. These roots are significantly larger than those seen on normal plants, with them often being 5 to 10 times longer than non-anomalous roots. All of these bind together in a web at the approximate central point of all plants. If the circumstances are met, all the plants become apart of Floraduc Alvearium. This web has been able to grow over time, adding on more plants and even creating new ones to support itself. The entity can recognize faces, and can recall those it interacted with for upwards of months at a time. This memory is not quite understood, though it is believed to be linked to hive-mind like properties in the Central Rooting System (or CRS).

The Central Rooting System is the host for the entity's collective consciousness, often buried many meters below what is usually expected from roots. This consciousness only forms after 10 or more plants all connect to the Central Rooting System, as more add on, the entity becomes more intelligent and protective. These instincts are what causes the entity to sink deeper into the terra, and add more plants to their system. The largest known CRS sits 50 meters underground, and hosts a population of 1.2k plants all across Level 83.1.

It is unknown why a Floraduc Alvearium is formed, outside of having the necessary amount of plants for one to materialize. The danger of a given level or other presences show no correlation to whether an instance of Entity 105 will appear. However, some theories have come to light about its existence.

Flora DNA Theory

Flora DNA Theory suggests there to be specific genetic coding in all flora life found in the Backrooms that gives them the ability to become an instance of Floraduc Alvearium. Because of the unknown requirements of creating one, the theory suggests that its formation is entirely by choice of the plants. The theory, does not explain their rarity, as if all plants were able to create Central Root Systems, they would likely be more common. It is argued that the conditions were not met for the plants that didn't join, but with the conditions remaining unknown, the theory isn't fully accepted.

Hive Mind Theory

The more popular Hive Mind Theory states that the Central Root System is its own entity, apart from the plants. These entities form beneath the earth in an amorphous state of life, despite not having a physical form. These entities then take in plant roots in order to create a physical form for itself. Questions around the theory mainly revolve around its capabilities of movement, as the Hive Mind Theory does not consider the CRS to be able of movement in a normal sense. Arguments have also formed over the limited communication, as Floraduc Alvearium is only able to reach certain stages with enough plants tied to it, instead of being always able.

Deep Root Theory

Deep Root Theory argues that the process of creating a Central Root System is entirely anomalous, with plants only being able to do it if they are "given" the ability to. This is largely explained by small, controlled diseases that, when tested on Entity 105, show a much stronger ability to spread amongst it. The theory believed that Floraduc Alvearium only forms when a group of already intertwined plants gets infected with the same disease. This illness, nicknamed "Deep Root Disease" or "Entity 105 Effect" forms the basis of the theory name.

"Don't be afraid, you're safe here."

By the time an instance of Floraduc Alvearium reaches 50 members, it is able to form sentences. This communication begins rough, but as more join, the speech abilities show a notable increase. Further tying into their population, the quality of discussion is also dependent. Smaller cases of Entity 105 are less able to recall information, provide shorter answers, and is less open to talking in comparison to their larger counterparts. Larger Central Root Systems may also show less intrigue in speaking to wanderers, often as a result of being "above" their intellect. The facts of this remain unconfirmed, as tests based upon intelligence either don't provide enough data, or are ignored.

Floraduc Alvearium, despite possible benefits, do not create or use vines or other resources as tools. According to Entity 105, the existence of said resources is not for combat, so it cannot override that. Many other questions followed similar answers, suggesting a moral code each Central Root System follows. Without protection, many parts of the entity could be eaten or damaged by others, but it doesn't appear to mind.

In the rare case of two instances of Entity 105 crossing paths, one of two options occur:
# The two of them may combine, forming a larger Central Root System, and providing them with much more intelligence and chances of survival. This involves plants from both Floraduc Alveariums to intertwine with both root systems to create a shared consciousness. In this state, all plants are likely to benefit only a little, which may be dangerous in environments where there is regular damage to the environment or sustenance is thin.
# Both entities will fight, with them aiming to take roots from the other's system and give it to their own. These are more common in harsher environments because of the possibility of reward. These battles may last hundreds of years, with the movement of the roots often slow and arduous.

"We all find ways to survive, and maybe one day we'll thrive."

Entity 105 is generally neutral around humans, with hostility only showing up in certain cases. Hostile encounters with Floraduc Alvearium mainly come from wanderers or other beings damaging the Central Root System, usually through cutting down trees or pulling plants out of the ground. When one does this multiple times, the entity will engage in protective measures, often considered hostile. These include trapping them with plants, striking them with fronds and leaves, and sometimes verbal attacks if the entity is intelligent enough to do so. These aggressive behaviors can last for multiple years, as the entity retains all memories of the individual that harmed it.

Attempts to revert this aggression have not been seen yet, though with interviews with Entity 105 instances, there have been suggestions on what will stop it. Most of which, involves replanting and restoring the parts that were removed. If Floraduc Alvearium is aware of this, it'll reduce hostile behavior significantly and may also be more open to getting damaged for the benefit of others.

With other entities, this same neutrality is usually shown. Though because of some entities being naturally more hostile or passive, it may act different. When around hostile entities, Floraduc Alvearium may guide the being closer to wanderers it doesn't like in order to get them killed. Or it may pull away the entity to protect those it cares for. With the case of dead or otherwise undead creatures, Entity 105 may try to eat it, by using roots to slowly pull the anomaly into the ground. This acts similarly to fertilizer for an instance, but with how uncommon the behavior is, the usefulness of it is considerably minimal.

Below is a list of known instances of Entity 105, and their general status towards wanderers. The total number of any Central Root System can only be estimated, as no official numbers have been provided by the entity.
Entity 105.lead:
"What do you wish for, child?"

First discovered in Level 83, Floraduc Alvearium, also known as Entity 105, is a large collection of plants all bounded together at the root system. These roots are significantly larger than those seen on normal plants, with them often being 5 to 10 times longer than non-anomalous roots. All of these bind together in a web at the approximate central point of all plants. If the circumstances are met, all the plants become apart of Floraduc Alvearium. This web has been able to grow over time, adding on more plants and even creating new ones to support itself. The entity can recognize faces, and can recall those it interacted with for upwards of months at a time. This memory is not quite understood, though it is believed to be linked to hive-mind like properties in the Central Rooting System (or CRS).

The Central Rooting System is the host for the entity's collective consciousness, often buried many meters below what is usually expected from roots. This consciousness only forms after 10 or more plants all connect to the Central Rooting System, as more add on, the entity becomes more intelligent and protective. These instincts are what causes the entity to sink deeper into the terra, and add more plants to their system. The largest known CRS sits 50 meters underground, and hosts a population of 1.2k plants all across Level 83.1.

It is unknown why a Floraduc Alvearium is formed, outside of having the necessary amount of plants for one to materialize. The danger of a given level or other presences show no correlation to whether an instance of Entity 105 will appear. However, some theories have come to light about its existence.

Flora DNA Theory

Flora DNA Theory suggests there to be specific genetic coding in all flora life found in the Backrooms that gives them the ability to become an instance of Floraduc Alvearium. Because of the unknown requirements of creating one, the theory suggests that its formation is entirely by choice of the plants. The theory, does not explain their rarity, as if all plants were able to create Central Root Systems, they would likely be more common. It is argued that the conditions were not met for the plants that didn't join, but with the conditions remaining unknown, the theory isn't fully accepted.

Hive Mind Theory

The more popular Hive Mind Theory states that the Central Root System is its own entity, apart from the plants. These entities form beneath the earth in an amorphous state of life, despite not having a physical form. These entities then take in plant roots in order to create a physical form for itself. Questions around the theory mainly revolve around its capabilities of movement, as the Hive Mind Theory does not consider the CRS to be able of movement in a normal sense. Arguments have also formed over the limited communication, as Floraduc Alvearium is only able to reach certain stages with enough plants tied to it, instead of being always able.

This illness, nicknamed "Deep Root Disease" or "Entity 105 Effect" forms the basis of the theory name.

Below is a list of known instances of Entity 105, and their general status towards wanderers. The total number of any Central Root System can only be estimated, as no official numbers have been provided by the entity.

entrances.text: In Level 119, sliding through a slide colored in a muted aqua-blue, have a rare chance of leading one here. Peculiarly, once the individual has gotten in the level, the slide behind will anomalously disappear despite the absent of Non-Euclidean properties.

Far in the grass field surrounding Level 105, a strange chain-wrapped pillar may suddenly emerge from the ground randomly. Upon physical contact, the individual will be transported to this level via unknown means.
Within Level 66, a door that resembles the architecture of this level may lead here, although it is unreliable due to its scarcity.
In Level 38, if an interior of a residential unit appears bigger than what its exterior displays, there is a likelihood that remaining within long enough may transition the level to here.

exits.text: Rarely, in dimmer, and deeper areas, an unseen source constantly emitting noticeable waves of vibrations is where the entrance to Level 109 is located.
No-Clipping through a concrete wall with wet vines proliferating upon its surface, puts the individual to Level 112.
Finding an out-of-place, and clean sliding glass door, and entering it will lead the wanderer to Level 114.
There are few reports of encountering an Elevator embedded in the wall, although it is an unreliable exit due to apparently rarity.

A rare dilapidated, and botanically overgrown section is an entry point into either Level 131 or Level 177. Upon entry, however, the entrance anomalously disappears when the individual looks away, and any form of traces of it being there before are not left behind.

Entity 100.title: Entity 100
Entity 100.description:
"P" IS FOR PARASITE

ENTITY 100?

THE TRAGEDY OF A SLOW DESTRUCTION

I - THE BREAKING POINT

The gloomy office spaces of Level 4 never exactly struck me as "cheery". Dimmed into a cold gray lighting by the overcast environment beyond every gently tapping window, each second spent in this edifice felt slowly but surely crushing on one's soul. While, yes, it does invoke some sense of being back "home", with the patter of rain against the windows almost inducing the sweet scent of petrichor, there's something about this place that still chips away at my hope. Like vultures gnawing at the deceased remains of my memories of a better life.

I rested my face in my palms — my elbows prodded into the desk, and my swiveling chair releasing a light creak as I slouched further. The office was unusually quiet today; where I would otherwise be hearing cacophonies of chatter, there was only whispers complemented by utter silence.

"MITCHELL!" A stern voice rang out from beside me, nearly forcing me to jolt up from my chair."Y-yeah?" I stammered out as I faced the young assistant to my left."Apologies if that came across as a little startling, but I've got an important task for you. I'm sure you've heard about the death of one of our lead researchers, have you not?" She flipped through dividers in the stocky binder between her hands, eventually encountering a specific set of papers. "I've got a new entity for you to document, believed to be his cause of death." She slid a folder marked "UNDOCUMENTED - E27" across my desk before fading back into the silence.I stared blank-eyed at the folder with questions erupting in my head, though she had left to her own cubicle before I could sputter out a word. With a new stack of documents on my desk and a large bearing of duty, I was merely left dazed.

Level 894.title: Level 894
Level 894.description:
Level 894, nicknamed The Crystal Fields, is estimated to span 1,000,000km2. The grassland that makes up the level is luscious and never seems to grow beyond a certain extent. Omnipresent winds gently blow as the eternal drizzle pours down. Wind turbines are quite common and brand a mint-blue rhombus on both sides of the nacelle. Crystals are commonplace; they can be found littered throughout the fields and quietly hum. The skies are dreary yet pleasing. The sun that radiates the environment seems larger and warmer, while the level's natural satellite appears bluish in hue.

The wind turbines found in Level 894 reach 100 meters in height and have a rotor diameter of 180 meters. These wind turbines are in constant operation throughout the year and will reach 40 decibels at their loudest. The turbines would serve as a good shelter due to the crates of supplies found within. Another reason would be their immense durability. They've been theorized to be immune to physical damage. All tests conducted have concluded that this assumption is likely correct. Said tests ranged from basic gunfire and Liquid Pain to attacks from Entity 89, which has been seen effortlessly crushing vehicles and fortifications.

Furthermore, the wind turbines can not be tampered with. Such examples would include attempting to change the direction it's facing, shutting a turbine down, and other related things that are not possible. These structures would be more commonly used. However, an estimated 50% of all wind turbines are inaccessible, so their use is limited.
Level 894.lead: Level 894 is the 895th level of the Backrooms.

entrances.text: Wandering through the Turbine Fields of Level 231 long enough will cause it to seamlessly transition to this level.

exits.text: On rare occasions, uttering the name Jason may cause one's vision to fade to black. Upon waking up they will find themselves in Level 21.
Falling asleep or going unconscious in the snow will cause one to awake in Level 276. Wanderers who have used this exit have reported waking up with a fur coat covering their body.

Entity 145.title: Entity 145
Entity 145.description:
The Tear Mold, or Lacrymipathos sordidus, is an anomalous viral pathogen found within Backrooms. It flourishes abundantly in mold-ridden, humid environments. Tear mold grows in hanging slimy growths, blackish-green clumps from ceilings or walls, which look like tears. The incubation period is short, showing possible symptoms after between 12-24 hours, but at the onset, they are often mistaken as environmental irritants. Under the microscope, the virus looks similar to a mold cluster, which, unlike any pathogen, is presumably why researchers think it is an aberration. The design of the virus may enhance the facilitation of the hivemind connections late in infection, but the mechanism is still unclear.

The infected organisms look like skinny, thin figures with faces streaked with tears and skin blemished by oozing, greenish sores. Their eyes are often swollen and leaking a viscous, greenish fluid. The odd qualities of this virus produce changes in behavior that are more or less constant in those who join The Nucleus, the cult worshiping the virus as a divinity. Researchers hypothesize that Lacrymipathos sordidus possesses some psychogenic properties, possibly having a connection to the hivemind at later stages, which will compel its victims to perform uniform actions.
Entity 145.lead:
Infection Of Interest

Infection Type: Viral

Influence on Mind: 10/10

Hostility to Others: 9/10

Cult: Yes

Entity 27.title: Entity 27
Entity 27.description:
A Conceptual Presence cannot be described as a unique entity. Its classification as an entity is debatable at best.

Conceptual Presences are a result of the temporal degradation of an entity due to the error of noclip or atomic destabilisation, mostly due to environmental factors in SITU. A Conceptual Presence can be one or multiple entities that have merged together to form a glitching mess of entities. A conceptual presence can bear any appearance, but typically they cannot be comprehended by the human mind, therefore not visible. The visible types of conceptual presences act almost like a refracting prism- light bends and shifts in their warping, translucent bodies, releasing a rapidly changing spectrum of colours. Depending on what entity (or entities) has suffered the degradation, the noises produced will be different. Conceptual Presences are quite rare due to the specificity of how they are created, but if one is near a conceptual presence (regardless of visibility), there will be noticeable changes in the environment.

Direct contact with a Conceptual Presence will typically leave lingering effects, mostly found on the surfaces they walk on (an increase in radioactive emissions). Conceptual Presences themselves, due to how most of the time their creation is a result of radioactive atomic destabilisation (also, mainly a result of the error of noclip), Conceptual Presences emit radioactive isotopes perpetually (roughly 1.4 Sv/h). Thankfully, the radius of these emissions is quite small (2ft) and dies off quite quickly. This is obviously quite easily mitigated.

Conceptual Presences have existed since the first error of noclip (the time of this cannot be pinpointed). The first discovered Conceptual Presence was found in Level 3, encountered in the vast tunnel network. The Presence itself was quite small, but still did not fit in the tunnels and was contorted into itself to fit. The noises it produced seemed to indicate it was in pain despite the lack of any biological matter. This presence has, obviously, degraded entirely since discovery.

Not every entity is susceptible to temporal degradation - this phenomenon has only been reported to occur in entities with subhuman intelligence. This means entities such as Smilers cannot suffer this degradation, though the reasoning for this is currently being researched. This also cannot happen to entities that are particularly resistant to radiation or are incapable of noclipping entirely.

Entity 28.title: Entity 28
Entity 28.description:
----

----

----

  ◈ OVERVIEW

Trotting snails (designated as Entity 28) are a group of gastropod entities in the Backrooms resembling terrestrial snails, albeit with four defined limbs that extend below the base foot. Primarily inhabiting damp and lush environments, these entities are abundant in nearly all outdoor levels containing life. Trotting snails are slightly larger than the typical snails that inhabit Earth, scaling to approximately 5 inches in height. Trotting snails are peaceful omnivorous scavengers that majorly feed on leaves, detritus, fruit, and lichen.

Trotting snails play an important role in the ecosystem for lower-scale biota residing in the Backrooms; due to their increased mobility as opposed to regular snails, they contribute heavily to recycling nutrients throughout different environments via waste. Typically, this means that a concentrated abundance of nutrients (such as corpses, fungal growths, etc.) can be spread across an area through the excreta of trotting snails after feeding from the source. As a result, much of the microscopic life in a specific environment remains at a consistent rate and variety, which — as life is preyed on further up the food chain — helps to stabilize the ecosystem of their inhabited levels.
  ◈ BEHAVIOR
Trotting snails are best known for their unusual affinity with engaging with wanderers in friendly interactions. This behavior is believed to be a consequence of their high social tendencies among themselves — often branching out to other non-aggressive species in fulfilling exchanges. Likely due to convergent evolution, brains similar to those of intelligent cephalopods such as octopuses are present in trotting snails to support this behavior. Friendly encounters between different species tend to be short-lived, however, as they are greatly vulnerable while outside of their shell. Trotting snails are likewise skittish and unpredictable upon sensing the slightest notation of a potential threat. Social encounters between trotting snails occur for various reasons; most often for sharing resources or better survival against predators through herds. To earn trust between individuals, trotting snails will find "gifts" for others, consisting of leaves, rubble, carpet tufts, and other natural items in the Backrooms.
  ◈ BIOLOGY
The four legs of trotting snails are the sole major feature distinguishing them from typical gastropods. These legs are an extension from the base foot, reaching under the entity's body and enabling them to move via walking or running. As opposed to the snails of Earth, trotting snails have a far higher metabolism which allows for swift locomotion; consequently, their demand for energy is comparatively much higher. The bottoms of the limbs possess a lesser form of traction used for scaling steep surfaces.

----

----

----
