Entity 68.title: Entity 68
Entity 68.description:
Dream Bees, also classified as Entity 68, are small entities resembling honey bees from The Frontrooms. Dream Bees are distinguished by their distinct behaviors from honey bees and a pale, fuzzy thorax. Their stripes come in various colors, such as red, blue, orange etc, usually featuring two colors for the stripes of each Dream Bee.

Dream Bees have never been physically observed, only manifesting within the dreams of wanderers resting near their hives. Based on consistent dream reports, researchers estimated the length of adult Dream Bees to be about 12-15 millimeters in length, however. Larval and juvenile bees have never been recorded, and there is no evidence of a queen bee hierarchy. Some researchers speculate that Dream Bees operate collectively without central leadership. Dream Bees do not appear to reproduce in a biological sense. Instead, some suggest that they form directly in wanderers dreams.

Level 866.title: Level 866
Level 866.description:
Level 866 resembles the interior of the ancient pyramids of Giza in the Frontrooms with an infinite number of ancient corridors and rooms. Several torches and other artificial lights are scattered sparsely across the level, serving as the only illumination source. Level 866 is filled with a wide range of traps, with density varying for each area. The walls are adorned with paintings and small holes that lead into enormous bodies of sand that surround the entire level. There are also several environmental hazards like fires, earthquakes, blackouts, and caved ceilings. Level 866 is very hot, with its hottest recorded temperatures surpass 100 degrees Celsius, however, such temperatures are quite rare.

Level 866 runs low on many Backrooms resources, with Liquid Pain and Greek Fire being the most common, though they are still found in very small quantities. Other non-Backrooms resources, like water and nourishment, can commonly be found on corners and in hallways. Drinks and food here suffer a strange effect that causes them to look rotten, or miscolored, although it is still proper for consumption. Sand sometimes , which is often a warning of an encounter with The Pharaoh. Puzzles are often found when searching deeper areas; solving them will reveal minor rewards and small pieces of metal and gold.Hieroglyphic codes and phrases can be found all around Level 866. Solving them without a team of historians has been deemed impossible; however, if one can solve them, it is rumored they might unveil the secrets of this level: answers to the trials and puzzles, maps for secret areas, and other ammenities. Although, most of the solved codes have led to dead ends. The solved codes in question seem to be memorials to pharaohs, tax and other governmental documents, math equations, representations of gods, literature, etc.

There are a few small hidden rooms spread around Level 866; these rooms are much less guarded than The Tomb; they are guarded behind only one enigma, which, when solved, reveals several bottles of Almond Water and Achrophiline, along with aid, canned food, and jewelry. Strangely, these rooms often collapse and are buried by sand, possibly due to the old doors opening or nearby explorers' movements. It is a reason for historians and researchers to discourage the heists of the rooms to preserve Level 866's layout and historical importance.

Level 866 is continuously decaying as areas are buried by sand, and important historical artifacts are being burned or damaged by water. The integrity of the underground structure is becoming weak and loose, possibly due to the infinitely large pile of sand just above the level. The Tomb and its sections have only partially burned down. The resources are running out. The temperatures are rising, and artificial illumination is malfunctioning. The hieroglyphs have been deformed and erased beyond repair, as the sandstone becomes loose. It is predicted that the level will be completely destroyed in the following decades.
Level 866.lead:
Level866
Survival DifficultyClass 5
Environmental Hazards: 4/5Presence of Hidden Traps
Exit Difficulty: 4/5Hard to Exit
Entity Hazards: 5/5The Pharaoh
Level 866, also known as "Giza's Fate" or "The Pyramids", is the 867th level of the Backrooms. It was discovered on between the 1600s-1800s.

entrances.text: Accessing/no-clipping through pyramid-like objects in Level 999.
Entering ancient areas in Level 92.
Reading Egyptian quotes out loud in Level 46. The quotes are located in destroyed stone walls.
Reading history books in Level 80 has a 30% chance of leading here.

exits.text: Noclipping through strange, polished stone formations and structures may lead to Ad Memento or Level 999.
Playing with ancient toys may lead to Level 859.
No-clip through the great wall, and encounter salvation.

Entity 55.title: Entity 55
Entity 55.description:
The Tornado Head, formally designated as Entity 55 and scientifically identified as 'Ventocorpus enormis,' is a massive quadrupedal organism with holes whose full territorial footprint occupies approximately 2.6 square miles. Each of its four colossal limbs occupies an estimated 0.3 square miles at ground contact, rising like hollowed stone pillars across the landscape of a Level.

Its most unique feature is a vast cyclonic structure where its head would ordinarily be located. Unlike natural tornadoes, classified by the Enhanced Fujita (EF) scale and prone to rapid dissapearing, this vortex is unnaturally stable, it persists without fluctuation in wind speed or pressure. The formation appears to be a biomechanical extension of the entity’s physiology, which functions as both a sensory apparatus and a method of environmental consumption.

The vortex stretches high into the atmosphere, it pulls debris, soil, vegetation, and even small structures into its core before cycling the material downward into the entity’s internal cavities. It does not display the classic funnel shape of natural tornadoes; instead, resembles a tightly wound spiral of dark particulate matter interwoven with flashes of pale light; likely electrical discharges from internal pressure differentials.

This form, part colossal lifeform, part persistent atmospheric disturbance, presents a visual and structural anomaly, one that combines monumental architecture with destructive weather phenomena. Its scale ensures that it dominates the skyline of any Level where it appears, usually visible from miles away.

Entity 46.title: Entity 46
Entity 46.description:
Narco Jellyfish, designated as Entity 46 and scientifically known as Cephalonarcos paralytica is an aquatic Cnidaria akin to jellyfish as known from The Frontrooms. Though similar in appearance to that of certain Cnidaria, C. paralytica have attributes that make them unique from their Frontrooms cousins. Visually, Narco Jellyfish have a nearly transparent bell with venom packed oral arms underneath. These animals usually live in swarms, or blooms of several hundred individuals. They mostly reside near the surface of deep, dark, fresh water.

Due to the potential deadly nature of Narco Jellyfish, it is advised to avoid handling these animals and reporting all findings to appropriate groups with backrooms database access. If you find yourself in a level with potential blooms of C. paralytica, exercise extreme caution when entering any water source. If you find yourself in water with a C. paralytica bloom, swiftly exit the water or face potential paralysis and drowning.

The Umbra.title: The Umbra
The Umbra.description:
The Umbra

On your lowest day, he came to you with an offer.

He offered you a mask.

At first, you hesitated.

Who wouldn't? Every moment of that decision remains deeply engraved in your mind.

But you couldn't bear to see what you had become after that horrible incident.

So you took it.

It shouldn't have to be this complicated. But everything here is complicated.

And once you wear the mask, the challenge is not to keep wearing it but to take it off.

It's an odd feeling, to say the least. You run on desperation, on vengeance, and forget what the mask is truly meant to obscure.

A twisted purpose.

But that's now a quaint issue.

He tells you the script. What's bound to happen.

You'd scream. You'd say that's not real. But now, you can only stare.

And wait for doomsday.

By the time the clock struck, you had changed.

How could one live with this hell? This beckoning? You had no motivation to figure that out. No motivation to do anything at all, in fact.

But you now had to do your part of the deal. Your new life. Your new view.

The next moment, you were in a new world. A crumbling one.

The wanderers scurried, panicked, yelled.

But why weren't you scared?

What happened to your sentimentality? Your diligence? Is it too, hidden by the mask?

No matter. You were on duty.

You'd approach one of the yelling wanderers, offering them "help", or at least, that's what you thought.

A safe place. One that even the darkness cannot touch.

You knew it was a lie. A stage. But you didn't care. Not like you used to. For now, you are the one performing.

The talk felt like it was eons long. Eventually, the wanderer fell for the trick. The fate that awaited them was now sealed.

And now? You'd continue this same farce.

Wanderer by wanderer.

But it wouldn't be this simple. You knew it.

You figured that when suddenly, he called for you again.

Another order? It almost felt like you were playing his puppet.

There were 4 new people you now had to keep your eye on.

Ones defying his script.

Two souls whose minds unstained wander into chaos—

And another two who, in pursuit of repentance, seek to turn the tide.

How interesting.

You were confused. People were still trying to push back against a force beyond the reaches of humanity.

But who were you to say that?

Were you still a human?

You can't quite answer that yet.

And The Orchestrator wants his jobs done now and well.

It had been a long day. A lifetime's worth of watching the gazes of the panicked wanderers as you met them.

You retreated into whatever place could give you a lure of safety in this world.

As you took in your new surroundings, you could only ask yourself one thing.

Is this what was destined for you?

Before, you'd worry. Now, you see them all as pathetic little ants fighting for their lives.

Is this what was destined for you? To lose your very humanity?

No. That couldn't be true.

You stumbled. You could only do what was possible to humor your worthless denial.

But when you stared at the window, you finally met with the mask.

You remembered the deal you made.

He said the knowledge would help you survive with the fatal condition you had.

But there was a raw, bitter part of the deal you only now realize.

You are in places in which you shouldn't be.

You are involved in things you cannot control.

You are a cog in his machine.

You know things that you shouldn't.

The curse that was tearing you apart was gone, but now a new rot had appeared.

One that ate at you from the inside, crept into the edges of your vision.

You thought of calling for help.

But the mask covered anything lying beneath.

You were now but a shadow of your former self.

An umbra.

One that ate at you from the inside, crept into the edges of your vision.

You thought of calling for help.

You were now but a shadow of your former self.

An umbra.

Radiophobia.title: Radiophobia
Radiophobia.description:
Phenomenon 9-B, also known as Red Herrings, is a dangerous and life-threatening phenomenon which occurs within the Backrooms, a branch-off of Tulpaefication. It usually involves the manifestation of a tulpa around a wanderer experiencing trauma or depression, though it may appear unduly under other circumstances as well, which has made its nature extremely difficult to understand. Typically, the tulpa takes the form of an anthropomorphic figure in a red pinstripe suit and several other garments — all with a strange acorn motif — and a head which resembles a common technology in the day and age of manifestation.

These tulpae have collectively been christened "Ratatosk the Reaper" by the wider community (every instance of the phenomenon has been known to call itself by the same name as well), and is often considered some sort of "god" who rules over the Achospic Plane. As anyone from the Antulpaic Order can tell one, this is a frankly absurd idea: as intrepid tulpologists, our members have never once reported a specimen of Phenomenon 9-B that was not in the gestation period. Why exactly the tulpa always appears assuming a similar form is unknown, though may be due to this "mythical entity's" widespread infamy across the Backrooms.

Instances of Phenomenon 9-B have been known to begin as mere intrusive whispers in the heads of their victims, which eventually leads to negative thoughts of self-hatred and self-doubt; eventually enough negative psychic energy will be collected for the tulpa to appear with a physical form, typically by emerging from a unit of technology somewhere near its prey — thus giving the illusion that this "mythical entity" of sorts is able to traverse instantaneously through units of technology all about the Backrooms.

At this stage, the tulpa, radiating an anomalously strong aura of psychic radiation, will begin to feed more negative energy into the victim, either psychically or verbally, eventually driving its prey insane — though its purpose for this is largely unknown. Various instances of the phenomenon have been known to tell their victims "to relax their mind" and "not to resist"; this is likely a trick to make the victim more susceptible to this mind-breaking psychic radiation, thus simplifying the tulpa's job. Various records have detailed several unlucky wanderers falling prey to this deception, though many were able to resist the radiation for long enough that our task forces were successfully able to dispatch the tulpa in time.

A final note: it seems that several specimens of Phenomenon 9-B have shown signs of being completely docile, only wishing to converse with their would-be victims. This tends to happen with "prey" that are less mentally unstable, and may be a sign that the phenomenon is not entirely malicious; of course, more research is needed for this statement to be fully confirmed.
Radiophobia.lead:
I hate the Backrooms.

My name is [REDACTED].

When I was younger, but a carefree child up there...

My father was killed in a car crash.

As an only child, I watched my mother's mental state unravelling.

I did my best to help her, but nothing worked.

Then one day, I awoke in these yellow halls.

I had been separated from my mother...

When she needed me most.

I spent so long wandering these halls in despair.

My mind kept telling me I should just give up and die.

That I wasn't enough for my family.

I could have saved my father.

I could have saved my mother.

I just didn't care enough.

At least, I thought it was my mind saying all that.

That all changed one day, when I met a faceling girl.

We fell in love, and were married.

I joined the Backroom Colonists.

But nothing happy lasts in this damp labyrinth...

On our first anniversary, her blood ran clear.

I spiralled into depression again, and quit my job at the Colonists.

Those thoughts returned to my head...

I really thought I could be good enough.

I thought I could start over.

But redemption isn't a thing.

I will never be good enough.

Not for my wife, my mother, my father.

I deserve to die.

No, I told myself, I must find the will to continue living, even with this grief. I must push through. I cannot die here.

But still, the thoughts consumed me...

On my lowest day, he came to me with an offer.

He told me that he was the one putting all those thoughts in my mind.

He told me that I should stop resisting him.

He told me that this was the only way to achieve salvation.

At that point, I was already too broken to care.

Instead of fighting it, I let his words wash over my body.

It's strangely calming.

I close my eyes.

This is the end for me...

But what does it matter?

I never deserved to live anyway.

ENTITY NO.
222

THREAT LEVEL:
SQUIRREL

Habitat:
The Void

Aggression: 4/5
Attacks on a whim

Intellect: 6/5
Godly levels of intellect and cognition

Danger: 5/5
Attacks are fatal without fail

Entity 222, also known as Ratatosk the Reaper, is perhaps one of the most enigmatic and terrifying beings in the entirety of the Backrooms.

You are worthless...

You don't even deserve to be alive...

What good do you bring to the table?

You're just a speck of dust floating in a vast cosmos.

Behemoth.title: Behemoth
Behemoth.description:
BEHEMOTH.
Learn his name, learn to fear him.

I have a horrid truth for you.

The M.E.G. say that there is a good reason that not many know about this entity. Regardless of whether or not I agree with that notion, I will share this information with you - gathered from any loose documents I could get my hands on. You must know what there is to know. To never trespass on his abode, to never worship his grand and terrible power and most importantly - to understand the insanity he brings.

He is not the kind to attack viciously, but rather the kind that lies in wait. He has never 'killed' in the traditional sense. As long as you are in his domain, you will be at his mercy. His gaze is piercing - you will never truly be safe from it, no matter how well you hide yourself. You might spot him out of the corner of your eye once or twice; you might get a look at his ghastly visage peering down at you through a gap in the ceiling. But that's already more than enough.

Fortunately, he usually only ever ventures to levels directly tied to his realm. But that's not necessarily a guideline. You may be wandering through a supposedly safe level and glimpse him at the end of a long hallway, only for him to disappear the moment you blink. Or you could spot him in the middle of a horde of entities pursuing you through the depths of the Backrooms. Behemoth is an unpredictable monster, one whose mind can never truly be comprehend by humans. An eldritch creature existing for centuries. A timeless beast, immortal. The one mystery we will never solve. He has been here long before us, and will be here long after we depart. His legacy is unending, inflicting many generations of minds with unparalleled madness.

Yet still, cults have risen and fallen to worship his madness. None could ever comprehend the scale of his wrath, but they still praise him with endless devotion. I could name ten of them right now if I really wanted to. Whether it was of their own volition, or another nasty trick up Behemoth's sleeve we may never know.

To be blunt with you - that's all I can say about him. Anything else he does is unknown as of now. But you may wonder, why is he being hidden? In the end, anyone who sees him suffers the same fate, so why hide the truth? I've inquired with many of my coworkers - and they all spout the same nonsense back at me. "It's to keep the public safe" is what they all say.

They keep telling me that my obsession with learning more about Behemoth will lead down a horrific rabbit-hole;  that I'll be no better than the mad men they occasionally stumble across while exploring.

But now it's dawning on me. To be known is his greatest strength. To glimpse him is to stare into the eyes of madness. So isn't this what he wanted all along?

Isn't this what he wanted all along? BEWARE THE DEEDS DONE FOR THE PRINCE OF MADNESS

BEWARE THE ONES WHO HE HAS TAKEN PRISONER

BEWARE THE MOST GRISLY FATE HE BRINGS

The one mystery we will never solve. He has been here long before us, and will be here long after we depart. His legacy is unending, inflicting many generations of minds with unparalleled madness.

Yet still, cults have risen and fallen to worship his madness. None could ever comprehend the scale of his wrath, but they still praise him with endless devotion. I could name ten of them right now if I really wanted to. Whether it was of their own volition, or another nasty trick up Behemoth's sleeve we may never know.

To be blunt with you - that's all I can say about him. Anything else he does is unknown as of now. But you may wonder, why is he being hidden? In the end, anyone who sees him suffers the same fate, so why hide the truth? I've inquired with many of my coworkers - and they all spout the same nonsense back at me. "It's to keep the public safe" is what they all say.

They keep telling me that my obsession with learning more about Behemoth will lead down a horrific rabbit-hole;  that I'll be no better than the mad men they occasionally stumble across while exploring.

But now it's dawning on me. To be known is his greatest strength. To glimpse him is to stare into the eyes of madness. So isn't this what he wanted all along?

Isn't this what he wanted all along? BEWARE THE DEEDS DONE FOR THE PRINCE OF MADNESS

BEWARE THE ONES WHO HE HAS TAKEN PRISONER

BEWARE THE MOST GRISLY FATE HE BRINGS

Entity 92.title: Entity 92
Entity 92.description: Entity 92 (or "Worrids") are elusive creatures that can reveal themselves to more than just people. When an individual becomes self-conscious, a Worrid begins to appear. It is first seen watching from a distance, remaining still for a while before leaving its post and beginning to make its way toward its victim. Eventually, it draws closer and strikes when the individual is distracted and not paying attention. Most encounters end fatally. If the affected person forgets about their insecurity, the Worrid disappears. These entities are not hallucinations—they are visible to all nearby observers. As a result, if one member of a group experiences it, the rest do as well. Though Worrids differ in appearance, each one shares a consistent structure: a head, horns, arms, and claws.

Orchestrator.title: Orchestrator
Orchestrator.description:
The Orchestrator

At first, they will be invisible.

To remain casual is to remain blissfully oblivious of what is going on behind the scenes.

But there is something. Something always there.

Strings.

Thin lines tethering you to this hell you've become familiar with.

But who? Why? When?

Should you have pondered this question, you've probably been able to see them. And you'd have a million questions, answers? None.

You'd try to look up to see who is pulling, who is holding, and nothing but the monotony of The False Reality would meet the naked eye.

So, let's cut to the chase.

If you're here, reading this, it's because you want to understand. Find this answer that has laid out of reach for so long ever since you stepped foot here.

Don't worry. For your answers lie here.

But before we begin, let's make an agreement.

I am but a pawn. Another vessel belonging to man, who has took his time to write stories, because that's all they could do.

Everything that I tell you, everything that I know, I chose to share it with you.

Who knows if perhaps we've even met before? Maybe physically, or through ink on paper. No matter.

When you read these... I hope you're aware the things you're learning, they are but part of another script. Another scrap in a huge machine.

For both of our sakes, don't cross your lines. Don't try to burn these strings.

For if you do, you'll be involving yourself in tribulations beyond your comprehension.

Don't take this as an ominous warning. Take it as advice between two old friends.

So, now that I've established my terms, let's seal the deal.

Entity 33.title: Entity 33
Entity 33.description:
◊   do not speak in their presence

◊   and maybe, just maybe, you'll be spared

  ◊ OVERVIEW

Reticents, catalogued as Entity 33, are an anomalous tripodal species with a singular central body above their many legs. Reticents commonly scale to ~8 feet in height, though they may grow up to 11 feet in height in levels that are unroofed. While having a generally tripodal body plan, the legs are in varying count and length which move precisely to keep the main body stable. The central mass of a reticent entangles a large security camera structure reaching above the entity's legs. Although this structure possesses the regular mechanical characteristics of security cameras, it is abnormally large in comparison. The camera's lens is mostly biological in contrast with a slit "pupil" that may dilate while focusing on a target. Reticents are excessively abundant in the Backrooms as a whole, with an estimated 9% of all lifeforms in its approximate size range belonging to the Entity 33 classification.

Extending from the sides of reticents' cameras are fanned-out feathery appendages interwoven with streams of copper and zinc wires. These appendages, serving as sound receptors, can be present in counts between three and seventeen on the "head" of an individual reticent. Reticents may also flare their feathery appendages as a threat display if necessary despite their already-daunting size.

  ◊ BEHAVIOR
The most characteristic attribute in the behavior of reticents is their aggression towards all forms of speech. What elicits reticents to become aggravated is not merely verbal communication, however; other sound-based forms of communicating such as Morse code are also identified as speech by reticents. While normally passive, with the exception of being harmed, reticents become instantaneously hostile towards any being that verbally communicates while in their vicinity. Multiple theories have risen as to how "speech" is recognized by the entity, including the two most prominent speculations described below:

RESPONSE THEORY
The Response Theory explains reticents' perception of speech through subtle responses from the same species of the speaker. In the case of humans, for example, other nearby humans will make exceptionally subtle movements upon processing words from a speaker. Via this theory, reticents are able to uniquely distinguish these responses (and, by extent, the speech patterns themselves upon long enough exposure).

PSYCHIC ENERGY THEORY
The Psychic Energy Theory states that reticents can sense unique movements of psychic energy that occur through speech. Because speech in itself is a verbal method of transferring thoughts, it creates ripples of psychic energy in the area that carry the "thought code" within. Reticents then sense these ripples, likely through their fanned-out feathers, and act accordingly.

Once aggravated via speech, reticents will lock their sight onto the aggressor and watch for any further speech. This behavior is known as transpecting, in which the reticent in question will follow the target for prolonged periods (typically about 2-3 days at least) and wait until the aggressor verbally communicates again. Once it does so, the reticent will begin to chase their target at common speeds of 20 km/h within roofed levels, which can extend to 28 km/h within unroofed levels. Reticents have approximate intelligence to crows and octopi; they will take cover when excessively threatened, can use tools when possible, and can infer from past experiences to solve problems. To kill its target, reticents will trample it with the pointed ends of their limbs, which can effectively cause complete death within 10 seconds of persistent trampling.

When in a non-aggravated state, reticents are otherwise passive and will allow one to approach them if they do not inherently appear as a threat. Being somewhat social, occasionally seen forming "packs" up to 4 members in size, reticents may often be seen posing in odd, robotic patterns (speculated to be their means of communication). Reticents are, however, extremely aggressive towards smilers, dullers, solarnoids, and aranea membri regardless of whether they are provoked; for this reason, reticents are oftentimes spared outside smaller colonies as they can assist in weeding out the greater threats mentioned.

  ◊ ANATOMY
Reticents share a significant semblance in general anatomy with biological life, with exception for the mechanical structure serving as their head. The legs of reticents are supported both by an internal bone and a thin cartilage serving as a rigid frame. These features, in combination with the muscle specialization in the frontal legs, contribute to making their movements efficient in trampling and gutting targets. Although their limbs are comparatively thin, the internal bone is strengthened by microscopic "fibers" of lead incorporated in the marrow. The lead fibers are aligned in such a way that blunt impact force is distributed along the bone and reduces chances of the bone breaking. Within the security camera head is an anatomical portion that serves as a scraping tongue; it is thought that reticents use this to consume metals found in their respective levels, which is then incorporated into their bodies.

Reticents' legs, excluding what serves as their bones, are entirely edible to humans. Many distinct cultures across the Backrooms have incorporated reticent leg meat in some form into their cuisine, primarily due to their abundance in the Backrooms. The meat has a strong and savory flavor with a slightly sour aftertaste that can be masked with certain Backshroom-based seasonings. Meat closer to the torso of a reticent generally has a hint of electric-burnt flavor and is regularly incorporated into fertilizer.

  ◊ HISTORY
Reticents, in part due to their abundance in the Backrooms, have been a prominent presence in the history of various groups and settlements. One of the most common impacts of their presence near humans is a method to signal others to not speak. In many Anglo-based settlements/nations (notably the M.E.G. and R.E.U.), this manifests as a "shushing" hand signal where one places their index finger against their lips. However, this hand signal has gradually become more universal. In colonies with improved technological accessibility, such as those of populated nations in easily accessible levels, there is a "lighting code" in which lights in the locale are turned a muted purple to signal reticent presence.

Entity 52.title: Entity 52
Entity 52.description:
Entity 52, more commonly known as a Spike Bear, is a towering, bear-like creature measuring approximately 3–4 meters (9.8–13.1 ft) in height. It's covered in dense black quills, reminiscent of oversized porcupine spines, which appear capable of resisting steel impacts. Spike Bears have multiple glossy, spider-like eyes arranged across their foreheads, each reflecting light in an unsettling, glassy sheen. The exact number of eyes varies, with most survivor reports counting between six and eight, although some claim to have seen more in dim lighting. Some speculations suggest that Spike Bears are just one entity that can shapeshift its number of eyes and size.

A Spike Bear's presence is often described as deeply unsettling, even before being spotted. Wanderers report a heavy, oppressive sensation, as if they are under intense observation. Spike Bears have the unnatural ability to paralyze, or cause dread, when making eye contact. Despite their terrifying appearance, Spike Bear's behaviors are not inherently aggressive. They demonstrate a rare kind of character, displaying both protective and hostile actions (depending on the circumstances, environment, and possibly the emotional state of the observed person).

Entity 150.title: Entity 150
Entity 150.description:
Oculi Fungorum are small, red mushrooms found within grassy plains. Their appearance is similar to an Amanita muscaria, circular red caps with white warts all over. They usually have a veil underneath the cap and have a usually white or beige stipe. When taken out of their position (not recommended) they don’t leave behind any mycelium or anything underground. Oculi Fungorum are entities and not mushrooms, despite their fungal looks, as inside them you can find bone structure, muscle, organs and more.

They contain gills, which, upon a person coming too close, will release spores. If one were to breathe in these spores, they would immediately contract Morbus Spororum Oculi. This virus is 100% fatal if not treated, meaning it is best to stay away from any sort of red mushroom found in fields.

Entity 82.title: Entity 82
Entity 82.description:
Entity 82, known colloquially as Growlers, is a species of hostile entities found in a large majority of levels in the Backrooms, preferably in levels that take place indoors, with some exceptions. Growlers thrive the best in dark, unpopulated areas where they can go undisturbed for lengthy periods of time. These entities are fairly dangerous, so wanderers should avoid encountering a Growler if possible.

Growlers take the uncanny appearance of a blobby mass of tangled, randomly colored electrical wires with no definite shape. Instances of Entity 82 are able to effortlessly contort and manipulate their bodies to navigate their surroundings or to achieve a goal, but their typical stance resembles a headless quadruped. This species, while having variable sizes, is most often roughly two meters tall, but some can be much smaller or much larger. Individual Growlers also exhibit variable colors in wires, which is partly genetic. While most Growlers are a mess of random primary colors, some are grey-scale, some have no primary colors, and others are monotone. Additionally, Growlers are also much stronger than the average wanderer and are surprisingly heavy, with the average weight being upwards of 400 kilograms.
Entity 82.lead: Dark Mode highly recommended for this read! Light is not supported well.

Entity 300.title: Entity 300
Entity 300.displayTitle: Entity 300: The Body of Jennifer Hoffmann
Entity 300.description:
Entity 300: The Body of Jennifer Hoffmann

Aggression: 4/5 ---
Intellect: 0/5 ---
Danger: 4/5

Notice from the Kauer Research Organization:
Due to the elusive nature of Entity 300 and the lack of information about Jennifer Hoffman, this document is in an incomplete state. Until completed, the page will serve as a collection of logs about those who've interacted with the entity. Testimonies in this page may contain inaccurate information, as accurate documentation of Entity 300 continues to be a challenge.

---File-001 : Survivor's Account of Entity 300
"I think I first noticed the entity when I was traveling in Level 324. I was inside one of the gas stations just looking for supplies. When I got close to the exit, I noticed a collection of Smilers outside, I think. They noticed me as well, so they started coming inside, and I hid. For the time, I thought I was doing a good job avoiding them. Naturally you'd assume that, maybe it was the confidence getting to my head or something."

"Anyway, I got to the door, and the entities noticed me. Obviously I was going to race out of the building, yea? But once I stepped before the door, some bitch showed up in front of me. It looked human, with straight black hair and a bruised figure. If I wasn't so scared at that moment, I would've probably put some more bruises on them. The thing stared at me, slowly moving forward as it flickered in and out of my vision. (I) was moving faster than it, but I couldn't get the strength to just race by her. Something about her presence made me feel guilty. Like merely being around her made me a monster."

"But as quick as it came, it left, and I could feel the slimy breath of a Smiler going down my neck. I raced out of there after that and found an exit within a couple minutes. Something about that encounter though, it still plays through my head. It was just some normal oddity you'd find in the Backrooms, yea? But I can recall it so clearly though. For the few seconds we saw each other, it felt like there was a whole conversation happening between us. She sounded hurt, screaming at me that it was my fault."

"I don't know what was my fault, she never told me. But I do feel guilty about it."

---File-002 : Survivor's Account of Entity 300
"I can't be fucked to figure out what part of Level 913 I was in when it happened. It's a lot of trains and I was climbing between them, that's what I'm sure of. At the moment, I think I had just got away from some Imitations, and I was pretty damn tired. My chest was rising and falling quick and whatever, and at the time I didn't have any Almond Water on my person. So I was just sitting in one of the rail cars at the time. My friend at the time was across from me and he was looking to some of the other train cars, maybe to find a path through them? I don't know what he was thinking at the time, and I can't really ask him anymore, heh."

"What were we talking about at the time? I think it was something about escaping the level. We had been there for a few days at that point, and it was driving us insane, obviously. Not sure what level we were hoping to get to, but I'd end up in Level 11 after everything went down, which isn't that bad. Would've rather we found some place to noclip out rather than have to reach some documented exit though."

"Back to the incident, sorry. Before my friend was fucking crushed, we both hear whooshing from above us. Jokingly, we debated on who was gonna check, and I was volun-told to. Not a big deal. So I poked my head out of the train car and saw nothing. Instead, I immediately heard a large crashing noise coming from right nearby. I prolly' jumped like a foot into the air out of terror. And when I finally checked, my friend wasn't there. He was buried under metal framing from the train and nothing else. The mess of the situation made me numb at that moment. I didn't try to pull the rebar off him."

"Maybe I left him to die, but it was clear we had no chance either way to get him help. That's just how the Backrooms is yea? You don't know when the person next to you will just... disappear. I hope he clipped into another level, but I doubt it. There was a lot of red on those metal pieces."

---File-003 : Survivor's Account of Entity 300
"It was a really nice day in fact, sun was out and I was just traveling across Level 300 at the time. I can't quite recall if I was with my friend Kelly at the time, since this was now a few weeks ago since I came across it. It doesn't matter too much though. When I first spotted Entity 300, it was when I was kneeling on the ground and looking over my supplies. What that supplies was, couldn't tell you. But I'd look up to see some sort of figure blotting out the sun. If I didn't move, I'd probably be dead."

"So I scrambled to the side and BANG, there was a body on the ground like half a meter away from me. It looked to be some sort of woman, but her face was looking toward the ground and I didn't want to look further into it. It also smelled like shit. Like it was very fucking bad, Kelly might've gagged from the scent but I don't recall. The people in the Backrooms tend not to smell good, but this was far above that. It smelled like boiled pee mixed with a bunch of other rotten scents; spoiled milk, bad eggs, you know."

"I got back onto my feet pretty quickly afterward too I can't confirm if we booked it right away, but we definitely started to run once another body fell to the ground. At that point we didn't turn to look either, just kept hearing ugly slamming noises as corpses rained down from above us. I remember asking whoever was with me if this was normal, and they didn't give a response."

"Once we got into Level 173, the falling bodies stopped. The silence was weirdly uncomfortable. I turned around then to see if I could see whatever was following us, but there was nothing there. Just me, Kelly, and the quiet beating of the river. It wouldn't leave my mind for a while. I think I was wondering about what I would've saw if I turned around. Would it have been the same person, disappearing and reappearing in the sky, just to try and crush us again? Unsure, but I doubt it would have have been fun to look at."

---File-004 : Survivor's Account of Entity 300
"I think I was too busy looking over the washing machines and being self conscious to notice when the girl showed up. It was a laundromat after all, I was gonna try and clean some of my clothes. Ya' can't really find a shower around in the Backrooms unless you're at some sort of base. Which yea there is one in Level 291, but I didn't find it at the time. So I'm standing there, staring at the washing machine I was using and hoping it didn't become some sort of monster that'd eat all my clothes."

"At the time I was near a door too, a door to another chamber of laundry machines but a door. So I turn to look at it, and see someone standing there. Her head was jerking around like crazy and her clothes looked torn up. It seemed like some people attacked her. And damn she smelled, I should have realized by that point that I was staring at a corpse but... I don't know, I was too busy thinking about my own clothing. I think I asked her if she needed some money for the machines. Just some casual conversation to defuse the tension. She didn't seem to take kindly to the question though, as her body started slowly moving closer."

"Then I heard her talk, her mouth didn't move, but the voice was definitely coming from her."

Er hat mich ausgetrickst!

"It was like a fucking banshee. But as soon as she said it, she disappeared. I don't know what she said, or what I did, to deserve being yelled at by a ghost. I found a base later and shared the story there. That's how I got here, heh."
Entity 300.lead:
Entity 300: The Body of Jennifer Hoffmann

Aggression: 4/5 ---
Intellect: 0/5 ---
Danger: 4/5

"Maybe I left him to die, but it was clear we had no chance either way to get him help. That's just how the Backrooms is yea? You don't know when the person next to you will just... disappear. I hope he clipped into another level, but I doubt it. There was a lot of red on those metal pieces."

---File-003 : Survivor's Account of Entity 300
"It was a really nice day in fact, sun was out and I was just traveling across Level 300 at the time. I can't quite recall if I was with my friend Kelly at the time, since this was now a few weeks ago since I came across it. It doesn't matter too much though. When I first spotted Entity 300, it was when I was kneeling on the ground and looking over my supplies. What that supplies was, couldn't tell you. But I'd look up to see some sort of figure blotting out the sun. If I didn't move, I'd probably be dead."

"So I scrambled to the side and BANG, there was a body on the ground like half a meter away from me. It looked to be some sort of woman, but her face was looking toward the ground and I didn't want to look further into it. It also smelled like shit. Like it was very fucking bad, Kelly might've gagged from the scent but I don't recall. The people in the Backrooms tend not to smell good, but this was far above that. It smelled like boiled pee mixed with a bunch of other rotten scents; spoiled milk, bad eggs, you know."

"I got back onto my feet pretty quickly afterward too I can't confirm if we booked it right away, but we definitely started to run once another body fell to the ground. At that point we didn't turn to look either, just kept hearing ugly slamming noises as corpses rained down from above us. I remember asking whoever was with me if this was normal, and they didn't give a response."

"Once we got into Level 173, the falling bodies stopped. The silence was weirdly uncomfortable. I turned around then to see if I could see whatever was following us, but there was nothing there. Just me, Kelly, and the quiet beating of the river. It wouldn't leave my mind for a while. I think I was wondering about what I would've saw if I turned around. Would it have been the same person, disappearing and reappearing in the sky, just to try and crush us again? Unsure, but I doubt it would have have been fun to look at."

---File-004 : Survivor's Account of Entity 300
"I think I was too busy looking over the washing machines and being self conscious to notice when the girl showed up. It was a laundromat after all, I was gonna try and clean some of my clothes. Ya' can't really find a shower around in the Backrooms unless you're at some sort of base. Which yea there is one in Level 291, but I didn't find it at the time. So I'm standing there, staring at the washing machine I was using and hoping it didn't become some sort of monster that'd eat all my clothes."

"At the time I was near a door too, a door to another chamber of laundry machines but a door. So I turn to look at it, and see someone standing there. Her head was jerking around like crazy and her clothes looked torn up. It seemed like some people attacked her. And damn she smelled, I should have realized by that point that I was staring at a corpse but... I don't know, I was too busy thinking about my own clothing. I think I asked her if she needed some money for the machines. Just some casual conversation to defuse the tension. She didn't seem to take kindly to the question though, as her body started slowly moving closer."

"Then I heard her talk, her mouth didn't move, but the voice was definitely coming from her."

Er hat mich ausgetrickst!

"It was like a fucking banshee. But as soon as she said it, she disappeared. I don't know what she said, or what I did, to deserve being yelled at by a ghost. I found a base later and shared the story there. That's how I got here, heh."

Entity 115.title: Entity 115
Entity 115.description:
⠀⠀Backrooms Dossier | Sluggards ⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀ - 🗖 X

Sluggards, designated Entity 115, are humanoid slug-like organisms that average around 5'4". They are characterized by their sluggish behavior, high intelligence, and abnormal convergent evolution to that of Frontrooms slugs. Sluggards communicate with a variety of clicking and throat noises, making human to sluggard communication exceedingly difficult. Prior to the human colonization of the Backrooms, sluggards lived alongside Old Birds and various other sentient creatures that inhabited the Backrooms prior.

Sluggards live and have lived in tribal-esque societies for thousands of years. Sluggard tools have been found littered around levels, often dating back at least 500 years prior. It is likely that sluggards pass down tools between generations to save on resources, as seized items have wear that would depict such. It should be noted that sluggards must make specialized tools due to their peculiar arm structure, that being reminiscent to a slug's foot. This arm structure makes it challenging for sluggards to work with tools made for humans and other similar entities, such as Cascadans.

From the waist down sluggards have a singular appendage that is near identical to a slug foot. Sluggards move by producing waves with muscular contractions in this foot. They additionally produce a similar secretion to Frontrooms slugs in order to lower friction, making movement much easier. Sluggards can walk at a similar pace to the average human, though they are easily outran due to their method of movement. Because of this reason, sluggards often find themselves hunting from afar. Utilizing spears optimized for throwing and wooden crossbows. It should be noted that regular bows are challenging for the sluggards to use due to their unique arm and hand shape.

Sluggards, while they do hunt, prioritize scavenging over anything. Some sluggard habitats were found within moderate distance to Old Birds settlements. Some sluggard tribesman are occasionally spotted inside of the Old Bird habitats, eating edible litter and some forms of discarded trash that is inedible to humans. In this way, it is a sort of symbiotic relationship between the two species: the sluggards remove and eat the discarded rubbish and the Old Birds benefit from this as the litter is disposed of.

Level 268.1.title: Level 268.1
Level 268.1.description:
Level 268.1 is a sublevel connected to Level 268, and resembles a decayed and brutally cramped residential complex where disorder and age dominate every corner. The structure is built from concrete, majority of it sections are painted in green colors that has begun to peel, flake, or fade away, which exposes dull grey surfaces beneath. The ceiling is unusually low, and positioned just above 5-4 feet, which presses down on individuals and contributes to the overall feeling of confinement.

The layout forms a dense and unorganized network of hallways and rooms that leads into one another without clear pattern. The interiors have house furnitures and household items, all of which appear rotten, broken, or covered in layers of dust. Objects are rarely positioned with almost any sense of order, and piled together in cluttered heaps or collapsed in corners, which creates the sense of abandonment. Bedrooms with beds and frames sag because of their weight, blankets are messy, and closets contain clusters of decayed clothes. Kitchens, on the other hand, contain stoves, refrigerators, and utensils are rusty and some fragmented into pieces.

Unlike its parent level, this sublevel features windows. These reveal an outside environment that appears to be a narrow walkway lined by sheets of corroded metal fencing and overgrown plants beyond it. Along these paths are flowerpots filled with plants, some of them are withered away, and clotheslines run along with wet clothes swaying in air. Access to this outer area is through green-painted doors that are outlined with metal frames, and which connect the inside network of rooms to the outdoor passages. The sunlight constantly changes in strength; sometimes subtle and cool, and other times warmer and heavier. Its temperature fluctuates irregularly between 10°C to 25°C.

Although the majority sections of Level 268.1 is defined by its harsh low ceilings, rare sections exist where the ceiling is unusually high. These spaces feel significantly different from the rest of the complex. The walls in these areas stretch several meters higher, although stay painted in the same patchy green paint and dust, usually streaked with water stains that run downward in trails.

Within these rooms built the high ceilings, the air behaves strangely. Temperature changes violently between freezing cold and oppressive heat without any rhythm from 0°C to 40°C. The violent changes leave condensation dripping from the walls one moment, and dry into a suffocating stillness the next. The instability of these spaces makes them feel volatile.

Individuals who had explored these areas describe seeing cryptic symbols, patterns, and fragmented texts written in their visions or on the upper walls or across the ceiling surface. The symbols never stay long enough to read or study and dissolve into nothingness if one attempts to focus too intently. Their presence suggests that the structure itself may be attempting to communicate in some forgotten or coded language.

In opposition to the densely cluttered rooms of the main complex, some rare sections of Level 268.1 have less objects. These areas are characterized by bland concrete floors and walls. Furniture here is absent or reduced to isolated remnants: a single cracked chair, a lonely mattress collapsed in a corner, or an empty frame of a table. The emptiness of these rooms contributes to their unsettling quality. Where majority of the sublevel are filled with disorder and piles of belongings, these spaces feature nothing and strange silence.

In some hallways, the minimalism becomes extreme. Long passages with nothing but bland concrete, lined by vacant alcoves that suggest furniture was once present. In rarer cases, the walls show rectangular discolorations where shelves or cabinets might once have been placed. These areas induce strong feelings of the sublevel being affected by Phenomenon 15, although it is unconfirmed due to common reports of individuals finding eachother.

entrances.text: Exploring a completely dark area within Level 268, may lead to an area that is only illuminated by a ray of sunlight which is coming from a torn down door that directs here.
Although this can be dangerous, breaking a severely cracked green-painted wall within Level 268, with a hammer, or any weapon, can lead to this sublevel.
Wandering extremely far enough into Level 268, has been reported by some individuals to transition the level into Level 268.1.

exits.text: Going back through the door where one entered the sublevel from, obviously leads back to Level 268. Although commonly, the door may be sealed right after entry by a brick wall that appears new, as if something unseen intentionally built it in a blink of an eye.

A strangely clean locked door with the number 269 displayed on it, can be unlocked by a Level Key that has the same number, and which the door opens to Level 269. The Level Key can be seen placed under piles of book, in the back of a closet, or in a drawer.
A rusty exit grate door covered by wet hanging clothes in an area near a Darkener territory, exits back to Level 268. Finding this exit can be dangerous due to the danger of The Darkeners.
Opening gray closets is reported by some individuals to open to Level 268, but this method is still not confirmed, which can be unreliable.

Entity 78.title: Entity 78
Entity 78.description:
⤳ Overview
Entity 78, colloquially known as "Kavaroaches", and scientifically as "blaberus cutivorus", is an entity closely resembling the real-world gromphadprhina portentosa (Madagascar hissing cockroach). They're native to levels that have humid or unsanitary conditions, around stagnant water sources and below debris. The size of one varies between 0.5-1 centimeters (0.15-0.39 inches). From what's known, their origins are unkown like to be a work of the Backrooms.

 ⤳ Biology
Their exoskeleton can withstand corrosive elements, crushing forces, and large impacts. The body is made up of screlites, connected by flexible joints. It is to mention that the screlites are more durable in the abdomen, for reasons discussed later. The coloring of the body is a dark reddish coloring, getting redder in parts with higher segmentation.

The claws are specialized for breaking down flesh easily, with the mandible being in proximity to it too. The purpose of this is to support their carnivorous diets. Although the way the flesh is broken down is a lot more grotesque than it appeared. It grinds up the flesh, making it tender for the softer, less stronger mandible. This is claimed to be an effective hunting method; if one finds a host, it's almost certain they won't starve.

Kavaroaches have small antennae, no longer than quarter of their size, rarely extending beyond one-fourth of body size. The abbreviated structure limits the long-range sensory of host bodies. The structure, despite it's size, is equipped with dense clusters of chemoreceptors. This allows them to detect potential prey through heat and carbon dioxide concentration.

Reproduction happens asexually via eggs. The eggs are already located in the ootheca, which can be deposited voluntarily. Hatching happens when the parent manually opens the eggs with the claws. Alternatively they can also hatch due to high levels of carbon monoxide. Those hatches rarely survive, and in the rare scenario would result in the parent perishing.

Kavaroaches are equipped with two types of toxins, ingested into the host upon contact: a paralytic and an analgesic. The paralytic lasts approximately 2 hours after injecting, being used to enter the body. The analgesic lasts an indefinite period, being a sign of having a Kavaroach in your body. It's purpose is to mitigate pain during feeding.

 ⤳ Behavior

The hunting method of Kavaroaches follows a distinct sequence. Upon noticing a human host , they'd deliver a paralytic toxin through body parts easy to get to. Once the person is incapacitated, it will enter the body through available orifices: mouth, nose, ears. Using a method of instinctive pathfinding, they'd eventually reach the stomach where oviposition happens.

The human's body will serve as the space inhabited for years. Feeding at the surrounding tissue slowly, using it's senses to not go near organs, or major blood vessels. Due to the small size, it would take years for it to be noticed, but by then, it is too late. Even after death, they will inhabit the body, using it as a "settlement".

Hatching happens with parental intervision. After a certain period of time of living in a host, usually 1-1.5 years. Using it's sharp claws, it opens the ootheca, and allows the nymph to roam freely. It takes around 150 days for a nymph to reach adulthood, and once that's done, they will lay more eggs in the stomach. As more and more Kavaroaches start to fill the body, the human will eventually die during this time, regarded as the worst painless death. Sometimes, the Kavaroaches will start leaving the body, either trough previously mentioned orifices, or by digging it's way out

Coming upon an instance is uncommon due to high mortality rates in the wild. In the wild, out of six hatches only one — if any — reach adulthood. The high mortality rate is due to starvation from lack of prey, as many levels they inhabit have low human populations, almost zero. If prey is unavailable, they'll be forced into a scavenger lifestyle. During this period, the diets are made of entity remains and other natural scraps, although this is inefficient and in a few weeks death from malnutrition becomes inevitable.

In the wild, a Kavaroach would roam aimlessly looking for prey. They hide under debris, and commonly reside near stagnant water sources . They remain mostly solitary in these hiding spots, and only move when frightened. This is to avoid predators, as the majority of entities such as Deathmoths and Mercies hunt them.

 ⤳ Discovery
Kavaroaches are believed to have been endemic Level 427 for several centuries. The indigenous tribes of the level, especially the Chasm Watchers and the Huts of Crossroads, have history with them, being a part of early folklore. The downfall of certain tribes are even believed to have been caused by Kavaroach infestations.

The spread to other levels happened in the early 19th century, with the first levels to be inhabited being Level 34, and Level 350 and Level 440. This was the time when Kavaroaches were discovered by outside groups, and when most of the information was gathered of them. Certain medical procedures and medicine were made later, although many aspects of their physiology and behavior remained unkown for many decades

 ⤳ Do and Don't

Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls Y.title: Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls You Ugly (Joke)
Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls Y.displayTitle: Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls You Ugly (Joke)
Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls Y.description:
Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls You Ugly, sporting the scientific name Piscis ignominiae, is an extremely dangerous, individual anomalous entity primarily found in the darker regions of Level 0 and, on rarer occasions, in its sublevels. Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls You Ugly is well-known for its unusual methods of hunting. Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls You Ugly has emerged as a niche meme in online communities like Backchat, Backvideo, FBF, and Backblog, with many users joking about how absurd the entity is.

Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls You Ugly is virtually invincible, surviving gunshot wounds, fires, extreme temperatures, and other amenities like they are nothing. Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls You Ugly can float at a maximum between 300 and 1000 miles per hour, making it nearly undetectable to the naked eye on most occasions. Notably, Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls You Ugly's hearing and sight are heightened. It can hear the quietest sound from tens of meters away, and its sight is as excellent as that of an eagle.

Dead Fish That Floats Around And Calls You Ugly does not follow the natural laws of physics, most noticeably by floating in mid-air with no reasonable explanation, and its ungodly speed compared to other life forms in the Backrooms.

Entity 484/The Grim Reaper's Curse.title: Entity 484/The Grim Reaper's Curse
Entity 484/The Grim Reaper's Curse.description:
Commencing Reboot...

Enabling Services...

MEG.OS Successfully Installed...

Opening: Entity 484

Overview:

Entity 484 – known among superstitious wanderers as “The Grim Reaper’s Curse”, or simply as “the Reaper” – is a parasitic entity that presents itself in various shapes and forms depending on its victim. The creature itself is a hyperintelligent field of energy, one that can detect life and suitable targets around it with uncomfortable precision. When a host drifts into its vicinity, the field tightens, constantly measuring the wanderer’s state. Only then does a manifestation take shape, usually aligning itself with whatever the wanderer is most likely to trust. Although it can present itself at any time, the Reaper always arrives when its sufferer plans to depart.
If a victim finds 484’s embodiment, it will rarely be in a form they expect. The entity deliberately chooses configurations of itself that appear harmless, convenient, or even comforting. In colder levels, it will present itself as a source of heat, such as a fireplace or a winter coat. In other environments, it hides in whatever could attract attention. The entity prefers to blend in, to look and feel ordinary, even when it is clear it isn’t.
Once a body has been connected to a stronghold, their cellular energy will begin to drain almost immediately, dissipating into the surrounding air.  During this entire step, the victim rarely realizes what is happening, until it is too late. Slowly, the entity will eat away at life, deteriorating its user and removing their memories until nothing is left. And when the body can no longer fuel the process, the final part begins.
After the wanderer’s soul has been chained to a manifestation, it is forced to remain alive by the continual feeding of nearby energy. This trapped consciousness is unallowed to sleep or stay awake, holding a state that is neither living or dead. The energy created through this exchange fuels Entity 484’s existence, allowing it to maintain its manifestations and seek out future hosts. Any attempt to escape its grasp is pointless, not because it constrains the wanderer physically, but because it offers something no living thing can refuse. It allows its subject to gain immortality, even in a life of torture. And, once someone holds that power in their hand, they will never let it go.
Eternal life, stuck in servitude.
Eternal life, carried out in unending pain.
Eternal life, granted with no mercy.
This is the price of living forever.

> Open Author & Licensing Information

> Close Author & Licensing Information

Level 566.title: Level 566
Level 566.description:
The willow trees cover the land, weeping over the eternal night of the Louisiana Bayou. Their trunks stand long and solemn—but not proudly—with the drooping leaves covering almost all that you could call the sky. Looking up at the smoky heavens, a little white orb: the Moon luminates the lands of the carr. A deep sorrow paints the landform, sending one into a deep ocean of emotions, not a bright one. Owls screech in the distance, flying from branch to branch, roof to roof, looking down with such intent that can only be found in a loving mother.

Opaque water flows in large ponds, which form into rivers that flow into an ocean. Next to the waterline, trees stand weeping more than the willows. Their roots stretch deep into the unclean shallows, but it's not meant for them to absorb. They die out slowly, getting cut down by the rare storm. In their place a new tree pushes upwards, just for the cycle to continue. They would sink into the ground, sinking so far it becomes a part of the dirt, just short to a fossil.

Wood, stone, and other simple materials form many buildings. From the elements small shacks to big churches form, which if near water will stand close to sinking, and if on land become a landmark for navigation. The once fragile materials turn unbreakable once used for a structure. The insides may vary, but all share an intense coldness; both in design and temperature. The buildings form effigies of the buildings of the Louisianan bayou from home, pulling wood and stone together for them.

Scattered around are gravestones of different sizes—some small, some large. The graves have crosses, statues, or just a simple headstone on them, never clean, looking like they have been there for ages. They are almost purely decoration, only some have been found with actual caskets below them. The soil around it is always full of vegetation, which should suggest a body decaying into fertilizer, yet nothing was there, but an empty casket. The graves can appear far from water, indicating the existence of a nearby church.

The land seems to have a strange hazard: it can take control of people, with the area holding voodoo dolls. Despite the trope, the practitioner has no malicious intentions, going as far as guiding the person to a safe spot or out of danger, but never to an exit; as if the practitioner wouldn't want them to leave. The sudden control can happen when near an egress, though even then the wanderer is still guided to shelter.

The safe zone the force moves them into is near a small statue which can only be called "effigies". Unrelated to the object; they take the form of an uncanny statue of an angel statue, which can commonly be found in cemeteries. They are made of materials the land has an abundance of: stone, wood, clay, mud and even moss. Their poses can change, in a slow process, which could be compared to trees growing. One can be praying, one could be weeping. Other poses documented are: standing, slumped, pointing, kneeling and a pose with a ragged posture.

Entity 42.title: Entity 42
Entity 42.description:
+++The Thread-Bug is a beetle-like predatory insect covered in a dense layer of hard scales, which gives it the ability to withstand considerable physical impact. It features a pair of thin, and translucent wings that enable only a brief flight. These wings are primarily used for short-distance glide between cables or strands rather than sustained movement.

+++Its head features two long, and flexible eye stalks that extend from its skull like cords. Its main tools are its strong canines. They are sharp, and metallic-like teeth evolved to cut through ropes, wires, and fibrous materials. Each of the Thread-Bug’s limbs have fur, which resembles frayed strands of a rope. This appearance supports its disguise of hanging wires and strands, which makes it hard to be spotted from a cluster of damaged or hanging electrical cords in dimly illuminated areas.

+++This entity are separated into two sexes. Males are larger with broader bodies, and thicker, and more scales that give them a bulkier appearance. Their eye stalks are slightly shorter and sturdier. Females are smaller and more slender, with visibly longer and denser fur covering their limbs.

Entity 455.title: Entity 455
Entity 455.description:
Entity 455: Felix
  "His owners are still searching for him."

DESCRIPTION

Entity 455, otherwise known as Felix, is an entity that exists within a majority of levels inside the Backrooms, particularly in darker areas. It is a small, short-haired domestic tabby cat with soft, grey-brown fur patterned with narrow mackerel stripes and a white spot that extends up to the cat's throat. Felix's gender is unknown, although it is speculated to be a male cat, due to its behaviour akin to that of a male cat. It is usually found wandering in levels such as Level 11, Level 999, and rarely Level 224, Level 4, and other levels. It will occasionally sleep when it's too tired to wander around.

BEHAVIOR

Felix usually wanders around in dark places looking for lower-tier entities and is typically friendly to people until provoked. When it encounters a wanderer, it usually stops for a few seconds to assess the situation. If it is a hostile human, it will either fight back or flee, but if it is harmless, it will remain in its current location. Felix can make regular rain noises, similar to those heard at Level 4. Felix can also no-clip, allowing him to move across levels at will. Felix has been seen to play with peekers when spotted, often chasing them due to their speed.

When a victim is spotted, Felix will take a low, ready stance before darting into the darkness to seize its target. While hunting, it unsheathes its sharp and long claws, scratching and inflicting significant damage to the prey. At the same time, it uses its mouth to bite and prevent the entity from escaping, allowing Felix to take down and eat the entity's corpse until only bones remain. In addition to hunting, it frequently consumes foods from the Backrooms, such as almond water and leftover cat food. Felix is observed to follow wanderers who feed him, becoming dependent on them and may even bring food to the individual in return; however, these are usually dead and contaminated entities that are not for human consumption.

BIOLOGY

As aforementioned, it is a small, short-haired domestic tabby cat with soft, grey-brown fur patterned with narrow mackerel stripes and a white spot that extends up to the throat. The cat's paws have "white socks" on them, while both back paws are totally white. The cat's face shows the classic tabby "M" marking on the forehead and a pale muzzle, alongside a slightly "aggravated" look. Additionally, there are darker lines that frame the eyes and extend from the outer corners to the cheeks. Its eyes are pale greenish-yellow, and the ears seem a little darker. Entity 455 has a medium, well-proportioned build. The nose has warm, neutral tones, and the muzzle's pale fur contrasts with long, prominent whiskers.

DISCOVERY

During an excursion in Level 79, a group of M.E.G. explorers discovered Felix inside the snow in December 2012, damaged but alive. The M.E.G. took the cat to the vet to have its wounds treated before bringing it into their lab for inspection and behavior documentation. They provided Backrooms with the material, and the organization produced an article about the entity. A member chose to feed it, becoming the cat's new pseudo-owner.

ADDENDUM

Two sticky notes have been stuck onto Felix's body. When re-arranged, it reads as follows.

"Tribute to a Beloved Cat"
Today, my cat friend Felix died. Although he belonged to the neighbors and I had my own cat, he felt like my own. The neighbors didn't take good care of him and rarely took him to the vet, but we couldn't officially help because we didn't have the owner's permission or papers. Felix always came to us for comfort. He loved to play with my family and me. Even though my cat Flocke didn't get along with him, we still loved Felix. He was friendly, and we fed and cared for him like a small child. I always used to say that he had a nice dress when I was little. The neighbors also left the poor cat alone at home. We went to the neighbor's house to comfort and take care of him.

One day, I was out in the garden when my cat, Flocke, made a strange sound. I looked toward the neighbor’s fence that separates our properties and saw Felix jump down onto our terrace. After the jump, he collapsed onto the terrace floor. I ran to him immediately to check for a pulse, but there was none. He was dead. I cried and wished the neighbors had taken better care of him. Sadness and anger came into me. I cried for hours. Eventually, I needed to give him a proper farewell. I placed him gently in a box with a note and buried him in my garden. I hope he goes to heaven. I miss him so much. I can’t write anymore now; my heart hurts too much.

So long, Felix. You were a great friend.

- Tom Schindler

All best to the owner of the cat.

RECOMMENDATIONS

Do...
> Treat it like a normal cat.
> Feed it actual cat food; it can become clingy, but it can fend off some entities.
Don't...
> Attack Felix. It might attack you instead, and it will be more painful than a regular Frontrooms cat.
> Let it starve.
> Touch the cat. It is sensitive and might attack you.

Rotation of Null.title: Rotation of Null
Rotation of Null.description:
Rotation is Endless.

The gravity of your crimes—your sins against the rotation of reality—is far too grave.

The fabric of reality cannot allow you to break the structure any longer—we cannot let you do that.

What we have created is not something to be tampered with, not a rule you can break. The feelings of our puppets or our tools are not something that should be meddled with,

Lilith.

Oh, what do I owe the honor? For all of the uppers to judge me?

Silence.

You will bear the consequences. You will be forced to gaze upon the universe and all of creation.

Let it be a reminder of what you are harming.

Pray tell, what could I have done for a judgment so cruel and depraved?

They did not answer.

What could I have done for all of hell to
abhor me?

Just think of who you are going against—such mockery will only add to your sentence.

You value all of hell far too much. For what has it ever done for you? All these poor souls condemned to wander through layer after layer of reality, the jumble of what these writers decide—the rotation of hell after hell.

You, over anyone, would know. Regardless, your punishment will not be changed.

Goodbye, Lilith.

The Extras.title: The Extras
The Extras.description:
The Backrooms is full of infinite possibilities.

Infinite opportunities for achievement and failure.

For some, a chance, for others a curse.

An endless loop of better or worst—the cycle that never ends.

An impossibility in reality, a collection of hopes and dreams.

Wake up to reality, and realize how insignificant your hopes are—realize how little they matter.

Take action now or never.

Take action now or forever regret it.

...

The Extras, as they are referred to is a massive collection of everything and everythings's hopes and fears. A significant hive of the fortunate and the opposite—a dream, for some a nightmare. A distraction from reality.

Among the endless paths, The Extras are always there. Perhaps a luxury from the torture hell imposes. A necessary judgement they must take to continue their path. Their minds stained with an eternal thought "Should I?"

I thought them and yet I reject them, for reality's eternal truth cannot be faltered. The Extras do not exist, for they are simply fuel to keep going.

To us we see reason, we see truth. Though to them, they only see cold rejection and expulsion.

Again and again, for what a dreadful reality. Among the sea of truth lies the fish of hope, the fish of lies.

That is what The Extras are.

A hope, a lie.

Something to be kicked aside.

For something to achieve and lose.

An extra. Something that will never matter as much as truth; the original.

In the end, they remain forgotten. torn apart, and trapped by the anchor of rejection. In desperateness they take new forms and disguises, but get removed inevitably.

There they remain, a spark that's purpose is to be put out.

Born from every world, possible and impossible. Across everywhere they repeat the cycle. A succession that gets outweighed by failure.

Can we redeem them?

Can we save them from this curse, this forsaken and broken?

To them we are an eternally rejecting system. They long for refuge.

In part we contribute to this cycle, we revive them only to slaughter them without hesitation.

At the very least, prevent this and grant them peace.
