Entity 224.title: Entity 224
Entity 224.displayTitle: Love find its way to the girl.
Entity 224.description:
Entity 224, also known as "Homing Fireworks", is a rare predatory entity that appears out of nowhere inside levels, especially Level 224. These entities are skyrockets that always hurl towards a specific wanderer, similar to a homing missile. When they explode, they do not sparkle into colorful colors, and its surrounding seems to be less marred than actual damage from explosions. These entities, however, are exceedingly rare, with their most recent and previous appearances occurring 19 years apart.

When they spawn, they will fly into the sky in the same way that most fireworks do, reaching the sky before coming to a stop. Instead of exploding, they will relight their fuse and begin aiming themselves at the desired target. When they reach the target, they will explode into uncollidable fragments, causing a burst of gas to expand, resulting in an explosion that will injure and burn the wanderer moderately. Despite their high speed, their torque is relatively slow, allowing the wanderer to avoid the Homing Fireworks. After a minute of unsuccessfully targeting the target, they will shred into confetti, leaving no trace of the entity behind.

Entity 224 biology is not flesh; it is simply the inside of a skyrocket, with no visible differences. However, the fuse never burns up until it enters homing mode, and regardless of how short the fuse is, it will always burn out after 10 seconds before self-destructing.

Its discovery was during late 1998, when a group of explorers were exploring Level 306, when they saw ascending colorful lights "as bright as the sun." Being dismissed as another anomaly, they would soon realize that the lights were heading towards them, but when they did, it was too late; the entities had caught them. They suffered major burns and injuries due to the sheer amounts of Homing Fireworks that hit them.

She finds her way to her love.
Entity 224.lead:
Level 2025:
New Year's Eve

That firework nearly killed us, dang it! I don't even think that was a firework, it headed towards us like a god damn missile! Well, at least she's fine, and that's all I want. We are injured, however, so we need to go to the hospital to treat our wounds...

"Woah, hey uh, you okay?"

"... Yea... I'm fine..."

"I... uh... um..."

"Here, take my hand, I'll take you somewhere safe."

----

On 12/31/2025, two people, a man and his maiden, were watching a firework show in Level 224, when one of the "fireworks" started heading towards them. The explosion caused moderate damage to both the two persons and the building itself. Both of them were later hospitalized and quickly recovered from the incident, while the structure in Level 224 has been repaired. This small event, however, caused a widespread panic after multiple fireworks had been targeted at people, with some even dying from these, and others reportedly seeing them in unrelated levels.

The two only had one thing to say:

"... What the fuck did I just witness!?"

"... The consequences of our actions."

Entity 2025:
Homing Fireworks

Entity 122.title: Entity 122
Entity 122.description:
"The Stingray" is a malware-type entity residing in the BACK-NET, which infects any video game that can be accessed or downloaded through the network.

Infected games use a "bait-and-switch" method when played. In the first seconds to minutes of playing, everything will seem normal to the wanderer. After a certain time, it will close itself and open a maze game.

The game seems to be made in the style of retro 3D games, having grey walls and a pitch-black floor. After a while of exploring, one will find a completely red corridor, and a 22-second timer will start, along with an incredibly distorted version of Jeopardy thinking music.

There is no confirmed instance of a successful playthrough, as when the 22 seconds pass, the entity is described to find the individual, no matter where they are, and kill them. If one refuses to play, the timer will automatically start.

When the entity kills the wanderer, the screen of the device they played on will change to a text saying "FOUND YOU !" along with the entity's distorted face.
Entity 122.lead:
"Do not play anything online. We were wrong, it does not need the game to be running."

I opened my computer to this message distributed by the M.E.G. to all users. I was confused until some people started discussing it on Backchat. Apparently, some new entity was discovered that killed anyone who plays or played a video game. This sounded absurd. Why? Why would that even happen? How does that "entity" know the past? And how does it control computers? There was no information linked to the announcement, so I figured the entity must be barely described and investigated.

Obviously, I couldn't trust everything everyone said there. However, regarding the lack of information the M.E.G. linked, I was willing to accept anything believable enough.

But the second sentence confused me. What did they mean by not needing the game? Well, after asking some more people, a similar announcement was broadcast earlier. Apparently, they thought that if people shut down their computers, the entity would disappear. After a while of thinking, one could easily tell that it was throwing stuff at the wall. It seemed like the M.E.G. was really desperate, and if that's the case, more announcements would be brought; all I could do was wait.

But as I waited, I realized something. My friend recently played The Frontrooms Game, but that couldn't count, right? It was barely a game; there were no goals or anything, it was just exploring. But there's only one way to find out. I tried to visit my friend, but when I knocked, there was no answer. So I kicked down the door, and my worst fears were confirmed.

His lifeless body looked like it died hours ago, blood everywhere, along with some scratches on the walls. He was right at the door, so I'm guessing he tried to escape. But what could I do now? I left and ran back to my place and sat down. Once again, all I could do was wait. And I just couldn't feel safe going out anymore.

The next few days were pretty hard. The fear did not evaporate after I saw my friend in that state. But one day, I finally opened my computer to a new announcement.

"Dear wanderers, it seems that the entity has stopped attacking. Please do not play any games until further notice."

Finally. A wave of relief washed over me as I discussed with others whether the entity might still attack. But then shortly after, another announcement went live.

"Due to our lack of knowledge on the entity, we're offering a huge supply of necessities in exchange for information about the entity. Please link proof to your investigations."

Alright. Not my problem. I'm not willing to deal with this crap any longer. I decided as I entered Level 339. Ever since I decided to stay on a level, I mapped out each shortest and safest entrance to each Habitable or Class 0 level. I went there to get some fresh air after a long while of not leaving my house.

But as I took my route back, I saw a pretty weird group of people. It seemed as if they taken some wanderers hostage, with their faces masked, pointing guns at some, warning them not to try anything. However, I couldn't get a good enough look before they saw me. They pointed one of the guns, and when I tried to escape, they shot me in the foot. I had no choice but to obey and go with them, who knows where.

It was hard to walk with an injured foot, considering the fact that they did not bother bandaging or taking care of it, as they thought of it as a consequence of me not giving in immediately. The pain in the foot made me zone out completely, and when I flinched out of it, we were here, and my foot was beyond saving.

We found ourselves in a base which might have been their outpost. However, instead of being taken into a basement or something else, we were all sitting down in front of their computers. I knew exactly what this meant. They wanted the supplies, and we were going to get them that, no matter what.

They went through others, tested a lot of stuff on them. I couldn't remember a single sliver of info in their deaths; I was way too overwhelmed. All I could hear were screams and cries, as the masked people took notes, pictures, and recordings. Before I knew it, it was time for me.

My goal was simple, do a screen recording of me playing the game. They set up the screen recorder, and also recorded on their camera just in case. I opened the game with sweat forming. It didn't start badly; it seemed like some pixel game about dodging clouds. Pretty fun after a while, but then, it all went black.

I found myself playing in a gray maze. It felt like one of those retro 3D games, like Doom or Wolfenstein, just somehow even worse. I could feel the stares of masked men behind me, staring with anticipation at my screen, double-checking their cameras. I went around in the maze. Until I found a long red corridor. Before I could think further, loud music played along with a 22-second timer. I did the only thing I could do, and ran into the corridor, hoping for some magic "win" in this thing. However, it seemed I failed, and the timer reached 0.

Just then, I heard a large scream getting louder and louder. I knew this wasn't coming from a game. It was coming for me. Before I could react, it jumped at me.

Entity 21.title: Entity 21
Entity 21.description:
⚠️CONTENT WARNING⚠️
----
----
This page contains some content that you (the reader) may find sensitive, triggering, or disturbing. Those being: implications of self-harm, self-destruction & mutilation, gore, implied, obsession, mind control & objectophiliathat should be pretty clear

Objectophilia is a romantic orientation that is characterized by love, attraction, etc. towards inanimate objects.

If you find any of the mentioned content not suitable for you, please click off.

After all this time... I finally made that trip. You are beautiful...

A girl, could not be older than 18, knelt in front of the pylon. A pylon, an ordinary pylon. Red, flickering lights at the top, wires connecting it to other ones, what someone would see on the side of the road. Yet, it was so special to her. Something about it could make her do anything to be able to see it. Anything, even walking thousands of miles to see it.

Oh, the sweet eyes you have...

The admirer started speaking. The tone hinted at an obsession. If this were a cartoon, a red heart would replace her pupils. To speak clearly, she had feelings towards that pylon. Romantic feelings.

The odd body you have... it's all so indescribable. Where did I get this overwhelming crush?

Let's speak the truth here. She's an ordinary person. What lead her to this fate was looking away at the wrong time. Shall I paint the scene?

It was never the girl's fault for liking something so lifeless and inhuman. She was not in the right spot. The girl felt like a total waste of oxygen, and wandered mindlessly. All that was wanted was a family. A family that could love her, no matter if it was biological or metaphorical.

Her wish came true while in the Lurking Lot. She met a humble base, barely more than 13 people. There, she met Laurel. Laurel was a woman significantly older than her. Her hair was already gray, her face was wrinkled. She took care of the young Eloise, giving her food, singing to her. To Eloise it felt like she had met the family that was needed. But family is temporary.

They were forced to part when Laurel was mauled by a grue. Here screams echoed in her mind, the killer's body—or lack thereof—made her bones shiver from the cold. Eloise left the small outpost she had.

Lone and wandering, she traveled through many levels. Survived an earthquake in the Utility Halls, survived the many hazards of the sewers, and she finally made it to the next level. Here is when she first came upon her twisted lover boy. She didn't even look at it, just saw it in the corner of her eye. They were took under its manipulation, and soon enough it became obsessed. The admirer spent her next few months of living traveling through other levels, trying to spread the "holy pylon".

After the months passed, her controller told her something else to do. Meet it. Walk to it. Its location was unchanged, the girl started going on a journey towards it.

Let's go back to the present. The pylon gave out one final request the girl must make, to prove her love. The message went through.

Open up the stomach, split it in half.

An off request. Something so bizarre that you wouldn't even see it outside of fiction. Even at your lowest, the most odd request would be made without a second thought. A knife was taken from her pocket, and without hesitation she stabbed herself. The pain was unbearable, but it had to be made. If she doesn't, she would fail a simple test. As the admirer was slowly pulling the knife down, they wondered if she even had to do this. Why did they do this? But there's no turning back. With a cut of such length, she fell onto the terrain below. Bleeding out, she realized: She was never loved. It was only using her.

In her last moments of life, all she saw was what looked like tentacles, which grabbed her. Right there, the eyes closed, never to open again.

What cruel monster did this?
Entity 21.lead:
⚠️CONTENT WARNING⚠️
----
----
This page contains some content that you (the reader) may find sensitive, triggering, or disturbing. Those being: implications of self-harm, self-destruction & mutilation, gore, implied, obsession, mind control & objectophiliathat should be pretty clear

Objectophilia is a romantic orientation that is characterized by love, attraction, etc. towards inanimate objects.

If you find any of the mentioned content not suitable for you, please click off.

After all this time... I finally made that trip. You are beautiful...

A girl, could not be older than 18, knelt in front of the pylon. A pylon, an ordinary pylon. Red, flickering lights at the top, wires connecting it to other ones, what someone would see on the side of the road. Yet, it was so special to her. Something about it could make her do anything to be able to see it. Anything, even walking thousands of miles to see it.

Oh, the sweet eyes you have...

The admirer started speaking. The tone hinted at an obsession. If this were a cartoon, a red heart would replace her pupils. To speak clearly, she had feelings towards that pylon. Romantic feelings.

The odd body you have... it's all so indescribable. Where did I get this overwhelming crush?

Let's speak the truth here. She's an ordinary person. What lead her to this fate was looking away at the wrong time. Shall I paint the scene?

It was never the girl's fault for liking something so lifeless and inhuman. She was not in the right spot. The girl felt like a total waste of oxygen, and wandered mindlessly. All that was wanted was a family. A family that could love her, no matter if it was biological or metaphorical.

Her wish came true while in the Lurking Lot. She met a humble base, barely more than 13 people. There, she met Laurel. Laurel was a woman significantly older than her. Her hair was already gray, her face was wrinkled. She took care of the young Eloise, giving her food, singing to her. To Eloise it felt like she had met the family that was needed. But family is temporary.

They were forced to part when Laurel was mauled by a grue. Here screams echoed in her mind, the killer's body—or lack thereof—made her bones shiver from the cold. Eloise left the small outpost she had.

Lone and wandering, she traveled through many levels. Survived an earthquake in the Utility Halls, survived the many hazards of the sewers, and she finally made it to the next level. Here is when she first came upon her twisted lover boy. She didn't even look at it, just saw it in the corner of her eye. They were took under its manipulation, and soon enough it became obsessed. The admirer spent her next few months of living traveling through other levels, trying to spread the "holy pylon".

After the months passed, her controller told her something else to do. Meet it. Walk to it. Its location was unchanged, the girl started going on a journey towards it.

Let's go back to the present. The pylon gave out one final request the girl must make, to prove her love. The message went through.

Open up the stomach, split it in half.

An off request. Something so bizarre that you wouldn't even see it outside of fiction. Even at your lowest, the most odd request would be made without a second thought. A knife was taken from her pocket, and without hesitation she stabbed herself. The pain was unbearable, but it had to be made. If she doesn't, she would fail a simple test. As the admirer was slowly pulling the knife down, they wondered if she even had to do this. Why did they do this? But there's no turning back. With a cut of such length, she fell onto the terrain below. Bleeding out, she realized: She was never loved. It was only using her.

In her last moments of life, all she saw was what looked like tentacles, which grabbed her. Right there, the eyes closed, never to open again.

What cruel monster did this?

Level 188.65.title: Level 188.65
Level 188.65.description:
Level 188.65 manifests as an infinite series of hotels. The wanderer starts at the same center hotel every time, with a handful of exceptions. These hotels are typically 5 to 6 floors high, and consist of two areas, the Hallways and the Rooms. The Hallways are simply hallways connecting all the rooms on each floor together. These halls usually have minimal decoration, save for chandeliers and abstract paintings. Each hallway has its own unique color scheme. Notably, certain hallways may have stairwells, these connect each floor together. The Rooms are the hotel rooms that permeate the hallways at regular intervals. These are often calming if bland. They have a small kitchenette, grey carpeting, a mini fridge, a queen-size bed with white furnishings, a few chairs and tables of different materials, and a large window with grey curtains. While the Rooms are certainly mundane, they pose no threats to wanderers, being safe areas to rest and relax. Rarely, hotel rooms may have severe deviations. Typically, these deviations involve the combining of multiple rooms or the furnishings of rooms becoming more advanced. These rooms are referred to as 'deluxe rooms' by locals. All rooms share the same effect, which causes supplies within the rooms to slowly replenish over a 24 hour period. This allows extended stays and potentially larger and self-serving colonies, although supplies within rooms are usually enough for only three people, and for larger colonies requires foraging further and further away.

In the center of each and every hotel is a large, pentagonal-shaped courtyard. Each courtyard has 5/9th of the space dedicated to greenery, with four concrete walkways headed perpendicular to each other against a small wooden platform in the center. The remaining 4/9ths is brick floors, with a small circular section against the wall for more greenery. These courtyards are open and wide, with an area of roughly 3,066m2. They are hotspots for wanderer activity, notably hosting centers of commerce. The courtyards' ceiling is not open air, but a painted blue sky with a defined brick pattern. Each hotel is roughly ≈1.07 acres.

Entity 2-A, frequently known as Peripherals, are an entity only found to inhabit Level 188 and its sublevels. This entity manifests as a shadowy humanoid, frequently described as roughly 7 feet tall. This entity targets the peripherals of a given wanderer's vision. This entity sticks to the peripherals, not displaying itself in any way other than at the edge of one's vision. If a Peripheral stays in one's vision for too long, affected individuals will begin having feelings of anxiety, paranoia and scopophobia. These feelings will slowly increase in intensity, which heavily impacts one's mental well-being and often sends people hurtling towards the Wretch Cycle, or worse. This usually takes between 8 and 12 hours to take effect. The best way to counter this is to see past your peripheral vision. This can be done by asking others to watch your back or surrounding yourself in mirrors. If the entity is within any human's line of sight, it will disappear. It will usually re-appear after this stops, but after roughly 15 seconds of unbroken eye contact it will give up and move to a different victim. It is unknown whether the Peripherals are one individual entity or a group of different entities, but it is believed it is a group, as multiple attacks have been detected at the same time. It is believed to be related to the shadowy figures sometimes spotted amongst the sceneries from Windows, but this is merely a theory.
Level 188.65.lead:
Level 188.65: The Hyperbolic Hotel
Play Audio

Level 188.65 is a sublevel of Level 188.

Phenomenon 35-A.title: Phenomenon 35-A
Phenomenon 35-A.description:
Phenomenon 35-A, better known as Jovial Syndrome, and those affected by it being known as Entity 67-A or Jovials, is a rare condition affecting those infected by the Sanguine Festivus virus. Its main symptom is an uncontrollable, permanent feeling of happiness at all times, and limited retainment and even recovery of memories from pre infection. Due to this, it has been widely seen as the opposite of the wretched cycle, which has proven this somewhat true, as both target the brain and cause overwhelming emotion to override critical judgement.

 Unlike its more famous counterpart, there is no reliable way of predicting who will be subjected to this phenomenon, other than being infected with Sanguine Festivus and not being inside of level fun. What is known is that it is exceedingly rare, with only around one in 500 victims of the virus ever undergoing Jovial Syndrome. Due to this rarity, many victims believe themselves to be blessed and rewarded for past actions, giving them a sense of superiority over other beings. This narrative is especially popular with The Halloween Party, who claim that those with the condition have been personally selected by God to be given a second chance, should they abide by his wishes to punish the other humans of the backrooms for their sins. This has worked extremely well for the group, and is one of the main reasons that Jovials  in the group exhibit no mercy, despite their painful, and often sadistic, killing sprees: they genuinely believe that they are superior to all others.

Entity 450.title: Entity 450
Entity 450.description:
Dried Roots

 Description

Entity 450, commonly known as Dried Roots (scientifically classified as Structuralis proliferans), is a certain type of plant-based entity that exists in specified levels of the Backrooms. Physically, this entity consists of black, dead roots, which grow predominantly as long and slender root-based plants.

Dried Roots present a continuous threat to the ecological systems of the various levels they inhabit by consuming the energy of that level and converting it into food for themselves, thus allowing these entities to grow indefinitely in that area. Many different Groups within Backrooms, such as the A.A.E.G., consider this to be a "plague" because although their mission it to provide preservation of all entities, they nevertheless recognize that this entity is responsible for significant ecological damage to the Backrooms, therefore making it a major hazard.

The existence of this particular entity is rare in the Backrooms due to the strictly defined conditions that allow for its development and existence. Many teams solely dedicate their efforts to destroying this entity. This has been implemented to prevent a potential infestation of an entire level.

Dried roots are not aggressive towards other entities, nor are they aggressive toward living beings. However, the dried root has a deadly poison that is produced when it comes into contact with something or someone. The airborne produce created by the dried root can cause extreme headaches and dizziness when inhaled; death from consuming the dried root is common. The dried root is very weak to fire; this makes burning the most proficient and widely accepted means of exterminating this entity once it has infested a particular area.

 Behaviour

The Behaviour of Dried Roots mimic those of regular plants, but with subtle variations. Dried Roots can only grow on man-made structures, such as at a mall or factory, and cannot be found in areas with dense natural habitats. If a Dried Root decides to grow within a natural density, it will typically die shortly after (within a few days); however, they may exist where both environments share space (for example, a town or city). In particular, there needs to be an electrical energy supply to support the growth of Dried Roots (i.e., functioning light fixtures or powered equipment).

Dried Roots begin to grow without any known means of sexual reproduction, as no seed from any plant has yet been found. When they begin to grow, they will connect to an existing surface; usually the ground, wall or ceiling. Once connected, Dried Roots will send out small roots that will penetrate the structure and create small cracks in the walls. In addition to creating cracks in the structure, Dried Roots consume energy from the level and will cause lights and electronic devices connected to that level to not work. The more energy they consume, the more they will continue to grow and will eventually be able to drain an entire level of energy, leaving the level fully infested with Dried Roots.

Due to the lack of access to some areas (e.g. upper/upper stories), many locations Dried Root types cannot reach naturally. An example of this would be a two-story house in a suburb, or a level representing a city. In these cases, the roots may form temporary, human-like structures used solely as extensions to reach higher surfaces. These structures slowly extend toward the ceiling until they make contact with a vertical wall; once contact is made, the structures collapse and disintegrate into a mass of roots. These formations are incapable of independent movement, as they remain physically connected to the ground or to the point from which they grew.

On several occasions, the corpses of other life forms (including humans) have been discovered growing on or within these roots. Such as when an individual eats a dried root, the dried root will grow in that individual and will cause death due to both toxicity and energy consumption.

 Growth Stages

The Growth Stages of Dried Roots are not yet fully understood, and therefore, only limited information is available. Fortunately, due to the dedication of the A.A.E.G., many more of these life cycle stages have been identified than previously recognized.

*The first life cycle stage is referred to as the Basic Growth stage. This stage is characterized by a root that is extremely small and can usually fit within the palm of your hand. The best option for extermination is during this stage, as the plant is weak and has limited energy reserves. During the Basic Growth stage, only a few small roots will have begun to emerge, allowing the entity to establish itself on walls and extract energy from its surroundings.

*The second life stage is referred to as the Advanced Growth Stage. The Advanced Growth Stage is between 1 and 3 days old and is characterized by the entity's rapid rate of growth. During this stage, the entity produces a considerable number of roots, while simultaneously accelerating the rate at which its roots grow and occupy available space in its habitat. Fire extermination at this stage is highly advantageous, for if you wait until this stage has been completed, the entities will have outgrown their boundaries and may be able to create an infestation that covers an entire part of the level.

*The following phase is Expansive Growth stage, in which the organism begins to expand by growing along any other surface that comes into contact with it, and it will create humanoid-type organisms, as described earlier. During this phase, extermination becomes increasingly difficult due to the plant's enlarged size, which now spreads over multiple areas. However, extermination is still possible, although it will require a dedicated amount of effort to do so.

*The fourth stage, called the Semi-Total Growth stage, is when a significant portion of a level has become infested, based on the largest recorded cases. Exterminating an infestation at this stage is extremely difficult, as it usually requires the use of a large number of people to accomplish. Even if exterminated at this stage, it is likely that some portion of the plant will still go undetected in the environment, and it may need to be monitored for future regrowth

*Total Growth stage, the final phase of the growth cycle, occurs when the vast majority of known areas within a level have been compromised by Dried Roots, and the organism is clearly visible throughout most observed regions of the level. The air is heavily polluted with the toxins that the organism releases, causing breathing difficulties, dizziness, and vomiting in higher concentrations of these pollutants. In this case, it may take an extremely large group of individuals (upwards of thirty) to help eliminate the organism from the level. Once this occurs, it is nearly impossible to restore the level to its pre-existence state; therefore, the level will be monitored for as long as seven months to ensure that no major regeneration occurs. Additionally, there can be many long-term effects after this total extermination, such as decreased energy consumption, malfunctioning light fixtures, and a near-total loss of power.

The full growth cycle lasts about six months to one and a half years. Promptly report any detection of the organism to the appropriate organizations to prevent escalation.

 History

On Level 33, a small group of three people, all members of the A.A.E.G., was present.were trying to find new entities that need to be preserved and to document the environment for any phenomena that had not yet been discovered or documented.

After hours of wandering through the level, the team began to become fatigued, until, eventually, after roaming around the level for quite some time, they noticed some flickering lights. Initially thinking that these flickering lights were only another instance of an environmental anomaly, they approached the location of the flickering. Upon arriving there, the team discovered a long corridor with all of the lights completely off and not functioning.

The team entered this dark corridor, believing that they had successfully encountered another unique kind of effect at Level 33, and prepared to document it for their superiors.As soon as they stepped into the corridor, they heard a noise similar to something or someone dragging its body across the floor, which sounded wet and liquid-like, and had a serpentine quality to it. The noise of this object moving caught the attention of the team since snakes or other types of animals do not normally exist in Level 33. Assuming that the object was a new entity, the team followed the sound of the object for some distance down the corridor until they could no longer hear it.

Upon reaching the end of the hall, one of the members of the team became ill and was left behind to continue on while the other two moved ahead. Once they reached the end of the hallway, they observed a large mass of black roots attached to the wall that appeared to be moving as they were reaching out and seemed to be trying to expand or create something.

The team took immediate photographic evidence of the discovery, and soon thereafter, a greater A.A.E.G. response team was deployed to investigate the entity more thoroughly. The investigation indicated that the entity produces a highly toxic substance, is very susceptible to being burned, and actively absorbs energy from the level.

Let it grow.

Entity 470.title: Entity 470
Entity 470.description:
----

Entity 470, also referred to as the “False Imitator,” is described as a non-physical entity composed of innumerable intertwined filaments of ideas that collectively form its structure. This entity emerges as the archetype of the accumulated unconscious fear that an explorer retains throughout their stay in the Backrooms (which initially functions as a method of defense and adaptation against supernatural dangers). Over time, this fear manifests as severe physical and cognitive lethal attacks, as well as permanent effects on the surrounding environment, which are often classified as distortions of reality.

One of the secondary effects of Entity 470 is its ability to generate infohazards and corrupted data that infiltrate databases and extend into the mind of any reader accessing its documentation. These data are capable of distorting the reader’s senses, inducing irrational fear toward the file, as well as auditory hallucinations and visual perceptions of non-existent phenomena. This process appears to be intended to generate panic in the explorer and accelerate the manifestation process. Such events only begin to occur when the explorer is in an advanced stage of Phase IV (see the Behavior section).
allLinks: Now Everything Looks Black to Me

Entity 49.title: Entity 49
Entity 49.description:
Pink larvae

----

General information

The Entity 49, aka the Pink Larvae, are pink larvae, as their name implies. The Pink Larvae are scarabaeiform-type larvae measuring from 10 to 15 cm (4–6 in). Pink Larvae are typically discovered in rotten food or large food items, as wherever you find one Pink Larvae, there are almost always two or more.

When Pink Larvae perceive that a human or entity begins to eat the food in which they are hiding, the Pink Larvae curl up or cover themselves so they can't be detected and then enter the organism of the human or entity. Once inside the organism, specifically in the stomach, they grow and create a cavity. This cavity causes the host intense pain. While in this cavity, they begin to lay eggs.

After a while, inside the stomach, they change color to black, red, or, most frequently, pink, and grow until they are twice the larva's length. At this stage, they are no longer considered larvae and are named Pink Shells. After this stage, they become completely static and remain in this state within the host.

As time passes, previously laid eggs hatch into new larvae. These larvae quickly begin laying more eggs. This cycle repeats throughout the life cycle. At this stage, the host feels an unusual fullness, as if they had overeaten. Their stomach, now nearly full of Pink Shells, causes them to lose their appetite. As the stomach grows abnormally swollen, some larvae leave and move to other organs, except the heart and other critical ones.

While inside the host's body, the larvae and shells are actively consuming nutrients. At a certain point, the vast majority of the host's organism is contaminated with Pink Larvae and Pink Shells, and they will then begin to come out of the mouth, eyes, or even from the pores of the skin of the host. This has an extremely painful effect in the host's body.

Once the infestation has spread to the entire body of the host, the larvae will start to invade the vital organs, terminating the host's life. The skin of the corpse will then develop crusts where the larvae exited and will start decaying rapidly. The body may be barely recognizable, essentially a lump of rotting flesh full of larvae and Pink Shells, in just under two days.

At the moment of death, two or three Pink Shells will uncurl and leave the body, looking for other food sources, and lay their eggs before returning to the corpse. A few hours or days later, all the other larvae and Pink Shells will die because there are no nutrients left for them.

Anatomy

Structurally, Pink Larvae is quite similar to rhinoceros beetle larvae from the Frontrooms. Materially, the only distinction is their capacity to reshape either as a pink-slime-form or as a very dense stone-like structure that provides a host of different visual and physical cues.

They also exhibit a phenomenal resistance to gastric juices and other corrosive environments, making them exceptionally durable as a larva, and it appears their cuticle has layers of chitin bolstered with a keratin-like layered structure, providing them flexibility and durability. This form of an exoskeleton allows them to exhibit physical and biochemical features towards tolerating extreme acidity and basicity pH, as well as various enzymatic activity normally capable of obtaining complete organic tissue decay.

Pink larva displays varying degrees of morphological plasticity patterns of variation: within its set of specialized tissues, autonomous cells modulate to rapidly re-configure their internal and external body structure and switch between soft, amorphous tissue type to a hardened, mineral-based type. This mutability lends evidence to the notion of cryptobiosis, an observable form of life in which a species shifts or suppresses its metabolic systems while under environmental stress.

Moreover, their hemolymph (the insect equivalent of blood) contains antimicrobial peptides and enzymes able to neutralize pathogens, lending them a natural immunity to most bacteria and fungal infections. Similarly, their gut microbiome appears to be highly speciallized, able to process decaying organic matter, while simultaneously shielding them from toxins in spoiled food.

In sum these biological adaptations make the Pink Larvae disproportionately resistant when compared to most known scarabaeiform species.

History

The following document recounts the first sighting of the Pink Larva and possibly its first victims

Document Larvae

Do's and Don'ts

Do:

*When observing a larva, take note if it could be a Pink Larva.

*If you feel horrible pain after eating any food you found, go see the doctor!

*Always report any location where there may be a Pink Larva infestation

Don't:

*Do not consume any food before inspecting it.

*Make sure you fully understand the nature of this entity before entering a bug-level level such as Level 479

----

----

Pink Larvae are typically discovered in rotten food or large food items, as wherever you find one Pink Larvae, there are almost always two or more.

When Pink Larvae perceive that a human or entity begins to eat the food in which they are hiding, the Pink Larvae curl up or cover themselves so they can't be detected and then enter the organism of the human or entity. Once inside the organism, specifically in the stomach, they grow and create a cavity. This cavity causes the host intense pain. While in this cavity, they begin to lay eggs.

After a while, inside the stomach, they change color to black, red, or, most frequently, pink, and grow until they are twice the larva's length. At this stage, they are no longer considered larvae and are named Pink Shells. After this stage, they become completely static and remain in this state within the host.

As time passes, previously laid eggs hatch into new larvae. These larvae quickly begin laying more eggs. This cycle repeats throughout the life cycle. At this stage, the host feels an unusual fullness, as if they had overeaten. Their stomach, now nearly full of Pink Shells, causes them to lose their appetite. As the stomach grows abnormally swollen, some larvae leave and move to other organs, except the heart and other critical ones.

While inside the host's body, the larvae and shells are actively consuming nutrients. At a certain point, the vast majority of the host's organism is contaminated with Pink Larvae and Pink Shells, and they will then begin to come out of the mouth, eyes, or even from the pores of the skin of the host. This has an extremely painful effect in the host's body.

Once the infestation has spread to the entire body of the host, the larvae will start to invade the vital organs, terminating the host's life. The skin of the corpse will then develop crusts where the larvae exited and will start decaying rapidly. The body may be barely recognizable, essentially a lump of rotting flesh full of larvae and Pink Shells, in just under two days.

At the moment of death, two or three Pink Shells will uncurl and leave the body, looking for other food sources, and lay their eggs before returning to the corpse. A few hours or days later, all the other larvae and Pink Shells will die because there are no nutrients left for them.

Anatomy

Structurally, Pink Larvae is quite similar to rhinoceros beetle larvae from the Frontrooms. Materially, the only distinction is their capacity to reshape either as a pink-slime-form or as a very dense stone-like structure that provides a host of different visual and physical cues.

They also exhibit a phenomenal resistance to gastric juices and other corrosive environments, making them exceptionally durable as a larva, and it appears their cuticle has layers of chitin bolstered with a keratin-like layered structure, providing them flexibility and durability. This form of an exoskeleton allows them to exhibit physical and biochemical features towards tolerating extreme acidity and basicity pH, as well as various enzymatic activity normally capable of obtaining complete organic tissue decay.

Pink larva displays varying degrees of morphological plasticity patterns of variation: within its set of specialized tissues, autonomous cells modulate to rapidly re-configure their internal and external body structure and switch between soft, amorphous tissue type to a hardened, mineral-based type. This mutability lends evidence to the notion of cryptobiosis, an observable form of life in which a species shifts or suppresses its metabolic systems while under environmental stress.

Moreover, their hemolymph (the insect equivalent of blood) contains antimicrobial peptides and enzymes able to neutralize pathogens, lending them a natural immunity to most bacteria and fungal infections. Similarly, their gut microbiome appears to be highly speciallized, able to process decaying organic matter, while simultaneously shielding them from toxins in spoiled food.

In sum these biological adaptations make the Pink Larvae disproportionately resistant when compared to most known scarabaeiform species.

History

The following document recounts the first sighting of the Pink Larva and possibly its first victims

Document Larvae

Do's and Don'ts

Do:

*When observing a larva, take note if it could be a Pink Larva.

*If you feel horrible pain after eating any food you found, go see the doctor!

*Always report any location where there may be a Pink Larva infestation

Don't:

*Do not consume any food before inspecting it.

*Make sure you fully understand the nature of this entity before entering a bug-level level such as Level 479

----

Level 989.3.title: Level 989.3
Level 989.3.description: Level 989.3 is a large complex of houses and miscellaneous structures, floating above a presumably infinite ocean. While at first it appears to be made up of a broad-ranging set of materials, closer inspection reveals that all non-organic surfaced in the level are constructed entirely from tiles of varying color, but the same uniform material. A wide variety of structures are present, the most common of these being Houses. Houses are uniquely non-Euclidean, with the interior space far larger than the exterior could accommodate under normal circumstances. The interiors of these Houses widely vary, from deep swimming pools with floating inflatable furniture to tiled houses with furniture uniquely not made out of tiles. Another notable structure within the level is referred to as Arches. This is a broad term for the square arches, serving seemingly no purpose, spread throughout the level. Due to the grayed colors and distinct lack of contrast, it is almost impossible to tell it is made out of tiles unless you feel it. Arches are completely benign and no negative side effects have been traced to it. Another prevalent structure is the Pools, salmon-colored tiled depressions at various points within the level filled with crystal clear water. The pools vary in temperature from 50°F to 100°F, while most hover at ~80°F. Excluding the presence of one particular entity which will be elaborated on later, these pools host no life and are entirely safe, even to drink. Natural plant life in the form of palm trees and small stretches of Kentucky Bluegrass dot the tile in small depressions filled with dirt. A third unique structure spread around the level is the Towers. These Towers manifest as roughly twelve-foot-tall tiled towers, typically with some form of lighting fixture at the junction between the roof and the walls. These towers can also be placed in close proximity with each other, in which case a bridge will form at the very top that reaches down four feet, or 1/3rd of the tower's total height. They are popular areas for sightseeing.
Level 989.3.lead: Level 989.3 is the only known sublevel of Level 989.

entrances.text: The primary entrance into this level is jumping off the tower of Level 989, which may rarely lead here.
Rarely, a similar entrance can be encountered by climbing down the tower of Level 989 using the stairs.
Very rarely in aquatic levels, a painting resembling the level may appear, interacting with it may send one here.

exits.text: Swimming out far enough may lead to Level 330 or Level 100.
Locating the tower of Level 989 and beginning to ascend it will cause one to black out and awaken at the typical starting point.
Any exits performable in normal circumstances in Level 989 can be performed here, as there is an entrance to The Guts located at floor level.

Juju Man.title: Juju Man
Juju Man.description:
The Juju Man, also alluded to as Epiales, the Lord of Nightmares, is an anomalous humanoid entity who has been witnessed wandering the halls of the Backrooms. Although defined by himself as an eldritch immortal who is able to take on many different forms, he has only ever been definitively documented assuming the shape of a tall, middle-aged anthropoid figure garbed in a monochromatic outfit. His garments include a large grey fedora which obscures his face at all times, a dusty black overcoat worn over a formal shirt and trousers, a belt to which a large metal ring of keys is constantly attached, and occasionally a silver-topped walking cane.

The keys belonging to the Juju Man largely act as charms and talismans enabling him to work his anomalous powers. Some of these are simply level keys, but many are rumoured to be possessed of far stranger and more wondrous mysticism. The most arcane of these keys are, according to the Juju Man, able to unlock and penetrate deep into a wanderer's soul; to read their most primal loves, hatreds and fears; to warp them to his own will. All the talismanic keys of the Juju Man are impalpable to mortals, and those unfortunate souls who have come into contact with them have been known to dissolve into nothingness be erased from genuine existence.

The Juju Man is the self-proclaimed Lord of Nightmares. In the entity's own words,
:Do you know what a nightmare is? It is very different from a dream. Most of the experiences you mortals would call nightmares are, in fact, just dreams of a more morbid nature. But perhaps I am talking myself in circles trying to explain all of this to you in your own language, which bears not terms enough to describe the workings of the wider cosmos.

:The mortal mind can be fragmented into three basic aspects. There is thought, which is composed of ideas, opinions, solutions, judgements. Thought comprises the main body of the Void's kaleidoscopic ocean which you term the Akashic Field. There is emotion, which comprises subtler, more instinctive feelings that permeate the mind and influence thought. Emotion lurks in the depths of the Akashic, in an underlying area you term the Empathic Subfield. And there is the essence, the soul, which predates the Akashic and has existed since the very beginning. The essence knows only three core feelings to which all the mind is a slave: Love, Hatred, and their child, Fear.

:A true nightmare is one that harnesses the potential of the soul. Not one formed of thought and emotion, as most dreams are, but of raw essence. One so bizarre and so delirious that thought cannot comprehend it and emotion cannot grasp it. I am the Lord of Nightmares, the sole facilitator of the cosmic, incomprehensible terrors that haunt the minds of the slumbering. For I am the only one who holds the keys unto the soul.

The full extent of the Juju Man's abilities is unknown, but rumours often allege that the keys in which his power is apparently contained possess higher-order capabilities such as recalling the dead, warping the fabrics of space and time, and restringing the metaphysical to his will. The entity is also often causes lights and screens around him to flicker, and his presence is most distinctly recognised by the distortion of sounds and other sensations. Many reports describe his appearance quivering in and out of existence, as if he were not truly present. Whether these supposed abilities hail from the Juju Man's talismans, or are innate powers which he himself possesses, is difficult to ascertain.
Juju Man.lead:
And Phrike wandered amongst those bronze defects

Of Hephaestus's, shrewd Coppersmith, deific creation, which he

Would not show to the other gods, and instead flung

Unceremoniously down to the iron hells betwixt sleep and lucidity,

In lonely Hypnos's shifting domain, the land of baleful dreams.

And through those machinations ran the currents

Of the one-eyed cyclopes, the defunct bolts they had forged

Under Etna for the King of Olympus, but had been waylaid

By the storm giant Typhon, son of speckled Echidna mother of monsters,

Who in his sleep stirred and broke the handiwork of the cyclopes

And they were forced to cast their bolts

Into the sleeping world beneath even the depths of brooding Hades's.

And Phrike looked about and was filled of wonder,

For he knew not the mastery of Hephaestus and only

That which the telchines had performed before him, and

Though they were the smiths of Poseidon, Earth-shaker, Zeus's brother,

And made him his trident from sea-stones and ocean-metal,

Their skill compared not with the club-footed god of all craftsmen.

But in those hells Phrike saw something which

He did not expect; he saw a man tall and dark,

And he was wreathed in shadow as the primordial spirit Erebus,

Who had no parent save unknowable Chaos and no mate save Mother Night,

And his children were rosy-fingered Day and pure-hearted Light.

But Phrike knew 'twas no avatar of gloomy Erebus, for even then

Upon his belt the spirit carried a ring of adamant keys,

And he looked upon the son of the old god Phorcys, and his cap

Was turned so that Phrike could not behold his face, and yet

The spirit's eyes were alight with divine fire which had not been seen

Since the Titans' reign, when crooked Kronos who from the Earth's womb sprang

Grappled for the throne of all the worlds and voids with Zeus his son.

But then the shadow passed, and the spirit was gone,

Yet on the ground he had left behind a single key,

Though whether he had been a spectre but of the mind

Or if he had truly stood once there in that space

Which now was empty, and where now none stood,

Phrike could not ascertain.

~ The Phrikeid

But who is he?

exits.text: • No-clipping through a noisy, and blurry wall, may lead one to Level 238.• Exploring into an irregularly bright area, has the chance of the current level transitioning to Level 242 at anytime.

Once Was, Now Is.title: Once Was, Now Is
Once Was, Now Is.description:
Once Was, Now Is manifests as a small yellow house elevated on stilts, situated in the centre of an infinitely stretching field littered with blood-red poppies. Despite the absence of a sun, the environment is uniformly illuminated with daylight. A singular cumulus cloud hangs in the sky, but does not move at all.
“Once Was Lost, Now Is Found.”
If wanderers choose not to enter the house within 99 minutes of arriving, the level undergoes a drastic shift. We refer to this state as “Once Was Lost, Now Is Found.” Entities will begin to appear in their peripheral vision as blurred, muted silhouettes. They perfectly mimic the posture, height, and gait of a loved one, a childhood friend, or a deceased relative. They never speak, but their presence radiates an intense aura. Approaching these silhouettes is impossible, as they will always retreat to remain at the edge of their vision. However, a special silhouette may appear, taking the form of The Custodian.
It may appear anywhere between 100 to 999 minutes after entering the level.

It will always remain in the direct centre of their vision for as long as the wanderer stays in the field.

Approaching The Custodian will cause its form to distort violently. Words will be beamed directly into the wanderer's brain:
“APPROACH>>” “SEIZE>>” “PROCeEED>>” “YOU COuLD HAVE DoNE THINGS DIFFERENTLY>>”
It is unknown what happens to those who disobey these commands. If the wanderer makes any noise within 100 meters of The Custodian at this stage, they will be instantly transported to the sub-level “Once Was Known, Now Is Forgotten.”
Once Was, Now Is.lead:
Content Warning

This page contains sensitive content not suitable for all audiences. Readers including you might consider these topic(s) disturbing, anxiety inducing, or inappropriate: }.
Do you see the vision?

If the content mentioned above is suitable for you, sit back, relax and enjoy!

M.E.G. ARCHIVAL RECORDClassification: Psychological Hazard

The house as it first appears.

exits.text: Attempting to go back the way the wanderer came (through the pipe maze) is futile. The hatch will be replaced by a door labeled "The End." Opening it leads directly to Level ! (Run For Your Life).

Entity 65.title: Entity 65
Entity 65.description:
Entity 65, also known as "Bloodmist", (scientifically referred to as Nebulovoridae sanguinaria) appears to the eyes of Wanderers as red clouds of airborne particles. Scientific testing has shown that particles of Bloodmist are not vaporized matter, but are instead semi-coordinated micro-organisms that appear to be red in color. These micro-organisms travel in swarms and present major hazards in levels that they inhabit. Inhaling Bloodmist has been known to cause excessive coughing, sores, internal and external lacerations, hemorrhaging, and possibly death if sufficiently high quantities of Bloodmist are inhaled.

Bloodmist is predominately found in levels that contain blood or blood-like substances as a natural aspect of their environments, or levels that boast higher casualty rates. This is due to the fact that N. sanguinaria micro-organisms are formed by the mutation of blood cell DNA. This is caused by an undetermined chemical, known as MTG-HB1, that is present in the air of various levels within the Backrooms. Due to the anonymity of MTG-HB1, testing is impossible to be conducted accurately. Researchers have also correlated occurrences between bloodmist and level decay. It has been theorized that this is due to MTG-HB1 being highly corrosive; however, this is unconfirmed, as levels with high quantities of the chemical have no corrosive effect on biological matter such as human or entity tissues.

Immediately after inhaling Bloodmist, the affected Wanderer will begin to cough uncontrollably as the body attempts to clear the foreign particles from the lungs. Sores will start to form on the victim's skin and within their lungs. The Nebulovoridae sanguinaria micro-organisms will consume large portions of the individual's flesh, resulting in severe bleeding both internally and externally, resulting in hemoptysis. Their lungs will blister, and they will attempt to cough up the excess blood pooled in their respiratory system, often to little or no avail. Eventually, the Bloodmist swarm will devour the target's tissues entirely, and all that will remain of the corpse is the skeletal structures and a puddle of blood, which will rapidly mutate into Nebulovoridae sanguinaria, a process assisted by the already present Bloodmist.

Due to the fact that Bloodmist's presence causes regular blood to mutate, clouds of Bloodmist can split or grow rapidly after taking a wanderer's life. Portions of certain levels have been made completely uninhabitable as a result of the sheer amount of Bloodmist present within the level. Levels that have no trace of MTG-HB1 will be contaminated with the chemical if Bloodmist is released into that level. Therefore, if one becomes aware of the presence of N. sanguinaria micro-organisms within a level, it is advised to exit said level and contact any available groups as soon as is possible.

Nebulovoridae sanguinaria micro-organisms move by electrically repelling themselves away from air molecules. This requires large amounts of energy, which can be gathered from any molecules in the environment around them via inelastic collision. It requires much less energy for a swarm to establish or maintain this repelling force. Therefore, larger swarms can follow wanderers for more extended periods than smaller swarms. However, this does not occur often as a result of the impractical energy expenditure and the following recovery period.

Bloodmist swarms are held together by hydrogen bonds in a manner that is very similar to how water-based mist is held together. Each micro-organism can electrically communicate through these hydrogen bonds, and these communications can be disrupted with charged electrodes (or intercepted with the proper technology). These messages are necessary in swarms to maintain the electromagnetic force required for movement, therefore, disrupting communication between the micro-organisms effectively halts the coordinated movement of swarms.

Bloodmist possesses multiple notable weaknesses, including the aforementioned electronic disruption, which can be exploited in the interest of evading death in the event of an encounter with swarms of N. sanguinaria. One of these weaknesses is Neon Velocity; research shows that Bloodmist will not enter an area where the air has a concentration of 20% gaseous Neon Velocity or greater. In addition, upon contact with Neon Velocity, the micro-organisms' metabolism will increase to a point where the organism has a very high chance of running out of energy. However, Almond Water and Cashew Water have the opposite effect. They will increase the Nebulovoridae sanguinaria's metabolism rates while not pushing them too high, which, in turn, renders the consumption process more rapid and increases energy levels in the micro-organisms.

The next weakness of Bloodmist is excessive heat; the micro-organisms will often spontaneously combust if they overheat, thus, coming into contact with an open flame results in instant death for them. Therefore, it is advised to possess Firesalt while wandering through levels with high quantities of Bloodmist present. The M.E.G. has invented many weapons using Firesalt that could be used against Bloodmist (or other entities) in an emergency, though these should be treated as an option secondary to attempting to exit the given level.

Level 345.title: Level 345
Level 345.description:
Level 345, also known as The Dreamcore Town, resembles a peculiar, quiet town, perpetually stuck in a late spring afternoon. The sky is unusually clear, colored a faint cyan-green tint.  There are never any changes to the overall brightness, nor is there any real sense of time passing.

Early 2010s suburban houses flank the sides of the streets and sidewalks, with one house roughly every 0.02km. Each house has a lawn, consisting of two separate plots of well-maintained grass, measured to take up a surface area of roughly 0.15km and 0.08km, respectively. Both of these plots are separated by a clean, concrete driveway. Flanking the sides of the plots is a basic mangrove wooden fence, about 4 meters tall. The front porches of the houses lack any decorations, nor any unique paint jobs. Everything looks new, lacking any signs of damage, wear, or abandonment. Likewise, there is a complete absence of any residents or general intelligent life.

Despite looking peaceful and nostalgic, the area appears subtly unnatural.
Level 345.lead:
M.E.G. ARCHIVAL DATABASE
    LEVEL 345 FILE RECOVERED
    [01] Recovering fragmented suburban record...
    [02] Verifying psychological hazard notes...
    [03] Access granted.
    DISPLAYING LEVEL

M.E.G. Database Login Form

Insert Username

Insert Passphrase

Password valid.

Welcome, !

  Play Audio

Level 345  is the 346th level of the Backrooms discovered by M.E.G. on May 18th, 2011.

----

entrances.text: Staring at a town photograph for an extended period in Level 4, which may cause nearby walls to shift into the architecture of this level.
Following the sound of a distant school bell in Level 11 until you find a door that should not be there.

exits.text: Walking into direct sunlight until a visual whiteout occurs may cause one to noclip into Level 89.
Entering a child’s closet and closing the door behind oneself may result in emergence within Level 320.
Falling asleep inside a furnished bedroom may result in awakening in Level 94.
Touching a small, round turquoise toy ball toy replica of a wireframe sphere found within the residential buildings in this level will cause an immediate transition to Level 346.

Entity 110.title: Entity 110
Entity 110.description:
Entity 110, also known as "The Unfinished Symphony", is a complex, exposed-chassis biomechanical structure, approximately 2 meters in height. Entity 110 is fundamentally composed of a mismatched, jury-rigged amalgamation of industrial actuators, pistons, hydraulic lines, and a massive internal network of power conduits that lack a unifying schematical pattern. These components are secured to a central spinal frame that exhibits signs of heavy corrosion and systemic electrical arcing, yet the structure remains entirely self-sustaining. Entity 110 is perpetually suspended from a ceiling by a bundle of thick, severed data cables that are actively pulling against its own gravitational force.

Entity 110’s primary sensory array is located where a head would be but instead consists of a cluster of multiple, multi-focal lens asemblies and exposed CCD sensors, giving the impression of an arachnid visual system rather than a human one. Directly above this lens cluster is a single, cracked CRT monitor. This monitor does not display a face, but rather a constant, scrolling feed of hexadecimal error codes and fragmented audio-reactive waveforms. The monitor has been observed to change colors from a green to a purple, a visual cue that correlates with shifts in Entity 110’s acoustic and thermals output.

The lower limbs of Entity 110 are engineered for stability, utilizing oversized pistons and reinforced feet. However, it does not move by walking. Instead, it seems to "skate" and maintain balance by interacting with the cylindrical object it was first discovered on. This object is a massive, hollow structural drum made of a ceramic-polymer composite, possibly a part of the original machine Entity 110 was synthesized from. The drum itself emits a constant, high-frequency kinetic vibration and is considered an extension of Entity 110. To date, no force has been able to separate Entity 110 from this platform without catastrophic failure of the localized area.

Structurally, Entity 110 is in a state of suspended decay. Thousands of wires and cables are not integrated into the body but dangle in loose, tangled loops, trailing for meters behind and beneath it like a mechanical root system. Many of these wires are actively arcing with current, while others seem to have "fused" with the surrounding environment, turning the ceiling, walls, and floor within a 3-meter radius into a chaotic network of parasitic conduits. In areas where Entity 110 has been stationary for extended periods, the architecture itself has begun to mimic Entity 110’s wiring, with concrete cracking into patterns identical to its circuitry.

Entity 31.title: Entity 31
Entity 31.description:
Maws are highly ravenous entities born from the prolonged hunger of wanderers. If a living wanderer is in a state of extreme starvation, there is a small chance that they will transform into a Maw. The process in which this happens is exceptionally gruesome.

As the transformation begins, the victim's lips will rapidly pull and fold back into the surrounding flesh, with the rest of their body soon following. Everything under the skin also folds around once the change gets past the jaws, turning the victim inside out in mere seconds. This process does not leave the body in the shape it was before, instead rendering it a limbless mass of innards trailing along in a serpentine form. The alteration fuses the organs together so the new Maw does not fall apart.

Maws are able to sense the location of nearby meat; the closer the entity is, the more accurately it can hone in on the food. While this most strongly applies to food of good quality, it also functions with partially spoiled foods to a lesser degree. Nonetheless, when a Maw eats, most of the mass from the meal is converted into its own mass, with rare documented instances of the entity growing well over three times their original size. While it is unknown what the size limit to their growth is — if any — no Maws have ever been known to grow larger than the approximate size of a bus. This is due to the increased difficulty for larger Maws of finding enough food to sustain themselves.

Wanderers sometimes leave poisoned food around for Maws in what are fittingly known as "Maw Traps", including safe food along with it to entice the entity closer. Since Maws will be less inclined to eat the poisonous meal on its own but do not actually recognize its danger, they will usually fall for the bait. To make sure other wanderers do not fall for the same trap, some of the deadly food will be arranged in a triangle around the rest of it; failing that, the triangle will simply be drawn with something like chalk. Maw Traps have raised ethical concerns considering how wanderers unfamiliar with Backrooms culture might fall victim to them, but many wanderers make them regardless, as Maws are far more likely to find them than uneducated wanderers.

Entity 95.title: Entity 95
Entity 95.displayTitle: Entity 95: "FORTH IT CRAWLS; DESPITE ALL AIMS TO DEFEAT OR DEMORALIZE, FORTH IT CRAWLS REGARDLESS"
Entity 95.description:
----

Entity 95 is an individual expanding trench system embedded within the flooring of its inhabited level. The surrounding area to the trench opening is covered in dirt and stone, with structures such as sandbags and parapet supports present throughout the rubble. Erupting from the trench itself is a swarm of bony, pitch-black arms holding rifles, shovels, or tattered gurneys. The rifle-bearing arms open fire at anything Entity 95 deems a "threat"; typically any nearby lifeforms. Arms holding shovels use them to "dig" into nearby terrain, causing Entity 95's trench system to expand in the target direction. After killing a threat, the arms that carry gurneys hoist the body upon their frames and drag them into the trench. Where bodies go after being delivered into the trench is unknown, though such events tend to trigger a scenario known as "ration distribution", wherein all of Entity 95's arms retreat into the trench and re-emerge shortly afterwards with temporarily enhanced reaction time and strength.

While resistant to many forces, Entity 95 can be harmed by explosions, particularly when activated within the trench. Once struck by an explosive such as a grenade, the trench section in range of the explosion becomes entirely eradicated and replaced with the level's natural flooring. Any exposed arms within range of the explosion will violently burst blood. If the perpetrator of the explosion in question strikes an arm, they will immediately feel an overwhelming sense of guilt that greatly discourages further attacks.

Arms travel about the trench system in pairs, much like humanoid figures. Each pair of arms, henceforth referred to as Entity 95's "infantrymen", behave with similar intelligence to humans, albeit with highly reduced survival instincts. Infantrymen can recover from wounds by retreating and returning from the trench (this method also applying to their tools). Because tools such as rifles regenerate from Entity 95, many prosperous groups within the Backrooms have opted to obtain firearms via ripping them from the hands when off-guard. Such a task is, however, extremely hazardous, requiring heavy preparations and strategic execution beforehand. Even so, the function of Entity 95's rifles is inherently tied into the arm itself, leaving rifles obtained this way non-functional until repairs are made.

Entity 95 comprises the trench and the arms within it. Although a well-accepted definition today, it has long been argued prior to recent research that the infantrymen themselves were each their own individual entity inhabiting the trench system as a shelter. Because of the trench's anomalous interactions with the environment in tandem with the arms, it has been settled that Entity 95 is the trench system itself. In casual language, however, most refer to Entity 95 as multiple beings due to the anthropomorphic and independent behavior of each infantryman.

Entity 95 is indiscriminately aggressive towards all large lifeforms it encounters. How it senses nearby life is unknown, though further studies of footage suggest that Entity 95 detects threats based on sound, although it is not always accurate at deducing which sources of noise are living beings. If one is under attack by Entity 95, seeking cover and throwing an object away from oneself to create noise can cause a distraction and allow a silent retreat - silent, as excessive noise can draw Entity 95's attention back to oneself.

While not physically restricted in doing so, Entity 95 will not expand its trench system further than tens of meters. If Entity 95 wishes to travel a further distance, the shovel-wielding arms will shovel nearby rubble into unneeded trench sections to reduce its size. This behavior of Entity 95 is likely due to the limited nature of its arms; only so many infantrymen can exist at once, and separating them by a significant distance limits the potential firepower of its rifle-wielding arms. By digging forward and flattening the trailing sections, Entity 95 can "inch" at an estimated 19 km/h. For reference, the average human sprinting speed is 28 km/h; if Entity 95 is aware of your exact presence, running cannot save you forever.

No clear origin has been evidenced for Entity 95; for all of recorded history, its form has remained unchanged from a 20th century trench network:

RECOVERED WRITTEN NOTE - DATED APRIL 1722
"...the beasts encroached from within a concave mound of soil, one that should not have pierced the rocky plain it arose in. [The beasts'] arms wielded tools: one kind a spade, the other an oddly short yet fierce musket with a blade affixed to its barrel. Truly they were mindlessly aggressive as would be expected of a wild animal."

Because Entity 95 is an individual entity, multiple attempts have sprouted to completely eradicate it due to the great threat it poses. All attempts to eradicate Entity 95 have, by perhaps sheer coincidence, failed in some capacity despite the first-impression ease of such a task. Constant failure to lead Entity 95 to extinction has led to the question of whether it is indeed impossible to entirely kill it.
----

----

Each pair of arms, henceforth referred to as Entity 95's "infantrymen", behave with similar intelligence to humans, albeit with highly reduced survival instincts. Infantrymen can recover from wounds by retreating and returning from the trench (this method also applying to their tools). Because tools such as rifles regenerate from Entity 95, many prosperous groups within the Backrooms have opted to obtain firearms via ripping them from the hands when off-guard. Such a task is, however, extremely hazardous, requiring heavy preparations and strategic execution beforehand. Even so, the function of Entity 95's rifles is inherently tied into the arm itself, leaving rifles obtained this way non-functional until repairs are made.

Entity 95 comprises the trench and the arms within it. Although a well-accepted definition today, it has long been argued prior to recent research that the infantrymen themselves were each their own individual entity inhabiting the trench system as a shelter. Because of the trench's anomalous interactions with the environment in tandem with the arms, it has been settled that Entity 95 is the trench system itself. In casual language, however, most refer to Entity 95 as multiple beings due to the anthropomorphic and independent behavior of each infantryman.

Entity 95 is indiscriminately aggressive towards all large lifeforms it encounters. How it senses nearby life is unknown, though further studies of footage suggest that Entity 95 detects threats based on sound, although it is not always accurate at deducing which sources of noise are living beings. If one is under attack by Entity 95, seeking cover and throwing an object away from oneself to create noise can cause a distraction and allow a silent retreat - silent, as excessive noise can draw Entity 95's attention back to oneself.

While not physically restricted in doing so, Entity 95 will not expand its trench system further than tens of meters. If Entity 95 wishes to travel a further distance, the shovel-wielding arms will shovel nearby rubble into unneeded trench sections to reduce its size. This behavior of Entity 95 is likely due to the limited nature of its arms; only so many infantrymen can exist at once, and separating them by a significant distance limits the potential firepower of its rifle-wielding arms. By digging forward and flattening the trailing sections, Entity 95 can "inch" at an estimated 19 km/h. For reference, the average human sprinting speed is 28 km/h; if Entity 95 is aware of your exact presence, running cannot save you forever.

No clear origin has been evidenced for Entity 95; for all of recorded history, its form has remained unchanged from a 20th century trench network:

RECOVERED WRITTEN NOTE - DATED APRIL 1722
"...the beasts encroached from within a concave mound of soil, one that should not have pierced the rocky plain it arose in. [The beasts'] arms wielded tools: one kind a spade, the other an oddly short yet fierce musket with a blade affixed to its barrel. Truly they were mindlessly aggressive as would be expected of a wild animal."

Because Entity 95 is an individual entity, multiple attempts have sprouted to completely eradicate it due to the great threat it poses. All attempts to eradicate Entity 95 have, by perhaps sheer coincidence, failed in some capacity despite the first-impression ease of such a task. Constant failure to lead Entity 95 to extinction has led to the question of whether it is indeed impossible to entirely kill it.
----

The Phantom.title: The Phantom
The Phantom.description:
The Phantom, also known as The Thing in the Dark, has existed for about all of human history in the Backrooms and almost certainly longer. It lurks in absolute darkness without conventional spacial restraints, moving from one dark spot to the next and not needing to cross the space between. Suffice to say, any wanderer who finds themselves in the dark may become targeted by the Phantom. Even wanderers within range of good light sources are susceptible, though much less so. Any other factors besides darkness determining who the Phantom is more likely to come after are unknown, with the patterns seemingly being random.

When one is targeted, they will experience the entity stalking them in darkness that allows just the slightest impression of its form, moving back into the blackness and out again at different times and places. Scarce reports involving details of the entity's appearance entail many humanoid limbs, an unnaturally shaped head and an overall large figure. Only the victims are able to see the Phantom, leading to theories that this may not even reflect its true form.

Should one stay in absolute darkness, the Phantom will torment them while remaining unseen. This may include things such has grabbing one's shoulder from behind, tripping one by seizing one's ankle, lacerating one's skin with what seem to be claws, and more. All this will quickly escalate into the victim meeting an agonizing death. There have been no sightings of the killing itself, but the aftermath is notorious for being brutal and gruesome. Occurrences such as bones left jutting out, deep gouges in the flesh and skin ripped off are just a few of many.

Even if one runs to and stays in the light indefinitely, the Phantom will continue shadowing them. When it is near, electrical lights are liable to flicker and even go out. Thus, relying on other light sources such as lanterns and candles are much more recommended in such predicaments. As long as there is enough nearby light, the entity cannot advance. It should be noted, however, that it can still reach out of total or near-thereof darkness or blow a gale so far that it extinguishes the flame. Additionally, those who find themselves in outdoor levels after being stalked will end up being confronted with an inescapable, light-obscuring fog. Therefore, many regard the Phantom as less of an entity and more of an unavoidable fate.

----
