Entity 25.title: Entity 25
Entity 25.description:
Entity 25, most often referred to as the Displays, are bipedal humanoids that resemble a construct of randomized pieces of furniture, hardware, appliances, or other household objects. As of now, it is believed that no two recorded Displays share the same appearance, all containing different arbitrary elements that almost seem to be fused together completely at random. Despite the entities' irregular physiology, instances have been observed moving at speeds estimated at up to 10 miles per hour (16 kilometers per hour). Displays, while standing upright, will vary in height depending on the instance, but typically range from 12 to 20 feet tall (3.6 to 6.1 meters tall). The Displays' origins are unknown as of now, but the leading theory (yet to be confirmed) is that the creature is using a form of hyperrealistic mimicry to imitate ordinary furniture.

Displays are most commonly encountered in levels containing large quantities of furniture, though sightings have been reported in a variety of unrelated environments. These entities have the ability to disassemble themselves at will, making them look identical to an unorganized pile of furniture. This ability makes them blend in with their surroundings so much so that they are indistinguishable from non-anomalous pieces of furniture. In fact, several pieces of recovered footage submitted to the M.E.G. depict wanderers passing directly by (what is assumed to be) dormant Displays without noticing them. Although no Displays share the same physical form, it is unknown why they all take the same basic humanoid shape, especially since the pieces that make up their body seem to be selected with little regard to practicality or symmetry. On top of this, many can have mutations, such as an extra "arm" or "head," or a limb that is much longer than the other, which seems to have no practical use.
Entity 25.lead:
The M.E.G. would like to preface by saying that this article is unfinished! Our goal is to provide accurate survival information for everything in the Backrooms, and we need your help to learn more about Entity 25. If you or someone you know has any valuable information about this entity, please bring it to the closest M.E.G. outpost immediately. Thank you and happy reading!

ENTITY NO.
25

THREAT LEVEL
4

Habitat:
}

Aggression: 5/5
Aggressive to humans and intelligent beings

Intellect: 2/5
Low intelligence, but capable of problem-solving

Danger: 5/5
Life-threatening

Entity 140.title: Entity 140
Entity 140.description:
Entity 140, commonly referred to as the Flesh Bacteria, is a pervasive microscopic biological organism endemic to Level 563. Unlike conventional pathogens, it does not appear to exist as a single localized infection, but rather as a distributed system present throughout the Banquet Hall’s food supply, organic residue, and structural composition.

Contamination is most commonly associated with consumable materials found within the level, though prolonged exposure to the environment itself has also been linked to infection onset. Once introduced into a host, Entity 140 initiates gradual physiological and cognitive alterations centered around hunger response, sensory perception, and food-related fixation.
Entity 140.lead:
Do not trust your hunger inside the Banquet Hall. After all, it is not yours anymore.

— M.E.G. Containment Advisory, Level 563
Entity 140.image: A microscopic picture taken in a M.E.G lab of The Flesh Bacteria.jpg

Entity 88.title: Entity 88
Entity 88.description:
The Reality Magnet is designated as the 88th entity of the Backrooms.

Entity 88, otherwise formally nicknamed as the Reality Magnet, is mostly known for its ability to forcefully pull and, thus, extract structural parts within its vicinity, and can seldom warp time of its surrounding. Because of this capability, crumbled sections of walls, destroyed light fixtures, and unrecognizable furnitures are constantly shielding the entity, which also effectively makes it impossible to identify its true, naked appearance. However, this swirling shield does not only consist of cold concrete and metal, as the entity roams through a level, it forcefully drags the remains of its previous victims into its orbit. The bodies of unfortunate wanderers are instantly crushed upon impact, their bones snapped and fused into the rotating mass of debris. Shredded clothing, dried blood, and mangled limbs can often be seen mangled and mixed between the broken bricks. It is, as of yet, inexplicable how the entity possess such form of gravitational pull, but the gruesome sight of human remains spinning alongside a sphere of shattered walls makes it one of the most terrifying phenomena in the Backrooms.

Entity 58.title: Entity 58
Entity 58.description: The Sleep Paralysis Demon, officially assigned the designation of Entity 58, is an entity that peculiarly mirrors the sleep paralysis phenomenon in The Frontrooms, experienced by a minority of individuals. However, unlike the harmless nature of the phenomenon in The Frontrooms, the phenomenon is significantly more harmful when it occurs in The Backrooms. Due to the extreme difficulty of tracking encounters and collecting testimonies from encounters, it is considered one of the least understood entities within The Backrooms. Entity 58 often selects wanderers who are sleeping on solid surfaces, such as floors, tables, or chairs, as preferred victims.

The Co-Hosts.title: The Co-Hosts
The Co-Hosts.description:
The Co-Hosts are an advisory group of around four to six jovials who comprise Caius Taggert's entourage, effectively fulfilling the entire rudimentary government system of Level Fun. They are some of the only infected individuals known to simultaneously be jovials and partygoers. The Party Host has alternatively referred to them as his "children", his "ministers" and his "pets"; while it was initially believed that they were "running dogs" utilised to preserve the illusion of freedom amongst the Partygoer Jamboree, through the recent procurement of more information regarding the matter, the Party Pooper has discovered that the Co-Hosts are indeed quite influential in the government of Level Fun, working almost in tandem with rather than beneath Sir Taggert.

The membership of the order has seen many iterations since the conception of Co-Hostship in the mid-1800s, ranging from the earliest, commedia dell'arte-inspired retinue, to the modern, practical advisory council of manipulative and, to some extent, politically capable jovials. The shift away from goofier characters to a more serious and practical approach has, according to Taggert, been in accordance with the general rise of anti-partygoer groups and sentiments in the Backrooms. Interestingly, every generation of Co-Hosts seemed to have died together, giving rise to the theory that the Party Host actively orchestrates the replacement of each iteration with new, younger Co-Hosts.

Each Co-Host is apparently hand-picked and infected, usually secretly, by the Party Host himself. Unlike other partygoers, their minds are not eclipsed upon infection, and instead they often find themselves wandering through levels in an uncoordinated and semi-lucid state, subconsciously wired to seek out the Party Rooms, where Taggert will greet them warmly and integrate them into his council. Each Co-Host devises a "stage name" for themselves, whereby they are known among the wider public of the Wake, but the Party Host also gives them a new personal name in the spirit that they ultimately belong to him.

It is presumed that the Co-Hosts themselves are fearful in some respect of Caius Taggert, even if they show no outward signs of it. They indeed appear to treat him as merely an older brother-figure and occasionally mock him for his age, and a "suicide interview" with Co-Host Festus "Festhead" Zacharias has revealed his opinions of Taggert as a "nice, harmless old friend, if a little too uptight at times"; however, medical expert Dr Asclepius Ang has expressed doubt that "a person, unless their amygdala is completely fried, is incapable of fearing, even just the tiniest bit, a man who has them hung up on puppet-strings, and whom they know has them hung up on puppet-strings, and whom they know they can never escape."

The Rot Infection.title: The Rot Infection
The Rot Infection.description:
The Rot Infection is a virus-based entity that targets the nervous system and takes over the body.

This virus is considered one of the great hazards of the Backrooms, with most encounters being lethal. The entity has two forms, the virus and the rotted, with subforms branching out from them.

The two forms are the Infection Virus form and the Rot Victim (or Rotted). The first form, or the virus form, is a basic group of 1–5 viruses whose only job is to find a host. Like most viruses, once inside the body, they start infecting cells to obtain nutrients and multiply. These viruses still do this, but in a different manner, which will be further expanded on later.

The second form is the Rot Victim, also known as the Rotted form, which occurs when the virus fully infects a victim. In this form, the virus is still inside the body, controlling it, but because of the drastic changes it causes to the person, we classify it as a different form. This is also because this form includes other subforms that may appear if the circumstances are met.

Once the infection takes hold, you can identify an infected victim by looking at their skin, mouth, and eyes. Their skin will develop dark spots, their mouth will move closer to the left side of the face and grow larger, and their eyes will shrink and be pushed toward the right side of the face. There are also 3 key stages of the infection process, as shown in the image.

The way the virus and its victims function is quite odd and poorly understood, and so far only basic information and observations have sufficed.

These things eat by collecting something called rot, which is anything that deforms the body, such as cuts and broken bones. Rot can be collected through physical contact between the virus and/or the Rotted form and a host already infected with rot. Once the collection process starts, these wounds will begin to heal. After the entity is done, poison will be injected into the wanderer, leaving them with only 5 minutes to live. It's also important to note that a Rotted can cut a wanderer and collect that rot, which is why their mouths have many teeth.

How this thing can take rot from people and convert it into a food source is a gray area, to say the least, though it is believed to be similar to plants getting food from the sun. However, this is not confirmed.

What we do know is that this rot collection process is extremely painful for the person undergoing it. We also know that the whole process takes about 30 seconds per source of rot, so if someone has 5 cuts, then it will take about 2 minutes and 30 seconds.

For the sake of clarity, we will now focus only on the rot infection victims, or Rotted, unless specified otherwise.
The Rot Infection.lead:
Oh, you're here. Thirsty for knowledge that you don't need. That you should not know.
You humans are so weird. Why do you wish to know things that should not be known? Information that will keep you up at night, cause you to lose sleep, and eventually lead you to see me.

You will run into me or one of my minions, no doubt. That’s the Backrooms for you, a cruel place where death shows its hand everywhere.

But if you see me,

And I see you,

There will be no escape,

no information that saves you,

No help that will help you,

no muscles that will evade me,

Prey is prey, and I will not let go of that. No matter how far you run, I will be there. If you go to a different level, I will be there. If you go back to the Frontrooms, an apocalypse will start, and it will all be because you thought you could escape.

So sit down and await my arrival. I'm going to catch up eventually, so why bother wasting your stamina? Why spend your last seconds in pain? You know, I'm really a nice guy if you think about it. If you cooperate, maybe I will make it painless.

I have needs, so you can't beg for your life, because I'm famished. I mean, have you ever fasted for a year? I could eat a human every day, but I choose not to. I only kill if I need to.

And you can thank my friend for that, the only logical human I know. In my first stages, the power consumed me. I felt so much of it, and I went on a rampage, killing everyone in sight. I loved the feeling of being powerful. I loved the feeling of having my stomach full after it had hungered so much in my journey through the Backrooms.

Despite all of my power, I was no better than a common hound, blinded by hunger, blinded by power.

As I feasted on humans, a lack of them started to appear. They became few and far between; the first signs of an apocalypse were showing.

And I was too blind to realize my power was killing everyone off,

Then one day, like usual, I spotted a human. I was going to attack and kill him, but he set up a trap that I fell for. At that moment, I knew that my life was in his hands. I was prepared to die.

But he was not like me, no. Instead of killing me, he talked, and I heard him. I considered his words, and as I took a moment, I realized that I finally got a bit of my old self again.

It was beautiful, not because I was good, I'm still far from that, this is not a fairy tale,

It's because I learned that a muscle is no good if there is no thought controlling it,

Now I feast a few and far between because the more I wait, the easier it is to find the next meal.

I gained more power,

And I feel better than ever. I don't even hunger now, that's what my new power gifted me, though I still have to eat.

So you can run all you want,

But we will come face-to-face soon,

no doubt about it,

Just remember, not all stories have a happy ending.

—RoT

(This note was found in Level 4 with a trail of black ink trailing away from it. It's safe to assume that this was probably the RoT. If one has more information about the note, we urge you to come and talk to the M.E.G. Thank you.)

The Poltergeist.title: The Poltergeist

Entity 75.title: Entity 75
Entity 75.description:
Description

Entity 75, also known as "Warding Phantasms" are a species of spectral entities that take the form of siamese fighting fish, with their physical form being comprised of a vibrant flame that can come in a variety of colors. Warding Phantasms are known to inhabit most levels where dangers may be present, such as dangerous entities or enviromental hazards; when encountering a wanderer about to encounter these dangers, the entity, usually in large groups, will swim away from the danger in perfect sync in an attempt to lead the wanderer away from said hazard.

Warding Phantasms also emit a constant mellow light from their bodies, known to further entice wanderers into following its path. This light is reported to have similar effects to a lit candle, illuminating a small area around the entity so it can navigate it's surroundings easier, along with helping wanderers follow it easier through dark areas. Along with light, Warding Phantasm are known known to produce a sound similar to a church bell ringing once it senses danger, alerting a wanderer to its prescence. This sound can only be heard by humans, sapient entities or any non-dangerous entities within a 2 meter range of a Warding Phantasm and instantly activates their flight response, regardless if they are attempting to instigate with the threat the Warding Phantasm is trying to warn them of.

Behaviour

Warding Phantasms are known to act curiously towards any other individuals it encounters, with entities such as Poltergeists being an exception to this rule, to which they will instatly instigate with them regardless of any provocation. Instances of a Warding Phantasm's curious behaviour usually include it pecking the individual, swimming around the individual and attempting to play with them. The reason for Entity 75's random behaviour appears to be the fact it is inept at judging entities and people, always viewing them as a playmate regardless of their previous interactions or level of hostility. Warding Phantasms has also been sighted to chase any entities that they deem hostile and attack them with concentrated spines made of the same fire that composes their body, setting the entity ablaze.

Warding Phantasms, when luring individuals away from danger, are known stop travelling until said individual has caught up with the entity. They may also give multiple visual or auditory clues to their location, such as heating themselves up to produce a short burst of flame, producing the church bell sound en masse and producing a scent similar to sugar. Some instances of Warding Phantasms may also remain by the wanderer to help them navigate dangerous areas, often attacking any hostile entities by the methods mentioned earlier, and providing an opening for wanderers to escape any fatal encounters.

Warding Phantasms usually group into small schools, often consisting of ten members maximum. Most instances usually choose to group up due to their social nature, however some prefer to remain solitary either due to conflict within the group or by their own will. The light emitted by Warding Phantasms usually grows more luminous when they are inside of a school, having around the same lumen as a high intensity spotlight when inside a school of ten members. The group hierarchy is usually decided by the color of the individual entity, with blue being the highest valued while green being the lowest. Being lower in the heirarchy appears to grant the individual entity more positive attention from the higher ranking members, such behaviours include bringing gifts and helping the higher ranked members to lead wanderers to safety.

Biology

Warding Phantasms appear as a spirit like entity resembling a siamese fighting fish, capable of phasing through walls and dispersing their physical form into small butterflies made of fire to remain ambiguous until they are needed, however they prefer to remain in their fish form most of the time.

The physical makeup of Warding Phantasms is constructed from a transparent flame that can appear in multiple different colors and variations, such include a crimson red that fades into black or a light blue that fades into white; with hundreds of variations yet to be found. The flame, despite its appearance; is cold to touch and cannot spread on to entities unless deemed hostile by a Warding Phantasm. The flame is also reported to smell like different fruits depending on the color of the individual Warding Phantasm, with red colored Warding Phantasms smelling of strawberries and rarely pomegranites; while green ones usually smell of apples and lime.

Warding Phantasams are also immortal, with some instances theorised to have lived over millenia. This is mainly due to their existence inside of the spiritual plane, leading to them requiring no food or water; and their immunity to death. While instances of Warding Phantasms chan be sealed within spritual objects, they cannot be permenantly sealed due to their bodies wearing down the object by corrosion.

Discovery

The first documented instance of Warding Phantasms dates to 1899, where a female wanderer working for the A.A.E.G. that managed to find themselves in Level 330 had noticed multicolored specs of light dashing about inside of a notably dark area of the level. Initially shrugging it off until the woman had come face to face with the entity, to which she described the sighting to the Backrooms Investigation Foundation and Backrooms Today to spread the news of her discovery. Later, more reports of Warding Phantasms began to appear within other levels inside of the backrooms, notably Level 188, Level 298 and Level 37.5.

Entity 444.title: Entity 444
Entity 444.description:
How many promises did you think the dead made to each other before dying?

Promises that still exist

Promises that rot, but are still there

What will happen to them?

Do you ask yourself many times what comes after death?

Whatever the answer, I don't think it's what happens after death that matters

It's what you leave behind

Objects, footprints, scars

Memories

You never truly died

They all still remember you

And they say that true death is when you are forgotten completely

How many tears fell because of you?

If you had the choice to spare them from a cycle of grief

Would you take it?

Entity 444:
ANONYMOUS ILLEGITIMATES

Overview
Entity 444, known as Anonymous Illegitimates, abbreviated as AIs or simply Illegitimates are entities that take the form of strictly deceased people, animals, or even other entities. Unlike other similar entities like Entity 10, Illegitimates are passive, and they have no differences in behavior or personality from the entity that they mimic. What they are and how they exist is unknown, but a recent discovery has proven that their formation is tied to two conditions:

*The lifeform it mimics must be deceased
*Said lifeform must have intense, unresolved mourning and grief tied to it, either from one person or multiple people

Anonymous Illegitimates' existence is entirely dependent on the people that grieve over the entity they take the form of, and as a result, Illegitimates will automatically know the location of the people that grieve over them at all times and will naturally form near said people. When an AI forms, the corpse of the entity it mimics is consumed permanently. Only one AI can be formed for each deceased entity, but one person can have multiple Illegitimates if they intensely grieve more than one person.

These entities, despite not behaving any differently from the entities they mimic, possess supernatural properties. They are functionally immortal in almost every scenario, and while they can die of natural causes or be "killed," they will eventually reawaken post-mortem and regenerate all wounds the instant their corpse or wound is not under direct sight for a prolonged period. They will grow and age just as a normal person would (unless they are mimicking another supernatural entity) and can even reproduce with other entities under specific conditions. Additionally, AIs can mimic all deceased lifeforms, even those that died outside of the Backrooms. The only way to identify an Illegitimate is if there is direct confirmation or proof that the person it mimics is deceased. Otherwise, Illegitimates are fully capable of blending into society unbeknownst, and they are associated with detrimental phenomena that they unconsciously bring onto other wanderers.
Anatomy and Behavior
Anonymous Illegitimates have varying behaviors. They have no natural behaviors; instead, they will perfectly copy the personality traits and habits of any lifeform it mimics. Anatomically, they also borrow the exact biology of the lifeform that they occupy. Fundamentally, there is no objective or physical difference between an Illegitimate and the entity it mimics. Even after decades of research, they remain ambiguous to the point that it is not far-fetched to assume they might share the same consciousness or body as the person they copy. The most glaring evidence that helped identify these as individual entities is that they will only take the form of deceased entities, even those that are widely known to be deceased. The origins behind how they form and any ulterior intentions they may have remain unknown to this day.

Illegitimates can be fully compatible with stable reproduction as long as the entity they mimic has the same species as the other entity or is capable of asexual reproduction. However, this is severely discouraged due to the severe mental hazards associated with AIs and a high risk of the offspring being exposed to said mental hazards.

However, perhaps the most notable thing about their behavior is that they aren't hostile or use their flawless impersonations to prey on wanderers. Illegitimates genuinely believe that they are the person that they mimic, even if they aren't. Attempting to contain or persecute them is mostly futile, as they will react just as a normal person would and are even vulnerable to the Roth Syndrome. Despite this, it is still dangerous to be in constant exposure to Illegitimates, as they are known to be the root of a phenomenon known as dulling.

Anonymous Illegitimates, as explained above, are immortal. They can die temporarily, but when their corpse is left unwatched for substantial time periods, they will eventually reincarnate and look just as they did before dying. There are only 2 known ways of fully erasing or killing AIs:

*If any consciousness completely forgets the person or entity they mimic, Illegitimates will gradually disappear permanently. This can happen if the person grieving the entity that an Illegitimate impersonates by any means forgets said entity, or if that person dies. In the case that the person remembering the entity dies, if they had a close bond, the Illegitimate will mourn the person before then disappearing. However, Illegitimates won't die if they are still intensely grieved by any other wanderers.
*If the person grieving over the entity the Illegitimate mimics accepts that said entity is gone forever and moves on, the Illegitimate will disappear permanently. This is notoriously difficult, as the constant presence of an Illegitimate is known to make it harder for wanderers to move on and even cause said wanderers to intentionally engage with the Illegitimate regardless of it not truly being the person they grieve. This is usually only achieved through therapy or rehabilitation but is still difficult due to the Dulling phenomenon.
Anomalies
Anonymous Illegitimates on their own are already suspected to be the result of severe anomalies.
They are capable of perfectly copying any living creature down to every exact detail, much unlike other shapeshifting entities like skin-stealers that can mimic appearances, memories, and personalities but will still possess flaws tied to their fundamental anatomy or visual appearance.
Entity 444.lead:
How many promises did you think the dead made to each other before dying?

Promises that still exist

Promises that rot, but are still there

What will happen to them?

Do you ask yourself many times what comes after death?

Whatever the answer, I don't think it's what happens after death that matters

It's what you leave behind

Objects, footprints, scars

Memories

You never truly died

They all still remember you

And they say that true death is when you are forgotten completely

How many tears fell because of you?

If you had the choice to spare them from a cycle of grief

Would you take it?

Entity 444:
ANONYMOUS ILLEGITIMATES

Overview
Entity 444, known as Anonymous Illegitimates, abbreviated as AIs or simply Illegitimates are entities that take the form of strictly deceased people, animals, or even other entities. Unlike other similar entities like Entity 10, Illegitimates are passive, and they have no differences in behavior or personality from the entity that they mimic. What they are and how they exist is unknown, but a recent discovery has proven that their formation is tied to two conditions:

*The lifeform it mimics must be deceased
*Said lifeform must have intense, unresolved mourning and grief tied to it, either from one person or multiple people

Anonymous Illegitimates' existence is entirely dependent on the people that grieve over the entity they take the form of, and as a result, Illegitimates will automatically know the location of the people that grieve over them at all times and will naturally form near said people. When an AI forms, the corpse of the entity it mimics is consumed permanently. Entity 87.title: Entity 87
Entity 87.description:
Entity 87, commonly referred to as "backbugs," or by the scientific name Imitatae is an order of arachnids that rely on the backrooms for their life cycle and biological functions. Backbugs are often mistaken for other organisms due to anatomical differences, with each grouped into 5 families based on survival strategy and anatomy: Insidious Vermis, Imitate Parasitus, Insecta Celens, Venator Inaturalist, and Edentes Liminales.

At agricultural levels, they are considered major pests and invasive species due to their more advanced adaptations relative to local predators. Backbugs are omnivores and decomposers and are most commonly found in entity-dense ecosystems; as such, they're considered by wanderers a sign of caution. Recently, health organizations such as the Backrooms Red Cross discovered many new enzymes within backbugs, which aid digestion and alleviate allergies.

Entity 4.title: Entity 4
Entity 4.description:
Entity 4:DeathmothsGigas Heterocera / The Cold-blooded Hunter

Description

Entity 4, also known as the Deathmoths, are entities that inhabit the great majority of The Backrooms' levels, resembling Frontrooms moths.

Deathmoths are truly colossal creatures, boasting a wingspan that measures approximately 1.5 meters. The configuration of their wings is highly irregular, presenting a jagged appearance along the edges. The surface of these wings is enveloped by a thick layer of gray hair, which is not only coarse but also exceptionally hard.

The head of Deathmoths is comparatively diminutive and stands in stark disproportion to its substantial body. Positioned at the extremity of the head are a pair of protruding compound eyes, each composed of thousands of minute eye surfaces that exhibit a profound black coloration. Situated between the compound eyes is a sharp and slender mouthpart, resembling a syringe in shape, and possesses the capability to effortlessly puncture the skin of its prey.

Deathmoths possesses a broad and robust chest that provides crucial support for its massive wings and weighty body. The surface of the thorax is adorned with a hard carapace, featuring a dark brown hue and marked by irregular textures and spots. Its abdomen is round and hypertrophied, segmented in nature. At the junctions between each segment, there exists a thin membrane, endowing the abdomen with a degree of flexibility during movement.

The surface of the abdomen is also covered with gray hairs, though they are more fine and supple in comparison to the hairs on the wings. At the extremity of the abdomen are a pair of slender tail whiskers, the tips of which are as sharp as needles. Its legs are short and stocky, featuring sharp spikes at the joints. The surface of the legs is enveloped by a hard cuticle, which is capable of furnishing adequate support and protection during walking and climbing activities.

Deathmoths hunt for energy and nutrition, which is the main reason they prey humans. M.E.G. scientists have also observed that Deathmoths hunt Facelings, Skin-Stealers, and Dullers for food.

As with many other species, Deathmoths have two genders - male and female. The females are the main hunters, neutralizing targets directly by brute force. Males, on the other hand, are typically smaller, around 3-5 times the size of a regular Frontrooms moth, and are passive unless provoked.

Behavior

Deathmoths are clearly divided into females and males, and this point has already been mentioned before. During our in-depth study and observation, we discovered that there exists a considerable difference in the behavior of male and female deathmoths. Due to this significant disparity, for the sake of providing a more comprehensive and detailed understanding, we will proceed to introduce them separately.

Male

Almond water possesses an exceptionally strong attraction for male death moths. Precisely because of this compelling allure, wanderers can ingeniously utilize almond water as a means to tame male death moths. Once male Deathmoths are successfully tamed, they will prove to be of considerable assistance to wanderers by helping to fend off various entities and locate specific items that are of crucial importance. Nevertheless, it is of paramount importance that the wanderer should replenish the tamed male Deathmoth with Almond Water in a timely manner. Failure to do so would potentially result in a dire situation where they would turn against the wanderer, causing unforeseen and perhaps perilous consequences.

The wings of male Deathmoths vibrate at a significantly higher frequency. This frequent wing vibration causes them to produce a distinct buzzing sound when they are in flight. Such a buzzing sound acts as an effective warning signal to potential enemies, alerting them to keep their distance. At the same time, when confronted with a threatening situation, they have the ability to release a special type of gas. This unique gas possesses properties that can intimidate and scare off enemies, providing them with an additional means of self-defense. Male deathmoths are equipped with eyes that are more sensitive to light. This heightened light sensitivity enables them to detect potential threats in their surroundings more rapidly, granting them a crucial advantage in avoiding danger and ensuring their survival.

Finally, upon encountering a female Deathmoth, the male Deathmoth will embark on a display of a series of elaborate and complex dance moves with the aim of initiating courtship. This elaborate dance routine is a carefully choreographed sequence of movements and postures, specifically designed to attract the attention and interest of the female counterpart and to convey the male's desirability and suitability as a potential mate.

Female

Female deathmoths secrete a particular substance known as moth jelly subsequent to the process of fertilization. This substance was initially formulated with the purpose of attracting male deathmoths. Male deathmoths, upon consuming the moth jelly, would become completely submissive to the female deathmoth. However, interestingly, it turns out to be an outstanding source of nutrition for wanderers. Not only that, but it can even have the effect of enhancing and improving one's mood. Further detailed and comprehensive information can be found here.

When threatened, female Deathmoths will remain stationary, adopting a strategy of immobility as their defense mechanism. They rely on this behavior to blend in seamlessly with their surroundings, becoming almost indistinguishable from the environment. In this way, they can effectively avoid the watchful eyes and predatory instincts of their numerous predators. Their stillness is a calculated move, a silent attempt to disappear into the background and escape detection.

Similarly, when they forage for food, they will also stay still. This cautious approach is employed to avoid being spotted by their prey. The female Deathmoths understand the importance of stealth and patience in the hunt for sustenance. By remaining motionless, they wait for the perfect moment to strike, minimizing the risk of alerting their potential food sources. But as humans, it's hard not to spot the huge presence of female death moths, even if they don't move.

Biology

Through an in-depth analysis of the DNA of deathmoths, M.E.G. geneticists discovered that in its genetic sequence, a large number of repeating series and non-coding regions were discovered, which hinted at the intricate regulatory mechanisms at play. These repeating elements seem to form a complex network of genetic signals, perhaps serving as a reservoir of potential evolutionary changes or playing a role in fine-tuning the expression of key genes.

Among the genetic wonders found within the dead moth's DNA are proteins that are highly sensitive to specific wavelengths of light. This unique characteristic endows the deathmoths with a remarkable ability to perceive and respond to their environment. However, rather than being drawn to bright lights as one might expect, these moths seem to have an affinity for the shadows. They are inclined to hide in the dark, perhaps as a survival strategy.

Deathmoths possess a unique respiratory system that features air sacs, a characteristic reminiscent of birds. These air sacs play a crucial role in meeting the extremely high oxygen demands of the death moths during flight and various activities. The efficient respiratory system enables them to maintain a high level of energy expenditure and perform complex maneuvers in the air.

It is a known fact that deathmoths are capable of devouring a diverse range of different entities. This includes not only traditional food sources but also rather unconventional ones such as Windows and Predatory Doors. Such an eclectic diet suggests that their digestive system is highly adaptable and capable of breaking down foods of varying hardness and composition. The digestive process likely involves a complex interplay of enzymes and physiological mechanisms that can handle the diverse materials ingested by the death moths.

Regarding their nervous system, it is highly developed, indicating a remarkable ability to sense and respond to their surroundings. However, despite this advanced nervous system, it appears that their brains are relatively immature. This dichotomy results in a unique set of characteristics. Death moths are extremely sensitive to changes in their environment, able to detect even the slightest disturbances. Yet, their low level of intelligence limits their cognitive abilities and decision-making processes. This combination of sensitivity and limited intelligence makes their behavior unpredictable.

Habitat

Theories

Discovery
The Lost, an organization that is thousands of years old or even older, possesses an ancient book. In this book, there are descriptions of creatures known as "The Flies of Terror." These creatures resemble giant moths and are known to hunt people. However, we cannot be entirely certain that this particular creature must be a death moth. There could be other possible explanations or identities for this menacing entity.

The official record of the actual Deathmoth dates back to 1891. On that fateful day, Agents Christina and Hertz were on duty in Level 1. While carrying out their routine patrols, they suddenly encountered an unknown giant creature. At first glance, the two agents assumed that this creature might be friendly. However, their initial perception was quickly proven wrong as the giant creature launched a sudden attack on Agent Christina without warning. As a result of this unanticipated assault, Agent Christina was severely wounded. In the face of this dire situation, Agent Hertz bravely stepped forward to resist. Displaying remarkable courage and resourcefulness, he engaged in a fierce battle with the menacing creature. After a long and arduous struggle, Agent Hertz finally managed to defeat the giant creature by using a dagger.

Without hesitation, he then quickly assisted Agent Christina and transported the creature's corpse back to the New London base. Agent Christina was resuscitated and eventually regained her vital signs. After extensive research and in-depth analysis by the scientists of the M.E.G., it was conclusively determined that this was a completely new and hitherto unknown creature. Thus, it was given the name "Deathmoths".

Footnotes

Entity 1.title: Entity 1
Entity 1.description:
Notice:

This document is pending revision following a recent alteration in the expected behavior of this phenomenon. A thorough review is scheduled to be held by the Kauer Board of Directors to ensure that the updated understanding is accurately reflected.

The Cameraman is a theoretical entity presumed to be responsible for capturing most of the currently known images of the Backrooms. The process by which these photos are captured is unknown, and evidence of The Cameraman has yet to be proven; however, its existence could serve as an explanation for the mysterious phenomenon of images appearing in level entries instantaneously after their submission into the Database. These files often have no attribution or provided context, and have even appeared in level documents when the explorers responsible for the article did not report carrying photography equipment with them. Some speculate that The Cameraman is not a single entity, but rather a collective of entities capturing such images. This theory may be supported by their vastly different manners of photography, implying that they were not taken by one being.

Furthermore, the quality of many of these anonymous photos indicates that they were not taken from a cellphone or other common device. Rather, the majority are high-quality and appear as if captured with a professional camera apparatus that a typical wanderer would not possess. If a wanderer did have camera equipment of this nature, it would be unreasonable to surmise that such an individual could manage to travel from level to level carrying the equipment alone. Many potential explanations have been provided to explain the source of this phenomenon, though, to date, none have been able to provide a conclusive answer. From this, The Cameraman is theorized. This entity, for unknown reasons and by means yet to be understood, is thought to travel throughout the Backrooms capturing images of various levels. Many images in the Database lack attribution to a specific photographer or an explanation as to how they were taken, and it is these entries that are attributed to The Cameraman, acting as their silent curator.

The physical characteristics of the entity remain mysterious, as it is believed to either avoid being seen or does not have a physical form altogether. The only evidence pertaining to the existence of The Cameraman includes unproven reports of leftover film rolls and undeveloped photographs being discovered in certain levels, supposedly near where their Database images were taken. As it stands, it has been agreed to not make attempts to hinder or capture this entity, as although its methods and intentions are unknown, it serves as a general asset to the Database.

----

Hydrolitis Plague.title: Hydrolitis Plague
Hydrolitis Plague.description:
Ring a-ring o' roses, A pocket full of posies,Ashes, ashes, We all fall down.

DescriptionEntity 11, also known as Hydrolitis Plague, simply referred to as Hydrolitis, is a type of bacterium. It serves as the pathogen responsible for Hydromonia, a highly virulent infectious disease. In its natural environment, it lurks in untreated water bodies at specific aquatic levels and can spread through direct skin-to-skin contact. Depending on the location of the lesion, Hydromonia is generally classified into several types, such as pulmonary Hydromonia and septicaemic Hydromonia.

Pulmonary Hydromonia mainly exhibits severe lung lesions. Patients typically experience coughing symptoms, which are often intense and frequent, and in severe cases, may be accompanied by hemoptysis. Meanwhile, breathing difficulties are also common, with patients feeling short of breath and experiencing aggravated shortness of breath upon physical activity. Even at rest, significant respiratory distress can occur, leading to a decrease in blood oxygen saturation and tissue hypoxia. The disease progresses extremely rapidly. Within a short period, the inflammatory range in the lung tissue continuously expands, and the alveoli are damaged, severely affecting the gas exchange function of the respiratory system and subsequently triggering a series of complications related to respiratory failure, posing a serious threat to the patient's life and health.

The main characteristic of Septicaemic Hydromonia is the massive reproduction of bacteria in the bloodstream. Once the disease occurs, the patient's body temperature rises sharply, with persistent high fever reaching 40℃ or even higher, accompanied by chills and shivering. As the disease progresses, the bacteria and their toxins circulating in the bloodstream trigger systemic infection symptoms, leading to vasodilation, a decrease in blood pressure, and subsequent shock. The patient may experience confusion and coma. This systemic infection causes severe and extensive damage to multiple organ systems of the body, including vital organs such as the heart, liver, and kidneys, which can lead to organ failure. It has an extremely high mortality rate and is an extremely dangerous type of infectious disease.

In-depth research and effective prevention and control of Hydrolitis Plague and the resulting Hydromonia are essential. We are studying the bacterium in depth and believe it will contribute to a deeper understanding of the biology, transmission and pathogenesis of the pathogen, providing a solid scientific basis for the development of targeted diagnostics, therapeutics and preventive vaccines.

Hydrolysis Plague exhibits a diverse range of transmission modalities. It possesses an extremely high level of biological contagiousness and has the characteristic of attaching to all animal or solid cells, thereby significantly enhancing its infection rate. In the case of individuals infected with Hydrolysis Plague, without the administration of appropriate medical treatment, fatality is an inescapable outcome. Nevertheless, if early and proper treatment is provided, the mortality rate can be decreased to approximately 80%.

Hydrolitis Plague exhibits an extremely high degree of instability and is prone to frequent mutations. Through extensive scientific research and surveillance, it has been conclusively established and is a matter of significant concern that certain mutated forms of the Hydrolitis Plague have acquired the ability to survive in water sources that have been subjected to only crude chemical sterilization procedures. This situation poses a serious threat to public health and water safety, as it implies that traditional and relatively simple chemical sterilization methods may not be sufficient to completely eradicate the risk of Hydrolitis Plague contamination in water supplies, potentially leading to widespread outbreaks and severe consequences for affected populations.

Subject #1: Level 37
Multiple M.E.G. locations have reported the discovery of Hydrolitis Plague bacteria in filtered water. Upon investigation, we discovered that the water sources for these colonies all came from Level 37. In order to delve deeper into this anomaly and the harm it can pose, we conducted this study.
We first took multiple samples of filtered water in Level 37 and brought them back to the specialized lab. In the laboratory, high-precision microbial detection equipment is used to isolate, purify and culture samples, and advanced gene sequencing technology is used to analyze the genetic structure of the bacterium to determine its species and characteristics, and then to study its survival mechanism in filtered water.Surprisingly, it was found that this plague has the ability to survive in filtered water. This discovery implies that our drinking water safety will be severely threatened in the future. After a detailed analysis, it was revealed that the Hydrolitis Plague has mutated into an extreme bioaerogel form. In this state, pathogens can penetrate water source filters through an adaptive shell, thereby evading the sterilization effect of the filters.Please remain calm. Since we have understood the principle, we are confident that a solution will be found in the near future. Until then, it is extremely necessary to carry out strict monitoring of all water sources, so as to detect potential risks in a timely manner and take corresponding measures to minimize the harm and impact that this plague may bring.Note: The image on the right is the microscoped mutated Hydrolitis Plague.

Phases
The phases of infection with this bacterium can be broadly divided into three phases.
Phase I:Acute Symptom Progression Phase
The Acute Symptom Progression Phase is a crucial stage in the disease's development. It commences approximately 4 hours subsequent to the onset of the first symptoms and concludes after a period of around two days. During this phase, in addition to the symptoms that have been previously noted, individuals may encounter a diminution or absence of the senses of taste and smell. Skin discoloration may manifest, which could be an indication of underlying physiological alterations. Respiratory distress in the form of shortness of breath might be present, along with mental confusion and ocular irritation. Notably, hallucinatory experiences, often with thematic elements associated with the concepts of Backrooms or Frontrooms, can also be a part of this complex symptomatology. This phase requires close monitoring and appropriate medical intervention to manage the diverse and potentially serious manifestations.
Phase II:Critical Decompensation Stage
The Critical Decompensation Stage represents a significant deterioration in the patient's condition. Two days post-onset, in the absence of proper medical treatment, the persistent vomiting can precipitate a state of heavy dehydration, which in turn can have a cascading effect on multiple physiological systems. Respiratory function is severely compromised, rendering breathing an arduous and often labored task. Episodes of internal bleeding pose a serious threat, as they can lead to rapid blood loss and shock. The patient may experience profound delirium, indicative of the impact on the central nervous system. Concurrently, the bacterium initiates an aggressive assault on diverse anatomical regions, such as the brain, compromising neurological function; the lungs, impeding gas exchange; the limbs, potentially causing tissue necrosis; and the vital organs like the kidneys and liver, disrupting their essential metabolic and detoxification processes. Immediate and intensive medical intervention is imperative to arrest the progression and mitigate the potentially fatal consequences.
Phase III:Terminal Lethal Phase
The Terminal Lethal Phase marks the gravest and final stage of the illness. As the disease progresses, a significant escalation in cell death within the tissue is observed, which accelerates the onset of necrosis at an alarming rate. The internal bleeding not only persists but also exacerbates, spreading to multiple organs and severely disrupting their normal physiological functions. At this juncture, the patient's condition is irreversible, and medical intervention is rendered ineffective. The formation of blood clots around the veins and arteries is a common occurrence, giving rise to thrombosis. This thrombotic condition poses a substantial risk as it may progress to various heart diseases, further compromising the already failing cardiovascular system. In the most extreme and unfortunate circumstances, the Hydrolitis Plague invariably leads to the patient's demise, emphasizing the highly lethal nature of this terminal stage.

Survival GuidesThe Backrooms Association of the Red Cross comprises experts in pathogens. Consequently, we have entrusted them with the task of formulating one or two survival guides. The remaining portion of the survival guide is currently being authored by The Backrooms Association of the Red Cross.
Survival Guide #1:

Stay away from water!
It is of utmost importance to maintain a safe distance from all types of water bodies. Unless it is an absolute necessity, refrain from setting foot in aquatic levels such as Level 37. As is widely known, these levels are prime breeding grounds for the Hydrolitis Plague. Once accidentally entered, the risk of infection is extremely high.
However, in the event that due to an emergency situation, you have no choice but to enter such levels, there is no need to worry unduly! Our outposts can provide you with high-quality protective gear. This protective clothing is developed using advanced technology and can offer excellent protection against the vast majority of pathogens in the Backrooms, be it tiny and elusive bacteria, cunning and variable viruses, stealthy fungi, stubborn parasites, and even certain dangerous bioweapons, ensuring a safe exploration journey for you!
It should be particularly noted that although some M.E.G. outposts also sell protective equipment, most of them are simple protective suits with uneven quality and can only deal with certain dangerous situations. It is recommended that you prioritize our professional protective clothing to obtain more comprehensive and reliable protection.
If you find yourself already in one of these dangerous levels, do not panic! Immediately seek the nearest medical outpost for a comprehensive health check after a safe evacuation. Our medical outposts are fully equipped with facilities and sufficient medicines, and the professional medical team is highly experienced. Groups such as A.E.G. and M.E.G. will also go all out to assist, ensuring that your health condition is properly evaluated and handled.

- The Backrooms Association of the Red Cross

Your health, Our wish.

Survival Guide #2:

Drink almond water regularly!
In light of the current health concerns, it is highly recommended that one consume a copious amount of almond water！Scientific research and field observations have indicated that this particular beverage exhibits remarkable prophylactic properties against the dreaded Hydrolitis Plague.

In the event that an individual has unfortunately been exposed to and infected by the noxious bacteria associated with this plague, the regular and ample intake of almond water assumes an even more crucial role. It has been empirically demonstrated that almond water can effectively decelerate the progress of the bacterial infection, affording the body's natural defenses an enhanced opportunity to mount a counteroffensive. Moreover, albeit with a rather slender probability, there have been documented instances where almond water has seemingly facilitated a complete recovery from the infection. Hence, it is not entirely unfounded or extravagant to assert that the somewhat hyperbolic adage "almond water cures all diseases" which has gained currency among the community of wanderers, might actually possess a kernel of truth.

- The Backrooms Association of the Red Cross

Your health, Our wish.

Original Author: OGominho
Rewritten by EveryBox

Object 6.title: Object 6
Object 6.description:
Moth Jelly is a nutritional, jelly-like substance produced by Death Moths to feed their young and maintain their hives. It typically takes on a violet coloration and has a similar consistency to honey although thinner. Moth Jelly produced by female Deathmoths has trace amounts of strong pheromones that attract male Deathmoths and cause them to enter an entranced state when consumed. This makes the male Deathmoth addicted to it and making them completely subservient to a particular hive's Queen and willing to do whatever the Queen wishes in exchange for more.

The main source of Moth Jelly is of course, female Deathmoths. The highly aggressive nature of female Death Moths makes trying to obtain Moth Jelly a dangerous and life-threatening endeavor, but the nutritious properties of the jelly makes it a reward that possibly outweighs the risk.

Moth Jelly can be found in any level with a high population of Deathmoths. Although rare, Moth Jelly packaged in bottles and jars also appear all throughout the Backrooms even where Deathmoths are not present. These are often labeled with a sticker reading "Property of Royal Jelly Farms™" accompanied by a logo of a purple crown.
Object 6.lead: Moth Jelly, also known as Queen Moth Jelly or Royal Jelly is a substance found in the Backrooms.

Ye (Joke).title: Ye (Joke)
Ye (Joke).description:
, inside Room 1342.]]Kanye West — also known as Yeezy, or maybe just Ye — is the single native entity present in the Yerooms. He appears to be similar to a human, with similar features as those found in the Backrooms. Although described as an entity, he does not present aggressive behavior, instead representing himself as a helpful being. Ye cannot harm other living beings, and cannot be intentionally harmed.

He is the (self-proclaimed) divinity that reigns all the territory present inside the Yerooms, a dimension all designed, produced and engineered by the genius himself, Ye. He happens to occasionally change his appearance when transporting from one Yevel to another, often wearing different types of masks covering his full face.

Ye speaks English fluently, though older reports affirm that the being was also able to vaguely communicate in Chinese.

Spoggors (Joke).title: Spoggors (Joke)
Spoggors (Joke).description: Entity 67-Z — otherwise known as Spoggors — are an especially strange derivation of Entity 67. The exact evolutionary process that birthed this subspecies is unknown. What is known, however, is that these entities are remarkably dangerous — posing a significant threat to all surrounding lifeforms. Local communities have issued warnings in areas where Spoggors are prevalent.

Entity 5.title: Entity 5
Entity 5.description:
Clumps are hostile entities that resemble bulky, massive clumps of human limbs that flail about and extend in every direction. They are known to inhabit vents, cabinets, and other tight spaces. These entities are infamous for their uncanny level of intelligence when it comes to ambushing and preying on Wanderers.

Clumps spend most of their time tucked away in tight spaces like closets, drains, pipes, chimneys, and most commonly, vents. Generally, the only part of a Clump that is seen by a Wanderer are their frontal arms, which they allow to hang freely and dangle from within their hunting space, a behavior theorized to serve as a form of "bait" to get Wanderers to approach them. While occasionally an arm will flex or a hand will clench, these arms are generally immobile when no Wanderers are present.

Entity 62.title: Entity 62
Entity 62.description:
Aranea Membri are large spider-like entities, attaining a quadrupedal stance with several sharp, featureless limbs. Aranea Membri bear faces resembling what appears to be some sort of carnivoran skull. Although their hostility and extreme speed make them difficult to observe closely, current estimates and autopsies put their average height at around seven feet 8 (21 decimetres) and their length at around eight feet (24 decimetres). The entity’s black skin appears to be made of a solidified version of Niralola Black, allowing them to melt or disappear into dark areas. In addition, as the entity walks, it makes a loud metallic clanking sound. Although the origin of this is unknown, it is extremely helpful for wanderers seeking to avoid these entities.

These entities are highly predatory and are known to pursue and attack humans on sight. As such, they should be avoided whenever possible.
Entity 62.lead: Aranea Membri, also known as Spider Legs, are a hostile entity inhabiting the Backrooms. They are distinguished by oftentimes taking residence in the ceilings of levels they inhabit, utilizing it as a method of ambushing prey.

Glowfolk.title: Glowfolk
Glowfolk.description:
Glowfolk, sometimes called will o’ the wisp or jinn, are somewhat humanoid entities which vary in stature and appearance. Glowfolk appear to possess two glowing white eyes and (occasionally) a mouth which shares similar attributes. While adolescent Glowfolk are incapable of communication, fully-developed Glowfolk are capable of both understanding human speech and holding a conversation. These creatures do not start out intelligent. Rather, they learn from their environment and are shaped accordingly. Their native level has not been recorded yet, but they have been reported to appear in a number of other levels.

Glowfolk tend to be physiologically androgynous, but are known to experiment with both masculine and feminine body types. They may be found in a variety of colors, with some bearing multiple colors. Being made up entirely of neon flames, their skin is – for the most part – impregnable. As such, Glowfolk manipulate the flames of their silhouette to mimic hair or other physical features, which are not inherent aspects of their forms. This manipulation is not entirely perfect, as the flames which make up their bodies are constantly in flux, subtly shifting from the desired appearance. One may observe the flames surrounding a glowfolk flickering.

Entity 37.title: Entity 37
Entity 37.description:
The Devil’s Ambrosia, also known as Black Ambrosia, Shadow Lilies, or the Flower from Hell, is an invasive species of edible flower that resemble lilies with a black coloration and multiple orange anthems sprouting from the center. Said anthems produce a pitch-black, sweet-smelling & tasting nectar.

The Devil’s Ambrosia is in nearly any level with high levels of vegetation and flora, such as forests, jungles, gardens, farms, greenhouses, and other places where its presence is unintended due to its nature as an invasive species. It can even be found in pots and in bouquets in urban-level's flower shops and other stores.
Entity 37.lead:
The Devil’s Ambrosia is a parasitic species of edible flower found in the Backrooms.

Devil’s Ambrosia.

Entity 2.title: Entity 2
Entity 2.description:
Predatory Windows are the most common types of Windows. They typically occupy indoor areas of levels, though they can be infrequently seen lurking within the outside regions.

Innocuous Windows are the second types of Windows, and the direct antithesis to the Predatory Windows.
Entity 2.lead:
As you wander through the bowels of Level 3, you reminisce on your time within the Frontrooms.

You remember the feeling of wind on your skin, the freshness of the air, the chirping of birds in the sky. Just mere days ago, your life had been like that.

And now here you are. Starving. Alone. In the darkness.

...

You see a faint glow in the distance.

You slowly approach it, almost as if the window itself was beckoning you to come closer.

"Is that... light? It's been so long since I've seen the sky."

And just as quickly, the sight is gone, a hollow void standing in its place.

Only then do you see the beast behind its facade.

A single eye opens inside the void. Numerous appendages emerge from the window, grabbing hold of your arms and dragging you into it.

    Entity 2: Windows

Entity 2, colloquially referred to as "The Windows" are sentient entities which inhabit the great majority of The Backrooms' levels.

Windows are split up into two groups: Predatory Windows and Innocuous Windows.

 |-| Predatory Windows=

Entity 7.title: Entity 7
Entity 7.description:
Entity 7, otherwise known as the "Mandela Virus", is a pathogen commonly found inside Level 1 and Level 2. Discoveries of the Mandela Virus in other levels had been made before; however, they are mostly obscure and unconfirmed. The Mandela Virus typically inhabits dark areas possessing low temperatures, in addition to expired bottles of Almond Water. It is resistant to acids and Liquid Pain; however, temperatures lower than 40 degrees Celsius can cause significant damage to the pathogen.

The Mandela Virus mainly transmits via contact with the skin, regardless of if it belongs to a wanderer or an entity. It possesses a retractable spike to puncture the skin, even piercing tougher exteriors. After transmission, the virus will then make its way to the blood vessels to further infect the circulatory system. Contact with any carriers of the the Mandela Virus additionally transmits the disease, thus creating the need for all victims to be immediately quarantined.

Symptoms of the the Mandela Virus are consistent within each victim, but slightly deviate when it comes to when the symptoms will show. It is assumed that the mutation of the virus seems to have halted; the disease has reached its figurative "endpoint" in evolution, its final product. A characteristic supporting this theory is that the the Mandela Virus attacks every single organism excluding its own. It is the only cure for the Hound Virus and many other incurable diseases present in the Backrooms, albeit its inherent danger.
Entity 7.lead:
The Meaning of 7"As the virus leaves the body, organs start to decay. There's no point of return; you are trapped inside yourself to feel the pain of the decomposition of your own body. Cremation and disappearance is your only way out; if not, you are doomed to experience the agonizing pain, loneliness, and the fear of being forgotten forever inside a meaningless, empty coffin. God saves you when Hell arrives at your place inside the crematorium chamber for you to become dust, a speck in the never-ending torii gates of Hell itself. You cannot escape; death cannot help you, ."
Language Notice: Parts of this page has been written in broken Russian.

СОБСТВЕННОСТЬ ОБЪЕДИНЕННОЙ СОЦИАЛИСТИЧЕСКОЙ СОВЕТСКОЙ РЕСПУБЛИКИ
ВСЕСОЮЗНОЕ ОРДЕНА КРАСНОГО ЗНАМЕНИ ДОБРОВОЛЬНОЕ ОБЩЕСТВО
ПОМОЩЬ ТОЛЬКО ОФИЦИАЛЬНЫМ И ВЫСОКОКЛАССНЫМ ЛИЦАМ
( Академия наук СССР )
СТАЛКИВАТЬСЯ 91
