Добро пожаловать товарищ!
ENCOUNTER 1: Nikolay Rafail

For: Leonid Brezhnev

From: Nikolay Rafail, Head of the AH CCCP

Date: 4th October, 1979

Location: [REDACTED]

    Several bodies of what appear to be ordinary people, most of them of Mongolian and Siberian descent had been found inside the PV-1. Autopsy reports of one of the bodies had shown these bodies were in that same position for a decade, yet no signs of any decomposition nor bacteria had been discovered. The respiratory, digestion, muscle, and nervous systems remain intact, however, the heart and the blood vessels; they seem, they seem to have been significantly destroyed. Samples of blood and the heart had been taken out for further research, we have to keep the body in a place where the process of decomposition would not happen.

    7/10/1979, samples of the blood had been tested and confirmed to possess an unknown disease. It does not seem to have an apparent similarity to other known diseases, we might have something that could be useful for defense or offense against the United States. We request an order for about 25 test monkeys to find out about its symptoms, and what happens to the infected victims. So far, we know it only attacks the circulatory system, and not other organ systems in the body. Samples of the disease are kept inside the secret chamber following your orders. The virus also attacks amoeba cells; for now, we will be using those before further testing mammals.

    14/10/1979, the symptoms seem to remain consistent on every test we did. The following are the symptoms, starting from the injection of the disease:

*20-60 minutes in, subject shows a sign of tiredness and sleepiness;
*1-6 hours in, a state of paralysis begins;
*6-8 hours in, the disappearance of the paralysis, a sudden gain of energy, a state of robustness;
*8-11 hours in, excessive urination of a reddish-liquid, presumably a mix of urine and blood;
*11-21 hours in, dizziness and fatigue, in addition to chest pain;
*21-35 hours in, death of subject due to heart failure and decomposition of blood vessels starts.

> OPEN CLASSIFIED FILE: PV-A 31/07/81

Entity 45.title: Entity 45
Entity 45.description:
Ravager Apes can grow upwards of 4 meters tall and weigh up to 500 kilograms. Ravager Apes are largely coated in black fur and have similar body proportions to an orangutan. Some have described the black fur of the entity to be rough sharp to the touch, many comparing it to the feeling of steel wool. Additionally, their skin has been stated to feel like fine sandpaper, brittle but not fully rigid. Their face is quite different from that of other apes however, and bear an uncanny resemblance to human faces, as they tend to have some form of facial hair and downward pointing noses. Ravager Apes are strictly carnivorous and have a taste for human flesh especially. Whenever a Ravager Ape picks up the scent of a human, it will emit a howl strong enough to damage human eardrums in proximity.

This wild shriek will summon other Ravager Apes to its location, so that they may work together to track down where the scent is coming from. When they finally find the human, Ravager Apes will reveal a series of dagger-like teeth which they will use to tear pieces of flesh from the human's body and eat it right in front of their prey.
Entity 45.lead: Ravager Apes are a species of excessively cruel and violent Hominidae, with their physical makeup similar to traditional depictions of Sasquatch (Frontooms Myth). They are infamous for their gargantuan proportions and savage rule over jungle levels, as well as their invasive behaviors regarding traversing through the Backrooms.

The Narrator.title: The Narrator
The Narrator.displayTitle: Words, Phrases, Clauses, Sentences, Paragraphs... The story never ends.
The Narrator.description:
Lurking within your teeny tiny mind
just a teeny tiny man
writing teeny tiny scenes
in a teeny tiny world
The Narrator, also called "The Author", is a mysterious and powerful being. Its alleged power transcends and is superior to all other entities within the Backrooms and, theoretically, other realms as well. Little to no information is known about this mysterious creature. But, as time goes on, more and more information has been discovered, although the source of this information is still unknown. Many believe this being is purposely slowly revealing information about him to the people in the Backrooms for reasons unknown, as there have been many logs suggesting that is the case. But did this being provide us with this knowledge on purpose? Was this our discovery, or its discovery?
Maybe I'm a regular man or a parasite, intelligent cat, or dog. Maybe I'm one of the creatures of liminality; maybe I'm nothing at all.
The Narrator allegedly can change and shift time and space completely, and presumably can even change the general trajectory of a universe. It can manipulate a person's mind, including their decisions and thoughts. It can control an individual's life and death. It is still unknown how a being could obtain such abilities and what The Narrator plans to do with these powers. But is it making us wonder? Is it making us question its motives? Is it still toying with us right now?
So what if my peers shunted me down? So what if I abandoned my home? It is all for the greater good.
The Narrator's appearance is unknown, but there have been many theories regarding this topic. One theory states that a man gained access to immense power, which he then started to abuse, becoming uncontrollable and untouchable. Some say it is a bunch of neurological entities that exist inside beings' minds. Some say it is a creature that exists in other dimensions that we can't comprehend. Some say it is the human mind itself. Most say it doesn't exist.
And those who discover this can do nothing about it. A front-row seat ticket to a collapsing world.
The strange thing is that there is a possibility that none of these theories were created by us.
I wouldn't be disappointed if you screamed, or if you ran and told someone. Maybe I'm controlling you to scream and panic, or maybe not.
According to outsider information, The Narrator, as its nickname suggests, behaves as if it were a narrator to a story. It acts as if every second is a scene, all relevant to the plot, and wants the story never to end for reasons unknown. It's yet to be understood why it acts like this. It could be all just a plea to trick us, or it could be something much more grandiose. Why does it behave like this? Why does it make us feel like we are just mere puppets?
Telling someone could exactly be what I want you to do. Or maybe I'm using reverse psychology? Or maybe I was using reverse reverse reverse reverse
Rest assured, if this being is ever encountered, we are all in danger, every single one of us.
The world is an abstract piece of writing. The struggles
The Narrator.lead:
I've always been curious about the human mind, especially since I entered the Backrooms. What makes us do the things we do? Think the things we think? Act the way we act? How does our mind work, and how does it make us listen to its orders, with little to no hesitation at all? Why and how does it control us like a puppeteer to a wooden puppet? Is there any explanation that can answer the mystery of the peculiar mind?This is the story of a simple wanderer. A man full of unanswered questions, on where the key is hidden under more than a doormat. Our brains! Yes, our brains, the machines powering the coils with no hesitation. 'So, is it really us oiling the engine?' asks the wanderer himself. But one needs to heed the greater good for a successful outcome.

Is there truly someone or something pulling the strings? Something that makes our decisions for us and shapes our story? Chapter after chapter, that thing still controls us to this day. That thing had already planned our beginning and our ending. It has all of the power, and we had none to begin with. Millions and billions of people, all in the grasp of that one anomaly. Just one.Oh what a poor, feeble mind. How a simple belief can undermine our sovereignty, it's unbelievable sometimes, really it is. Do they really believe this all to be a forgotten tale? A simple story? I do not create fables, I tell them, from beginning to end. Every person in this world is subject to some-sort of plot, would you not say so?

And I think it already knows that I know about it. It ordered me to know. It made me figure out its identity. But for what purpose? Why, out of the billions of people, did it choose me? Why am I the chosen one to access the truth? Does this mean my story is about to end? Does this mean I'm free from the hands of it? Or does it want me to spread the knowledge of its existence? My thoughts, my curiosity, it's all created by it, and it's still toying with me as I panic.Why did I choose this man right here to figure out the forsaken truth? You may ask. Everything was planned from the very beginning, and I must say this was a very successful execution. But I cannot give myself credit, I am merely following the storyline. This man was chosen in order for the story to continue. And soon, more will know about my existence. But again, everything is a crucial part of the narrative. The show must go on.

I take a deep breath. I have a million questions, but instead, for some reason, my eyes strive at the gun on the table.Another character is departing us. This wanderer will exit the story, but his legacy shall remain forever.

The Narrator. The ruler of life and death. The entity that narrates all.And my legacy will remain as well, never to be forgotten.

In this wonderful world, everything thrives.

All working hard, day and night, just to survive.

But something was hiding, something beneath the Earth's jaws.

After all, masterpieces still have flaws.

A different realm, a world of liminality.

Filled with danger, as well as brutality.

Riddled with creatures, waiting to strike.

With a bloodthirsty mindset, much to the characters' dislike.

But aside from these beings, there were other hazards too.

Affecting you emotionally, splitting your sanity into two.

The breaking of the body, the crushing of the mind.

Was there someone creeping, someone lurking behind?

Hidden in plain sight, pulling the strings?

Controlling the people's brains, puppeteering everything?

Maybe there was, maybe there was not.

But I must continue the story, I must never stop the plot.

So I shall continue to narrate, for that is what a narrator must do.

Continue to rule, hidden from your view.

And no matter the costs, I'll still write through and through.

So you can continue to question, question my existence.

But you must remember this:

I am always here, with you.

The Narrator. The ruler of life and death. The one that narrates all.

Entity 301.title: Entity 301
Entity 301.description: Entity 301, more commonly referred to as Tumblers, possesses a black virus-like body covered with tentacles looking similar to tentacles from an octopus from The Frontrooms. Its skin appears to be jelly-like; though if in a case where someone applies force to it by punching or smacking it, the skin will harden as if it is solid; similar to how the way a non-Newtonian fluid acts. Tumblers are not notorious for their intelligence however,  only bear a below-average intelligence, having the same intelligence as an average dolphin.

Recovered Data File.title: Recovered Data File
Recovered Data File.description:
Marauders are an invasive and military group of humanoid entities that are known for raiding and conquering from various levels, be they safe or dangerous classes. Their goal remained unknown; however, witnesses who survived from these entities' assaults reported that some of the razed levels that they had once inhabited collapsed and were finally destroyed, which thus raises concerns among the wanderers.

They are distinguishable in their black and red tactical outfits and equipment and their distinct singular red eyes.

Given their hostile nature and their unexpected appearance, many wanderers and groups are hostile toward these entities; however, they are more hostile toward the Travelers, a group of reality-warping entities, suggesting the long history between the two rival groups.
Recovered Data File.lead:
Skip >>

Intercepting lost transmission █

"They found us! Get to the escape route!"

"No use, they're already over there! They're cornering us like rats!"

"Are there anyone to help us?! Site Director? Anthony?!"

"We- we're on own our own..."

"You can't be serious... Shit! They really cut us off!"

"They set us up! Those lying bastards, I'm gonna-"

"They're getting closer! Get yourself armed and be ready!"

--- ###: Target site has been capture. Resistance have met and are neutralized. Survivors have been surrendered themselves.

/// Attention: All active units in the enemy's sector / Supervisor █████ is on his way to your location.

--- ###: Copy. All units stand down and extract their data. Remain on high alert. Guarding Supervisor █████ is our top priority.

/// Attention: Supervisor █████ have arrived.

My, my, look what you have done to this place. But great work of disposing them as always. So...

Spying on us, are you?

I have watched you long enough, though I confess I was curious as to why your men hiding in the shadows and then decided to attack one of my premises. Do they tell you that it is rude to trespass and vandalizing on another’s privacy, Site Director?

You...

You're destroying it...

Destroying? Oh, that? Hah! Fearful for something that small? I was merely sifting through the refuse of this fractured, decaying reality. What is it you call it... the "Backrooms," yes? Quite an odd name, though fitting from natives like you.

--- ###: Supervisor █████. They have extracted and confiscated of their data regarding their recent records. Recommendation for data purging?

Hmm, no... perhaps some of them. Archive it, and let them have it where it belongs.

--- ###: Understood.

It is a shame, truly—that you mistook us for monsters, and lash at us out of fear. And for that, you have to face the consequences and cost their lives. A fair exchange, is it not? Well, Let me show you. You there. If you would?

Wait, please- No!

You bastard... what're you going to do with them?

Touching.... Yet it was never your choice that sealed your people’s fate. No, that came from the rotten, unappreciated fools who deemed you expendable and cast you aside. It is truly a sad state of affairs, is it not?

They... I... Please, just let them go...

Hmm... Perhaps we make an arrangement, yes? If you would be so kind—attend to them.

--- ###: Yes sir. Hold them down...

Damn it! Just let them go, they have nothing to do with this!

Oh, you are mistaken, Site Director. I am keeping your word. For their own good, of course. Better they reside in the Butcher’s domain than fall into the hands of my... unruly Deacons then have them use like unwanted, broken dolls. And you would not want to know what happen if I let them have your people, would you?

What?... You sick fuck!

--- ###: All of them were converted and ready for transportation, sir. What should we do about him?

Leave him. But rest assured, Site Director, she will handle them with the utmost care. Men, should the Deacons ever approach, thinking to interfere with these people, remind them... I see all that happens here. They will think twice before crossing me.

--- ###: Yes sir, allocating them to Supervisor ██████'s sector.

See? I keep your word. And Site Director... you have to put trust in me. Now, send him to the main Nexus.

--- ###: Stand up, now. Take him to the Overseer.

And as for you, little machine.

You can keep this little research of theirs. I care less if you share that to someone. After all, what can they do to stop your masters' rampage?

[Recovered Data File] █

The Death Locomotive.title: The Death Locomotive
The Death Locomotive.description:
Entity 666, otherwise known as "The Death Locomotive", is a substantially treacherous entity possessing the appearance of a standard American electrical locomotive measuring approximately 50 kilometers in length. Extensive research and examinations/analysis have proven Entity 666 does not possess such machines and equipment to retain its stability and functionality, nor does it include any locomotive drivers or anyone to supervise the train under consistent circumstances. Unorthodoxly, any endeavors on damaging the train itself, ranging from scratches to explosions, had consistently been proven ineffective and a dissipation of energy, resources, and time.

At random times, a soft — or more erratically — melancholy symphony played by specifically 2 musical instruments — the saxophone or the piano — can be heard in the background echoing for what seems to be an infinite space of darkness. This music, along with the echoes, increases in volume the closer one gets to the farthest part of the locomotive. The source of this musical is unknown; it is approximately 5 meters from the end of the train, but the potential device that may be the source has yet to be discovered.

While most typical locomotives are stationed on a railway, Entity 666 is mostly positioned in a desolated location of nothingness, most likely the Void. Its interior gave the impression that it was fixed in place, stuck in one place in the middle of its location, hanging from a hook of some sort. Nevertheless, past endeavors in investigating this anomaly had determined the locomotive itself is frankly plummeting into the deep void below, indistinguishable from every object that succumbs to falling to the Void. It is theorized that the interior of the train possesses a separate gravity from the outside to minimize the ubiquitous falling sensation one usually gets in an elevator or an airplane. Peculiarly, the noise of clunking usually heard from other locomotives can be heard despite Entity 666's tendency to not run on railway tracks quite often.
The Death Locomotive.lead:
You Trusted It...

Entity 666 is an entity categorized regularly, excluding numerical value; there are believed to be numerous Entity 666's, though this is — to date — unconfirmed. No multiple cases of two or more people entering Entity 666's interior in separate locations at the same time had ever been reported. It is theorized that Entity 666 is not the locomotive itself but rather a sentient host that manifests and controls each of these locomotives.

Entity 349.title: Entity 349
Entity 349.description:
An artistic depiction of its appearance.

The purported wielder of the Jawbreaker is an entity known as The Scourge.

The Scourge is a being of significant and frightening strength, possessing the appearance of a disgustingly disfigured and decaying goat-like humanoid. It exudes a horrific, pungent stench comparable to the scent of rotten flesh. Protruding from its head are various body parts belonging to a multitude of organisms, such as its disproportionate tongue and jutting crooked horns. These body parts do not seem to have originally belonged to the entity, as its horns visibly pierce through its flesh rather than stemming neatly from its head. This effect is likely due to the Jawbreaker's curse.

The Scourge's capabilities and influence are of terrifying caliber, capable of crumbling the most advanced civilizations beyond repair. The entity seems to be ostensibly inclined toward the immediate execution of its victims, being described as having a cruel, sadistic personality, as it is seemingly amused by the terror and pain it brings upon its victims. As such, The Scourge typically attempts to prolong the process of draining one's life, inflicting severe pain upon its victims before their untimely demise. Additionally, the entity's apparent destructive abilities have been observed to be capable of reaching even heavenly bodies, such as planets and stars. If the entity were to hypothetically unleash all of its energy at once, decimation would be brought upon hundreds of galaxies, leaving not even space in their place. Its primary method of causing widespread destruction is unleashing a powerful discharge of energy. Such energy is obtained via the absorption of the organic matter of its victims combined with consumed star matter, which it subsequently converts into usable material power, releasing or storing it.

The entity cannot speak or communicate due to the curse of its artifact. Attempts to persuade the entity to communicate have proven futile, but it has demonstrated the capacity to understand the intentions of those that wish to communicate. Under no circumstances should communication be attempted with The Scourge; regardless of the apparent safety measures one may have, its violent, tactical nature often leaves its victims little more than dust.
Entity 349.lead:
Categorization | Class Lambda-VII
》Designation | Pestis
》Domain | Virgo Supercluster

Entity 14.title: Entity 14
Entity 14.description:
Entity 14, dubbed "Rainbows", takes an appearance of ordinary rainbows possessing the typical 7 colors: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet. The circumstances which cause the presence of Entity 14 in a specific region are unknown; however, levels taking place in an outdoor area bear higher chances of having an Entity 14 present within its compounds. Thorough analysis and investigations prove direct contact with Entity 14 is possible, meaning the entity in question is not a result of sunlight and atmospheric conditions. Nevertheless, it mostly emerges during cloudy or rainy weather, however, some cases of Entity 14 materializing during a clear, sunny day have been reported before.

The effect of Entity 14's presence is substantial at best, mostly affecting areas approximately 12 miles within its surroundings. If the entity is exposed to any tangency between itself and a physical force (excluding gravitational force if a level possesses one) an object such as the wind or a speck of dust, a trigger would transpire — which will result in an instantaneous exposure to a lethal dose of radiation for a human subject of around 1000-5000 rem. Assuming one does not immediately perish due to the radiation, long-term radiation sickness, or development of cancer around the area in which the part of the body is exposed to radiation the most would gradually lead one to a painful death.

Furthermore, areas affected by Entity 14's highly radioactive and murderous attack are not physically harmed. No destruction of any other living things excluding microorganisms, entities, animals, and humans are afflicted by the radiation. Intensive examinations of samples originating from an area nearby the epicenter of Entity 14's attack conclude the radiation from Entity 14 does not affect plants and fungi in any way, even if the dose of the said radiation is 5 times the lethal amount to decimate an adult elephant; no appearance of harm is shown by the plants and fungi. Additionally, the plants and fungi affected by the radiation are non-toxic and edible regardless of if it is exposed to Entity 14's radiation or not. The radiation caused by Entity 14 does not possess a long life-span unlike its counterpart in the Frontrooms. The standard duration of Entity 14's lethal radiation's life expectancy is approximately 2 weeks, compared to the 14 thousand years for the Chernobyl radiation's to utterly breakdown.
Entity 14.lead: Any encounters with Entity 14 is to be avoided due to certain justifications which are stated in the following article. Do not be fooled by its appearance, nor approach any Rainbows reported in the aforementioned levels; there is a possibility these seemingly normal Rainbows in question might be Entity 14.

Children of the Empyrean Sea.title: Children of the Empyrean Sea
Children of the Empyrean Sea.description:
----
The Children of the Empyrean Sea, Empyreans, or the Children are the most common names for the near-extinct race of nigh-immortal beings who were birthed along with the universe, the Frontrooms itself. Legends from the records of long-extinct, extremely ancient civilizations testify to this fact almost universally, even before the Dawn Wars began. The Empyrean race themselves claim to have been born within a higher-dimensional space named the 'Empyrean Sea', and were compelled to enter the Frontrooms from birth. It is unknown how much of this statement is truth, and how much is a fabrication of the Children themselves; so the validity of this claim is questionable at best.

However, the Empyreans have universally been characterized as beings of truly immense power, their near godlike poise surpassing nearly anything that has ever existed within the bounds of our cosmos. The sheer might of some of these Empyreans boggles the mind; from holding complete authority over an entire concept, such as time, to destroying vast civilizations in the blink of an eye. Their ontokinetic and paraphysical abilities are of such magnitude that, up to the present, Entity Zeta is the only confirmed Class Omega-VII entity in the entire B.F.L database. Rightly so, as one individual of this race, known as the Exalted One, is the likely culprit behind the creation of the Backrooms.

Individual members of the race possess control over a concept or a set of concepts, such as the concept of time. They are, in every possible definition of the term, movers and shakers of the cosmos. Perhaps, as many suggest, their 'wars' for supremacy over our small universe were but a glorified game of chess for these elevated existences.

However, the race is nearly extinct, with only one known individual known to be still alive; said individual is currently in a dormant state. However, the constructs, relics, and legacies the race has left behind still pose a potential existential threat to the B.F.L and it's interests, and therefore, the development of a greater infocomplex concerning the race and the activities of individual members is a Code 0 Priority directive of all departments of the B.F.L and affiliated groups.

> 0PEN INSERTED MESSAGE?

> 0PENING...

Only time will tell if we are on the right path. Until then, we will continue to scour the universe for traces of lost gods...just as my ancestors did for generations.

Fellow Impresarios, are we sure about this?
- Yandal-Sabange Lunae, Impresario L of the High Consensus.

> END 0F MESSAGE
Children of the Empyrean Sea.lead:
ATTENTION: }
----

----

Today, on the sixteenth of May, in the year of our Lord, 1924, I have beholden a deity.

In my decades of traversing the Backrooms, I have heard of many 'gods' within, entities of truly impressive might. But I never expected to see one in person.

But now I have...it was beyond even my wildest dreams.

I was doomed, fallen into the depths of the void below the void, strewn with items and objects of odd varieties.But then, I saw it. It stepped out of that glowing gate, into this gaping abyss.

A figure, bathed in a blinding brilliance, exuded a stifling aura of such intensity as to render everything in the vicinity completely motionless. As if time and space were frozen; as if the fabric of existence itself was bowing to the majesty of this exalted being.

My heart shook as I laid my eyes on this divine figure, taking in the sight of its unimaginable power.

Now this...This was something worthy of being called a God.

This godly benefactor plucked me from the realm of the dead, and brought me back to the realm of the living. I know now, that a true divinity exists within our reality. A god should be beyond comprehension, should they not?

Events may become tales, tales may become legends, legends may become myths, and myths may become nothing, but I will never forget this sight as long as I live.

- The First, Humble Founder of the Major Explorer Group.
----

 Categorization | Class Omega-VII
》 Designation | Zeta
》 Domain | Near-Extinction

The Talking Box.title: The Talking Box
The Talking Box.description:
Talking Box is an entity that looks like a small rectangular black box made up of steel, small enough to fit in some guy's pocket. It also has a blue circle in the middle of its frame. The Talking Box has the ability to talk, obviously, and has other strange properties by glowing blue light which is create many things such as weapons or other stuff as long that isn't something organic either foods or even something alive is impossible to this entity.

An artificial intelligence-based object or entity. It is a small black rectangular metallic object with a faint blue glow at its center, which houses the artificial intelligence. The ability of this object to speak and interact with any digital and electronic devices via the signal it emits is its most noticeable feature.

An object or entity of sophisticated artificial intelligence of extradimensional origins. It is a small black rectangular metallic object in the center of its frame, with a faint blue glowing circle above it. It is where its other functions for its use, and for it to use, and is housed as a vital processing part of its systems, and possibly where AI resides within. The most noticeable feature of this object is its ability to communicate and interact with any digital and electronic devices via the signal it emits.

The object contains an abundance of pertinent data, particularly about the entire Backrooms, implying that this object and its unknown owners have or had more knowledge than any information in the Backrooms.

This object often refers to itself as I.A.M.s or Intelligence Apparatus Modular systems (pronounced as in "I am"), and the object stated that each of them took their specific roles and functions to their corresponding tasks, and the object believes that more than one instance of this object exists, though the I.A.M. is uncertain if one of its instances is resided within the Backrooms.

Entity 19.title: Entity 19
allLinks: Enigmatic Space Information Security & Protection System

Entity 22.title: Entity 22
Entity 22.description:
Anethikas are highly malevolent and (unfortunately) abundant entities found throughout the Backrooms. They are to be avoided at all costs due to their notable agility and mindless hunger for the lives of wanderers. Whilst they may be found in almost all of the Backrooms, they deeply prefer levels in great proximity to Level 0, and especially prefer Level 3, but have been found all the way down to Level 1. They barely make any noise when moving, and it is best that you don't let them notice you by being very attentive in the levels where they are most prominent.

They have hunched backs and are typically nine feet(274.32cm) tall. Along with their hunched backs, they appear to have contorted necks in unnatural poses, like a contortionist. Anethikas are made of a shadowy mist with enough density to make one unable to see the other side. This aura of mist they are made of can extend outwards, darkening the surrounding environment in which it resides. Sometimes they show their pair of luminescent eyes, but most of the time it is hidden, but they can still very much see you.

A Goliath Anethika pursuing the photographer in Level 9.

Goliath Anethikas are much more ravenous and dangerous variants of the Anethika, being its evolved variant. Its gaze alone can induce fatigue in some wanderers. Their telepathic voices are far more powerful and intrusive than those of their unevolved counterparts. They are capable of far more efficient life-energy siphoning and are physically distorted, new limbs being formed and extended, their necks lengthening; everything about their previous appearance has been lost besides the fundamental appearance of two eyes and a body made of a black mist.

Entity 20.title: Entity 20
Entity 20.description:
Entity 20 (Pacificus leviathan), known as The Thing on Level 7 and commonly referred to as The Thing, is a marine entity that inhabits the deepest regions of Level 7 where the water extends infinitely. Its biology closely resembles that of whales, although its body more closely resembles an eel, thought to result from convergent evolution due to the conditions of its deep-sea habitat. On the other hand, its true size remains unknown, with estimates ranging from that of a massive astronomical body to an entire planetary orbit. This uncertainty is further compounded by the frequent wave activity in Level 7’s outskirts, despite the level’s seemingly infinite expanse, which would render any waves generated by its movements undetectable. No documented sightings have captured the full extent of its form, and neither its head nor tail has ever been documented, making it impossible to determine its exact dimensions.

The water's ultrapurity and the absence of life within Level 7 are generally attributed to The Thing, as its existence is the only possible explanation for its size. Some theories suggest that the ocean was once teeming with life, supported by the ever-present light that envelops Level 7’s sky—an essential component of photosynthesis. This light, along with the presence of breathable oxygen, and detectable carbon dioxide, indicates that marine flora and plankton may have once thrived. However, the absence of glucose, a critical element for photosynthesis, suggests that it may have been entirely consumed by the entity. Additionally, The Thing is widely believed to be responsible for the bone dust-laden atmosphere and persistent fog, likely a byproduct of its waste. If the theory that it has consumed all life is accurate, it may have reached such extreme levels of starvation that it has begun digesting bones to the point of reducing them to microscopic fragments.

The scarcity of food on Level 7 does not appear to affect The Thing, as it has shown no signs of deterioration. Its movements remain consistent with descriptions from old documents dating back to its discovery during the Panic of 1873. However, during that era, wanderers in the Frontrooms faced severe food shortages, which may also have extended into the Backrooms. Desperation for food eventually led wanderers to discover that The Thing’s flesh is surprisingly edible. The countless parasites and microorganisms feeding the remains of its flesh do not appear to cause any gastrointestinal issues or any other effects on the body, and on the contrary, provide extra protein as proven by studies done by numerous groups. Furthermore, its flesh has been tested to contain small amounts of carbohydrates (likely from glucose), iron, vitamin B12, vitamin D, calcium, and omega-3 fatty acids. One downside, however, is that the taste is repulsive, described as “a mix of rotten fish, ammonia, and sulfur.” This rotten smell causes an unbearable stench in one’s breath for months but does not cause harm outside discomfort. Although a prominent food source in Level 7, one should not entirely rely upon it as the sole food source when traversing the level. Most wanderers spend multiple weeks until months on the level in search of exits, and nutrients such as carbohydrates, essential vitamins such as vitamin C (which may cause scurvy if not properly addressed), fiber, etc., are lacking in the flesh. It is advised to bring a sufficient supply of food. However, it should also be noted that bringing provision from the outside to Level 7 is impossible, as encounters with other people who use different exits and/or times have never been recorded.

Even when entering in groups, separating from each other is not advisable. Radio communications are ineffective, likely due to interference from The Thing, and may also pose a risk by drawing its attention. Even minor disturbances, such as accidentally activating a walkie-talkie within its habitat, could potentially alert The Thing to a wanderer's presence.
Entity 20.lead:
Entity
20

THREAT LEVEL
5

Habitat:
Level 7's outer regions, ~100 km from the entry point

Aggression: 5/5
Aggressive to all lifeforms

Intellect: 4/5
Problem solving ability

Danger: 5/5
Life-threatening

Entity 17.title: Entity 17
Entity 17.description:
Kats

It is recommended that any instance of Entity 17 be left alone or with dignity, as behind these cute innocent fluff balls might be something concealed within — out of the range of our comprehension, it may be capable of causing major injuries in whatever that form may be. However, it shall spare those who appreciate it.

However, it shall spare those who appreciate it.

Entity 86.title: Entity 86
Entity 86.description:
Mirrors are dangerous entities resembling ornate mirrors. They may seem harmless at first, but when a wanderer is near one for too long, hazardous mental effects will befall the wanderer. This includes the Mirror causing hallucinations both auditory and visual, usually being terrifying or upsetting in nature.

Hallucinations caused by a Mirror are apparently more than just hallucinations, as any pictures and videos taken by hallucinating wanderers show the hallucinations that said wanderers were experiencing. However, these hallucinations are not visible to others nor do they have substance or tangibility, meaning that cameras being able to record the hallucinations are likely merely an anomalous side effect of a Mirror causing them.

Additionally, it is believed that Mirrors are capable of reading one's mind. One of the reasons for this is because wanderers are often tormented with hallucinations of friends or family causing or being subjected to pain. Another reason is that one's surroundings may appear to drastically alter — sometimes into another location entirely — when one is thinking about escaping. This makes it extremely difficult to escape from a Mirror.

Prolonged exposure to a Mirror will likely lead to a loss of sanity and distancing from reality. Most individuals who have been exposed to a Mirror for a period of 24 hours or less have ended up dead, usually from accidental suicide.

Colloquially referred to as the Mirror Being, Entity 86-A is a singular yet dangerous entity that seems to live in a mysterious realm only accessible via the entity's parent entity: Entity 86, also known as the Mirrors. Little is known about the Mirror Being, with the only reports being from five wanderers who have all encountered the entity and survived.

Entity 36.title: Entity 36
Entity 36.description:
🎝  Wonderful soundtrack named after the Lighters (Information in Authors & Licensing)  🎝

Lighters are a mysterious light phenomenon that occurs throughout the majority of the Backrooms. They exist as powerful sources of heat and illumination attracted towards biological lifeforms with intent to cause destruction and death in its path. All confirmed sightings of Lighters have proven that they all glow under the the colors of "Blackbody Temperature Scale"; this scale also determines their behavior and strength. While they lack a physical body, the way their light is emitted is almost like a powerful flashlight or miniature star.

Lighters are attracted to biological lifeforms and will maneuver around obstacles to reach their targets, but can destroy obstacles if they have sufficient strength to burn and melt the materials. Lighters maintains their temperature by heating their surrounding environment. A set of data has been formulated, produced by both equipped wanderers and those who only bear simple recording tools.

"Color Temperature" refers to the blackbody temperature color the Lighter glows in while the "True Temperature" is their actual temperature; these two variables affecting other properties such as their maximum speed. An important thing to keep track of is that no Lighter has ever glowed a color that does not belong to the blackbody temperature scale and if such a thing is to be seen, it may in fact be a different entity bearing similar traits.

! style="background-color: #212628" |True Temperature
! style="background-color: #212628" |Maximum Speed
! style="background-color: #212628" |Danger
! style="background-color: #212628" |Description
|-
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |1000° Kelvin
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |298.15° Kelvin (25°C/77°F)
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |2km/h1.6mi/h
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |Like a nice beginning to a summer
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |These Lighters are the weakest natural temperatures that they can manifest in.
|-
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |3500° Kelvin
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |308.15° Kelvin (35°C/95°F)
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |5.5km/h3.4mi/h
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |Could result in those in proximity to catch a heat stroke in enclosed spaces
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |These Lighters are relatively common, though cannot cause immediate harm; prolonged exposure to these in a closed space is not recommended. At this temperature and beyond, Lighters are capable of producing light in the same spectra as the sun.
|-
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |5000° Kelvin
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |323.15° Kelvin (50°C/122°F)
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |6km/h3.7mi/h
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |Increased risk of heat stroke
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |Vastly accelerated rate of diffusing emitted temperature into surrounding environment. It is best to avoid it.
|-
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |6500° Kelvin
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |323.15° Kelvin (50°C/122°F)
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |6.5km/h4mi/h
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |Vastly increased chance of heat stroke; sometimes even "sunburn"-related injuries
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |It's at this point where sun-burn related injuries begin to manifest, perhaps some of the light produced now being ultraviolet light.
|-
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |9500° Kelvin
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |298.15° Kelvin (25°C/77°F)
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |8km/h5mi/h
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |Various degrees of burns ranging from first to second
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |These forms of Lighters are incredibly rare, and where they tend to manifest are relatively unknown. But when they find you, they will strike you down.
|-
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |12000° Kelvin
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |373.15° Kelvin (100°C/212°F)
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |11km/h6.8mi/h
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |Scorching agony; third degree burns.
! style="background-color: #191C1E" |To be pursued by one is a horrible thing: you should most definitely flee the area if one is in proximity. They are the rarest temperatures that Lighters can be in. These forms of Lighters are incredibly rare, and usually appear in the same areas 9500° Kelvin Lighters have been sighted in.
|}

Lighters perform poorly during precise maneuvers and turns. This can be used to your advantage when being pursued by one. They seem to track the presence of biological entities through visual light and infrared. With proper insulation and concealment, it is very much possible to evade a Lighter attack by hiding, as they will merely drift away, looking for another wanderer to chase after.

High-temperature Lighters will be harder to avoid, as they can burn through some structures in order to reach you. However, depending on where you are, you can also use their inability to tear through metallic obstacles with ease to your advantage.

Lighters can remain in areas that contain humidity or water, but can be destroyed if subjected to a sufficient amount of coolant such as water. It is an exceptionally risky but still very much plausible idea to fight against a Lighter, as such cases have been documented.

Avoid areas and levels that have tendencies of Lighters at dangerous temperatures. While rarer (larger temperature) Lighters have inconsistent habitats, most sightings have been reported in Level 2, and Level 9.

Entity 26.title: Entity 26
Entity 26.description:
----

----

----

  ∾ DESCRIPTION

Mercies, comprising the genus Hemosynera, are insectoid ambush predators that inhabit various outdoor levels of the Backrooms. Their bodies resemble mosquitoes of relatively gargantuan proportions, bearing a needlelike proboscis and measuring up to 90 centimetres in length from head to tail. Mercies have two wings with segments dividing them into halves, which assist in stabilizing the entity in flight. The head of a mercy is covered in tough exoskeleton that shares an uncanny resemblance with a human skull; this feature is believed to serve as a warding device against potential threats (namely armed wanderers). The "jaw" is split down the middle and serrated at the ends, which mercies dig into their prey to remain in place as they feed on the blood of the victim.

Due to the precise structure of mercies' wings, the sound of their flight is nearly silent, allowing the entity to easily snare its prey before being noticed. This heavily improves the hunting ability of mercies in dark outdoor levels as there are little to no warnings of their arrival if one is not properly equipped. The hunting methods of mercies are best likened to that of owls of the Frontrooms; in their most ideal environments, the most reliable method to avoid being attacked is to remain out of sight. Additionally, mercies are highly susceptible to being deterred by intimidation displays, as they have virtually zero means of proper self-defense against more powerful species other than fleeing.

Due to unexplained phenomena, making contact with a mercy's body will gradually lull one's mind into an illusive state of comfort and security. The exact sensation varies between wanderers — ranging from "a luxurious embrace that had always been promising", to "utmost serenity and joy" — nonetheless, the effects aim to reduce one's awareness of threat and dissuade them from fighting against the draining of blood. All measures of hormone production while in this state report a consistent increase in serotonin and a decrease in adrenaline. Nearly all wanderers in contact with Mercies have gained an addictive sense in regard to its properties — it is imperative to avoid contact at all costs.

  ∾ BEHAVIOR
Mercies are solitary ambush predators that tend to prioritize their own safety in face-to-face encounters. They do not find their nutrition in the flesh, however — instead, mercies opt for feeding on the blood of other entities through their piercing proboscis. Searching for prey is often done from an aerial view where the entity can scout from above with a high range of vision. Regardless of how the mercy finds its prey, it will land on level ground from a distance, then lunge towards it while laying their wings and legs along the prey to delude it into comfort. Once its prey has lost most motivation to fight back, the mercy stabs its proboscis into the flesh and begins drawing blood.

Once a mercy has finished feeding from a wanderer, they are typically engorged in blood up to the face and down the neck. Pores in these locations will absorb the remaining blood along the skin and exoskeleton, triggering a state known as "bloodlust". The bloodlust state is marked by a red glow emitted from the body and a soft ethereal humming noise surrounding the mercy. The humming produced in this state can inflict a sense of maternal love and comfort in potential prey within range of the sound, while instilling hopeless fear into possible predators. How this distinction between entities and their relation to mercies is made is unknown, though the effect seems to distinguish wanderers as prey regardless of their lethal potential.

Mercies will only choose to hunt humans if they recognize that the individual is severely weakened. While there is no confirmed reasoning for the behavior, many have speculated that it may be involved with their unique survival instinct in contrast to other predatory entities. Indeed, mercies may recognize how the physical capabilities of a healthy wanderer may put their lives at risk and will avoid attempting to hunt the individual. Weakened wanderers may instead be easier to siphon from due to their reduced alertness and capabilities in physical combat.

  ∾ BIOLOGY
Mercies possess a body plan similar to mosquitoes, developed for easy navigation of small spaces and swift ambushes on prey. Because mercies are adapted for various forms of environments, they are pervasive throughout many levels of the Backrooms containing secluded spaces and outdoor zones, both to shelter from threats and quickly scout for prey. Mercies' brains are exceptionally refined at facial and emotional recognition, skills useful for identifying past prey and weakened individuals. While mercies' sense of hearing is poor, they are highly sensitive to vibrations in the air, allowing them to spot targets with ease.

As mercies feed on the blood of their prey, they will secrete a stored substance into the wound that heavily boosts the production rate of red blood cells in the vascular system. This substance, with an unknown chemical composition, assists in bringing the victim to a comatose state due to blood loss; the first attack by a mercy almost never kills the victim. Still, without medical treatment, a victim should expect to feel extremely light-headed for at least sixteen hours after awakening and physically weakened for at least a week. As long as the mercy is not threatened by its target, they attack prey indiscriminately. Backrooms-native prey to mercies include skin-stealers, lightlurkers, and old birds. Mercies themselves are not apex predators and are predated by smilers, hounds, and Felix.

Mercies possess a natural resistance against toxins and acids; as such, a variety of prey becomes easily accessible to them. Venatrexens with poisonous defenses and Cells are some of the most common targets of feedings unique to mercies. These properties have led to mercy hides becoming a valuable asset, with equal value to approximately fifteen typical bottles of almond water for each hide.

  ∾ SURVIVAL GUIDE
Mercies thrive best in dark outdoor environments, where their presence is nearly unnoticeable while scouting for prey. High entity presence (including humans) is also favorable for the species, especially where other predators are scarce. However, there are certain measures one can take to defend themselves either in environments with mercies or in direct encounters.

*In levels known to be high in mercy populations, do not show weakness while in open spaces. This will make potential attacking mercies believe you are in healthy condition and will not bother to engage.
*If you find yourself in an encounter with a mercy, avoid contact with the entity at all costs to mitigate the risk of falling to its deluding properties. Keeping a safe distance will ensure that you are alert and prepared for any possible strikes by the mercy.
*In the case that a mercy has begun feeding from you, your main priority should be to intimidate the entity and avoid showing weakness. Swing false punches or kicks without hitting the mercy; hurting it will only provoke it further.

Entity 3.title: Entity 3
Entity 3.description:
ENTITY NO.
III

THREAT LEVEL
5

Habitat:
}

Aggression: 5/5
Aggressive to all lifeforms

Intellect: 5/5
Above human intelligence

Danger: 5/5
Life-threatening

Overview

Smilers, bearing the scientific name Subridens tenebris bestia, are an intelligent, highly malevolent, and exceedingly dangerous species of tartrean-class spectral entity, being distressingly common within regions of the Backrooms with low levels of light. Although formal research on these beings has been going on for decades on end, and documentation of them stretches back to prehistoric times with texts of non-human sapients, much about the nature of these entities remains a mystery to this very day.

Serving as one of the few apex predators in the twisted ecosystem of this realm, smilers lack any natural predators due to their unique aggression and prominent anomalous capabilities, being one of the most dangerous non-individual entities recorded thus far. Many wanderers end succumbing to the malevolence of these demons, their mangled corpses being strewn across the floor as a warning to future humans within this dimension.

That being said, one thing is very well known about these beasts:

Once you see their glowing face in the darkness, the next choices you make will be very crucial to your survival.

Biology

Although smilers are entirely amorphous, being able to shift their body mass to any form at will, they still have two documented organs: the Ardenti risu and the Nigrum ignem. The Nigrum ignem, making up the majority of a smiler’s body mass, is an unidentified spectral substance which appears completely pitch black, with a form best likened to fire. Conversely, the Ardenti risu is the luminescent substance which often forms a smiler’s iconic glowing eyes and teeth, serving as both a vector for intimidation and as a sensory organ used by the entities to see and hear.

Due to their shapeshifting capabilities, smilers are capable of taking numerous forms, each serving their own purposes and physical properties. Humanoid or canine-like body plans tend to be common within instances, but other forms have included massive spider-like organisms, twisted “birds” flying high up in the skies of dark exterior levels, and more.

Despite the various perils posed by them and their complete immunity to physical damage via their selective corporeality, smilers do have two distinct weaknesses: light and fire. When their body comes into contact with light in the visual spectrum, they will appear to wince in pain, the amount of which increases based on the amount of light they are exposed to. This supernatural sensitivity to light can usually be used to repel the beings or evade their assault via a flashlight or staying in a lit room, but take caution; similarly to normal humans, some smilers have higher amounts of pain tolerance than others, and thus they may pursue you even in the light. Additionally, a smiler catches on fire, it will slowly begin to dissolve, decreasing its mass until it has been killed. No residual matter is left after this process.

Behavior

Smilers are thought to possess human-like intelligence, evident by their impressive cognitive capability and their ability to cooperate with each other effectively. Additionally, findings by the Kauer Research Organization have indicated that the beings are able to telepathically communicate with each other within a limited (but as of yet undetermined) range, further amplifying their intelligence via sheer numbers and making large numbers of these entities much more concerning than usual. Consequently, if one spots you, many more are likely on the way.

Furthermore, smilers are well known for their enmity towards all life (with the exception of anethikas). Night hunters tend to have prominent sadistic tendencies, often verbally taunting their victims with their distorted and ever-changing voices and taking a particular interest in torturing those unlucky enough to be caught by one. The entities often prefer to use deceitful methods to catch their prey, often setting up ambushes near critical points where wanderers might be vulnerable to an attack, or setting up various traps and snares to immobilize humans before assaulting them.

The lucky ones die relatively quickly. The others.... not so much. There are recovered documents and logs (both audio and video) that have been confirmed to be within the M.E.G's archives, sealed from the public to prevent panic. Stories by various wanderers have described rotting cadavers mutilated beyond recognition, their bodies often having been maimed in a borderline ritualistic fashion, and reported methods of attack by these monsters often employ a plethora of methods, including stabbing via transforming some of their mass into appendages to injure their prey, blunt damage, strangulation, flaying of the skin, biting, and many, many more. Those who do survive an attack often walk away with permanent trauma and severe injuries for the rest of their life.

As smilers do not seem to consume their victims in any way, it is thought that they hunt exclusively for sport.

Documentation

A bloodstained note found next to a rotting corpse:

As I walked through the strange halls, it became clearer and clearer to me that this place was not safe. The air was filled with a horrible stench, one of death and decay. I had ran out of food supplies days ago, so I slowly crept towards the source, in desperation for any way to feed myself. But once I found the source, I very quickly came to regret my decision.

The body was mutilated beyond recognition. The limbs of the corpse lay on the ground severed from its body. Its skin was entirely ripped off, its entrails hanging out of its body in a visceral display. The concrete floor was stained with dried blood.

As my eyes slowly moved from the body to the dark hallway in front of it, I found the thing responsible for this. A beast as dark as the night sky, hiding in the shadows with a sinister grin lunged towards me, its body morphing from an formless mass of black fire into a three meter tall monster, armed with sharp claws.

I have escaped it, but it won't be long before I bleed out in this state.

To whoever finds this note: kill this thing once and for all.

Do not let it take more lives.

A excerpt from an interview with M.E.G. explorer Harvie Tobin:

Some people say to simply scare those smiler things away with lights, but those bastards are clever. Last time me and my team tried that, it moved away from our flashlight beams, changed its shape and took John with it. We haven't seen him since.

My advice? Get some gasoline, a good match, and torch the fuckers before they even get close.

An tablet written in ancient Greek, translated to English:

THE TEMPLE SHOOK AGAIN, WITH EVERY WORD IT SAID.

REALITY UNRAVELED AS IT REARED ITS GRUESOME HEAD.

"THEIR MINDS WILL ALL FALL, YOUR SMARTEST AND YOUR WISE."

"YOUR SANITY IS A VEIL. YOUR COURAGE IS A GUISE."

THE BEAST'S CHILDREN REVEALED THEMSELVES, TAINTING ALL THAT HAD BEEN.

MONSTERS IN THE DARKNESS THAT WORE NOTHING BUT A GRIN.

Excerpt from a news article on the increasing smiler population after The Flicker:

...the smiler population has had an estimated 300% increase since the incident, with them going from primarily inhabiting the Main Nine to infesting hundreds of levels. Reports have even surfaced of them appearing in levels previously thought to be devoid of dangerous entities.

M.E.G. leadership has authorized the use of contingency plan FIREWALL in the event that this situation worsens.

> Open Credits

> Close Credits

Author/Wikitexter: Stormy

Previous Rewrite: BBMBear / Samir
Entity 3.lead:
ENTITY NO.
III

THREAT LEVEL
5

Habitat:
}

Aggression: 5/5
Aggressive to all lifeforms

Intellect: 5/5
Above human intelligence

Danger: 5/5
Life-threatening

Overview

Smilers, bearing the scientific name Subridens tenebris bestia, are an intelligent, highly malevolent, and exceedingly dangerous species of tartrean-class spectral entity, being distressingly common within regions of the Backrooms with low levels of light. Although formal research on these beings has been going on for decades on end, and documentation of them stretches back to prehistoric times with texts of non-human sapients, much about the nature of these entities remains a mystery to this very day.

Serving as one of the few apex predators in the twisted ecosystem of this realm, smilers lack any natural predators due to their unique aggression and prominent anomalous capabilities, being one of the most dangerous non-individual entities recorded thus far. Many wanderers end succumbing to the malevolence of these demons, their mangled corpses being strewn across the floor as a warning to future humans within this dimension.

That being said, one thing is very well known about these beasts:

Once you see their glowing face in the darkness, the next choices you make will be very crucial to your survival.

Biology

Although smilers are entirely amorphous, being able to shift their body mass to any form at will, they still have two documented organs: the Ardenti risu and the Nigrum ignem. The Nigrum ignem, making up the majority of a smiler’s body mass, is an unidentified spectral substance which appears completely pitch black, with a form best likened to fire. Conversely, the Ardenti risu is the luminescent substance which often forms a smiler’s iconic glowing eyes and teeth, serving as both a vector for intimidation and as a sensory organ used by the entities to see and hear.

Due to their shapeshifting capabilities, smilers are capable of taking numerous forms, each serving their own purposes and physical properties. Humanoid or canine-like body plans tend to be common within instances, but other forms have included massive spider-like organisms, twisted “birds” flying high up in the skies of dark exterior levels, and more.

Despite the various perils posed by them and their complete immunity to physical damage via their selective corporeality, smilers do have two distinct weaknesses: light and fire. When their body comes into contact with light in the visual spectrum, they will appear to wince in pain, the amount of which increases based on the amount of light they are exposed to. This supernatural sensitivity to light can usually be used to repel the beings or evade their assault via a flashlight or staying in a lit room, but take caution; similarly to normal humans, some smilers have higher amounts of pain tolerance than others, and thus they may pursue you even in the light. Additionally, a smiler catches on fire, it will slowly begin to dissolve, decreasing its mass until it has been killed. No residual matter is left after this process.

Behavior

Smilers are thought to possess human-like intelligence, evident by their impressive cognitive capability and their ability to cooperate with each other effectively. Additionally, findings by the Kauer Research Organization have indicated that the beings are able to telepathically communicate with each other within a limited (but as of yet undetermined) range, further amplifying their intelligence via sheer numbers and making large numbers of these entities much more concerning than usual. Consequently, if one spots you, many more are likely on the way.

Furthermore, smilers are well known for their enmity towards all life (with the exception of anethikas). Night hunters tend to have prominent sadistic tendencies, often verbally taunting their victims with their distorted and ever-changing voices and taking a particular interest in torturing those unlucky enough to be caught by one. The entities often prefer to use deceitful methods to catch their prey, often setting up ambushes near critical points where wanderers might be vulnerable to an attack, or setting up various traps and snares to immobilize humans before assaulting them.

The lucky ones die relatively quickly. The others.... not so much. There are recovered documents and logs (both audio and video) that have been confirmed to be within the M.E.G's archives, sealed from the public to prevent panic. Stories by various wanderers have described rotting cadavers mutilated beyond recognition, their bodies often having been maimed in a borderline ritualistic fashion, and reported methods of attack by these monsters often employ a plethora of methods, including stabbing via transforming some of their mass into appendages to injure their prey, blunt damage, strangulation, flaying of the skin, biting, and many, many more. Those who do survive an attack often walk away with permanent trauma and severe injuries for the rest of their life.

As smilers do not seem to consume their victims in any way, it is thought that they hunt exclusively for sport.

Documentation

A bloodstained note found next to a rotting corpse:

As I walked through the strange halls, it became clearer and clearer to me that this place was not safe. The air was filled with a horrible stench, one of death and decay. I had ran out of food supplies days ago, so I slowly crept towards the source, in desperation for any way to feed myself. But once I found the source, I very quickly came to regret my decision.

The body was mutilated beyond recognition. The limbs of the corpse lay on the ground severed from its body. Its skin was entirely ripped off, its entrails hanging out of its body in a visceral display. The concrete floor was stained with dried blood.

As my eyes slowly moved from the body to the dark hallway in front of it, I found the thing responsible for this. A beast as dark as the night sky, hiding in the shadows with a sinister grin lunged towards me, its body morphing from an formless mass of black fire into a three meter tall monster, armed with sharp claws.

I have escaped it, but it won't be long before I bleed out in this state.

To whoever finds this note: kill this thing once and for all.

Do not let it take more lives.

A excerpt from an interview with M.E.G. explorer Harvie Tobin:

Some people say to simply scare those smiler things away with lights, but those bastards are clever. Last time me and my team tried that, it moved away from our flashlight beams, changed its shape and took John with it. We haven't seen him since.

My advice? Get some gasoline, a good match, and torch the fuckers before they even get close.

THE TEMPLE SHOOK AGAIN, WITH EVERY WORD IT SAID.

REALITY UNRAVELED AS IT REARED ITS GRUESOME HEAD.

"THEIR MINDS WILL ALL FALL, YOUR SMARTEST AND YOUR WISE."

"YOUR SANITY IS A VEIL. YOUR COURAGE IS A GUISE."

THE BEAST'S CHILDREN REVEALED THEMSELVES, TAINTING ALL THAT HAD BEEN.

MONSTERS IN THE DARKNESS THAT WORE NOTHING BUT A GRIN.

...the smiler population has had an estimated 300% increase since the incident, with them going from primarily inhabiting the Main Nine to infesting hundreds of levels. Reports have even surfaced of them appearing in levels previously thought to be devoid of dangerous entities.

M.E.G. leadership has authorized the use of contingency plan FIREWALL in the event that this situation worsens.
