Entity 473.title: Entity 473
Entity 473.description:
The Living Grave, or "The Mass", as some people have begun to call it, is a recently discovered entity residing within Level 10.4, in which it appears to have repurposed a cellar into a nest of sorts. With any remaining structures of the original cellar being quite scarce. While the M.E.G.'s knowledge of this entity is still limited, we will do our best to continue research on The Living Grave in order to inform and educate wanderers about this strange, new creature in order to ensure their safety and security.

Given what little information we possess regarding The Living Grave, it seems that this entity could be best compared to siphonophores, deep sea creatures found in The Frontrooms that are made up of various organisms that work together to form one cohesive unit. The entity seems to be mostly comprised of various terrestrial animals found in The Frontrooms, which could provide a potential explanation for why it seemed for a time that only humans were capable of noclipping into The Backrooms. These animals have merged into a shapeless, graying mass connected by a network of large nerves and muscles. While the reason for all animals that noclip into The Backrooms to seemingly end up merging with The Living Grave is unknown, there is a likelihood that the entity possesses some form of influence on other levels outside of its own, luring animals into its habitat in order for them to assimilate with The Living Grave, though this only remains as speculation for the time being.

Entity 83.title: Entity 83
Entity 83.description:
Cone spiders are a clade of arthropods that can be found hunting throughout a wide variety of levels within the backrooms. Cone spiders have a large cone, resembling a man-made traffic cone, protecting their upper body, while their mandibles, 10 legs, and eyes are exposed. It is currently unknown how cone spiders receive their shell, as no juvenile specimens of the species have been discovered, and chemical analysis of these shells simply show polyvinyl chloride, the same material traffic cones on earth are made out of. Most species of cone spiders prefer dark and moist levels that at least have a 'natural' semblance to it such as looking like a forest. While forests are the general habitat of the cone spiders, due to their immense genetic diversity, many species prefer completely different habitats,

Theories exist that cone spiders have a home level due to the fact that no permanent and sustained population of cone spiders has been found, although no way to enter such a level has been discovered as of yet.

Entity 90.title: Entity 90
Entity 90.description:
Legoms Igasaleo, derogatorily known as "Nine-to-Fives," are abnormally large centipedes, with most of their species currently being held in captivity by the Auriellen government. They have the designation of Entity 90 in the global database.

Legoms Igasaleo, as previously stated, are anomalously large, usually up to 20 feet long and 5 feet tall. They are described as having a red-ish orange head with two large antennae and a dull black body with orange legs. Legoms Igasaleo have a faint glow, which is presumed to be caused by their diet of rocks and Object 15.
Entity 90.lead:
ENTITY 90 - LEGOMS IGASALEO

HABITAT
PROTECTED

AGGRESSION
NEUTRAL

INTELLECT
INTRICATE PATTERN RECOGNITION AND COMPLEX PROBLEM-SOLVING SKILLS

DANGER
LIFE THREATENING

Entity 18.title: Entity 18
Entity 18.description:
Entity 18, most typically alluded to as the Beast of Level 5 (albeit also less commonly known as the Beast of Terror Hotel or the Terror of Level 5), is an eldritch sapient creature lurking deep within Level 5. Though he is mostly considered a force of nature by many wanderers, being the supposed cause or even the embodiment of the constant unease and insanity permeating the Terror Hotel, the Beast does indeed have a physical form; taking the vaguely humanoid shape of a chimaeric conglomeration of human, octopus and deathmoth dressed in a furred black suit. Additionally, he is known to possess an anomalous camouflaging ability similar to those of regular octopi, allowing him to blend in so well with his surroundings that he is practically invisible to the mortal eye.

The Beast of Level 5 appears to possess godlike powers, able to make the hallways and chambers of Level 5 shift at his will. The architecture of the level has been known to even defy the laws of conventional physics at his command, with corridors looping back on each other to trap wanderers; entire sections of the hotel intersecting hallways at a perpendicular angle to block them off; and even walls closing in on wanderers and crushing them in some gruesome imitation of a hydraulic press. How exactly these satanic powers were acquired is left to speculation, though it is worth noting that the Beast only possesses his reality-warping powers in hotel levels such as the Abyss Inn, Level 188, and — of course — his home turf; in other levels, he is simply as dangerous as any demonic human-moth-octopus chimaera would be.

It is believed that the Beast is currently in possession of Level 5's level key.

Entity 32.title: Entity 32
Entity 32.description: There have been only a few documented encounters with the Anomalous in existence, as it almost always stays out of direct sight, so the exact physical appearance of the Anomalous is not definitively known. However, there have been many sources coming from different M.E.G. officials describing what the Anomalous looks like. These sources describe the Anomalous as a tall, humanoid-like creature with a disfigured face. Multiple wanderers have described the Anomalous as having "█████████████████," and some people who have encountered it has multiple arms, heads and legs, but the legitimacy of these claims is still being investigated and changes in appearance may be cause of hallucination and paranoia.
Entity 32.lead: The Anomalous, also known as Entity 32, is an entity that can appear anywhere in the Backrooms. It can usually be found following random explorers. Being close to this entity for extended periods can cause certain mental deteriorations, which can be severe.

Entity 43.title: Entity 43
Entity 43.description:
Nutcrackers are vicious entities that normally appear as regular Christmas nutcracker dolls from the Frontrooms. However, when they spot a wanderer, they will take on a more horrifying and dangerous form.

Nutcrackers vary in size, and while all Nutcrackers are dangerous and should be treated as such, the threat a Nutcracker poses to a wanderer is somewhat determined by its size; life-size Nutcrackers are far more dangerous and life-threatening than miniature Nutcrackers. The largest known possible height of a Nutcracker is 1.7 m (5.6 ft), approximately the size of an adult male, while the smallest recorded height is 11 cm (4.3 in).

Nutcrackers can be found in certain levels associated with Christmas, though not all Christmas-related levels harbor these entities. One should always be prepared for encounters with Nutcrackers in Christmas-themed levels. However, if one sees a nutcracker outside of a Christmas-related level, one should still avoid it unless absolutely necessary as a precaution. Subtly retreating is a good way to avoid Nutcrackers, though if the Nutcracker knows of one's awareness to its true nature, one should run away instead.

Entity 23.title: Entity 23
Entity 23.description:
Carbonations are mysterious anomalous events forming exotic lifeforms made of solely oxocarbons (Oxocarbons are compounds made of only carbon atoms and oxygen atoms), especially gaseous compounds such as carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The process of a Carbonation forming appears to consist of an unpredictable field affecting a space, and if said space were to contain a sufficient amount of oxocarbon compounds, they would cluster and be altered to allow the creation of a Carbonation.

Further study may need to be conducted on Level 2 and Level 28. There is an unusually high frequency of Carbonation manifestation events occurring in the spaces within these listed levels. It is most likely as a result of the machinery and piping present throughout either of these levels.

Entity 250.title: Entity 250
Entity 250.description:
Entity 250, better known as “The Hamster”, is a mainly passive entity which resides within The Delta Mall, roaming its corridors and stores and protecting the level. Its appearance is very similar to hamsters from The Frontrooms, although larger, somewhat more slender and often bipedal. Other striking features are its hidden fangs and its long, somewhat furry tail, which might help make it look like a ferret, a stoat or a chinchilla, confusing some wanderers a little.

Even though some think this entity is simply an enlarged version of conventional hamsters from The Frontrooms, it was discovered that it is intelligent at a human level and can even communicate with us through the Polish language. It was also found out it has teleportation abilities, which he uses to teleport itself, objects and other entities or wanderers.

Entity 47.title: Entity 47
Entity 47.description:
The Mushroom Maw or Fauxrana muscipula is an ambush predator from the amphibia class. This entity is known for its symbiotic relationship with the fungus Sarinocybe nervosa and its 2m (6.5ft) tall, bulbous mouth. A mushroom maw primarily lives its entire life dormant, underground; the tip of its upward-facing mouth resides just under the surface of the ground.

This entity is only known to live in wintery levels as they lure in prey with warm spots on the ground. These spots will be seemingly perfect circles of ground where snow and ice have melted, with white mushrooms protruding from the soil. Getting eaten by this entity is a sure death sentence due to its chemical emissions and properties; however, they can easily be avoided with caution.
Entity 47.lead: Fauxrana muscipula or commonly referred as a Mushroom Maw.

Entity 39.title: Entity 39
Entity 39.description:
Entity 39, also known as Mimicry,is a very dangerous being that most M.E.G operatives are unaware of. Only a small number of people who have met it in the endless corridors of the backrooms know about its existence. Each person sees Entity 39 differently, based on their own memories and experiences. Typically, it is described as a smoky figure with a disturbing face and thin, scattered hair.

Mimicry is a patient hunter that often lurks around windows and doors, waiting for unsuspecting wanderers to cross its path. Despite its weak physical form, it possesses unique abilities that make it a formidable opponent. It is said to be able to mimic the sounds of loved ones in The Frontrooms, luring its prey into a false sense of security before it strikes. When it detects a wanderer nearby, it will pursue them relentlessly, forcing them to run as far as they can. Those who fall victim to Mimicry will experience a fate worse than death as they are tortured in the most horrifying ways imaginable before being torn to shreds and wrapped in cocoons made of their own bones and internal organs. Mimicry will then consume their remains.

Although it is difficult to kill this entity, powerful weapons are capable of doing so. However, most weapons will merely phase through its body. Despite its fearsome reputation, Mimicry is not a fast entity, making it easier to outrun with one's own speed. Its large size can also be a disadvantage as it sometimes bumps into objects, giving wanderers extra time to find escape routes away from it. Nevertheless, its long body allows it to easily grab and ensnare its prey with its spindly fingers.
Entity 39.lead: "They could mimic any result of all reality."

Entity 16.title: Entity 16
Entity 16.description:
Undying

----

>General information

Entity 16, also known as "Undying," is an intelligent yet hostile tall humanoid creature. A method of killing has not yet been found for this creature. It is due to this very reason that they are named "Undying."

Undying are just as smart as humans. They are able to speak or, at least, pronounce a few words in foreign languages. They always appear to be on their guard (for reasons unknown), and so they always attack any moving and foreign object to them. That makes them extremely aggressive, and most interactions with an Undying specimen result in the explorer's death.

They typically roam alone or in small packs of up to five members, but never packs of more than ten. Undying are incredibly quick, but their eyesight is quite poor, so it is possible to outmaneuver them. They are quick, but incredibly easy to evade, as they cannot swim or climb.

Were it to attempt attack an Undying (a measure certainly not encouraged by any stretch), the most effective means of doing so would be to blast the head with solid objects such as metal pipe or other bludgeoning-type weapons. In typical fashion, though, should Undying take damage, it will go into a frantic rage, whereby it has super-strength and agility for a short time frame, rendering it an even more formidable opponent.

>Biology

There are three stages in the life of an Undying.

The first stage is the Root. The Root looks like a normal tree root, but it's made up of raw, living flesh. These tend to emerge from the ground or walls. This is the beginning stage of their life when they're newly "born." They're completely harmless now, but it's nearly impossible to hurt them at this point. They remain in this stage for 1 to 6 months.

The second phase is referred to as the Parasite. This is where the Root transforms into the form of a human body part—a head, hand, or leg. During this phase, the fundamental organ of an Undying is developed: the Parasite, from which this phase gets its name. The Parasite is always located in the original body part that had been developed. For example, if an adult Undying has its Parasite organ on its foot, this means the foot was the first thing it developed during the Parasite stage.

They cannot sense or move at this time. They only grow slowly, slowly forming a complete body. It takes anywhere from 4 months to 1 year for this stage.

The final stage is the Adult Undying. They are humanoid in form at this stage, typically 1.90 meters (6'3\") or similar in height. The distinction is that they are composed entirely of raw, living flesh with patches of dead skin. They may be missing typical organs, limbs, eyes, fingers, or other body parts, yet they will always possess a minimum of one major mutation that sets them apart from a typical human body.

During this stage, they behave as an adult Undying. They can move, speak, and feel emotions. This stage lasts between 6 to 8 years. The longest recorded life span of an Undying has been 8 years and 10 months.

>Relationships & Abilities

Contrary to other beings, Entity 16 occasionally engages in companionship with Entity 8. They have been seen communicating with each other and, at times, staging cooperative attacks to chase their quarry. Because Undyings, with all their heightened speed and acrobatic maneuverability, can only continue short attacks, they depend on assistance from other entities—namely Hounds—to hunt more effectively.

As explained above, Undyings are capable of conversing in most human languages, mostly English and Spanish. However, their speech is unreliable and unsystematic; they speak at random with empty words or senseless phrases and occasionally attempt to build sentences, albeit these are mostly unintelligible. What must be noted is that they barely do this, and it is not worth the attempt to converse with them as this will prove futile when confronted face-to-face and may at best alert them to the explorer's presence.

The Undying's reproduction process is entirely asexual. It entails an adult Undying selecting a surface—primarily the ground or a wall—to be on which it will be completely still. The creature might sit or lie down on this surface for one to two months, transforming into a dormant-like quality and demonstrating no apparent responsiveness to stimuli.

By the time the Undying gets up again, a Root Offspring will have developed at the position where it remained behind. This new creature, in its Root Stage, is quite "exposed." Due to this, the fully-grown Undying automatically protects and cares for the Root, letting it develop until it is in the Parasite Stage.

Once the offspring is in the Parasite Phase, the adult Undying abandons it to develop by itself, without further assistance. After that, the new animal must complete development on its own.

>Attachment

>Open Addendum  >Close Addendum

16/5/2003

In recent days, A.A.E.G. has received numerous reports regarding unusual behaviour among Undyings. These behaviours include attempts to communicate with humans, self-harm, and even signs of self-awareness.

If you have any additional information regarding this phenomenon, please report it immediately to Gabriel, head of the entity division at A.A.E.G..

----

----

Entity 38.title: Entity 38
Entity 38.description: The Needlelimb is a tall, black, bony figure that has only two limbs: one arm and one leg. It does not have a foot, but instead the Needlelimb floats and has five long, thin fingers, that resemble needles attached to its arm. Its head is large, long, and slim, and it is faceless. Some of them have small spikes on their back, some don't. Its body is built crooked and the texture of its skin is moist. It gives off the smell of rotten bodies. Needlelimbs feed on people's consciousness or souls through their fingers and leave the bodies in a pile. These entities are very rare and are almost extinct. The most frequent places for Needlelimbs are on Level 1 and Level 0.8.

Travelers of Stars.title: Travelers of Stars
Travelers of Stars.description:
Travelers are an advanced race of entities possessing powerful reality-bending properties. They are distinguishable by their lavish suits of armor.

They consider themselves part of the "Outsiders," a group of beings originating from the aptly named "Outside," in a sense, they do not belong to the Backrooms. Having recently arrived, they are actively exploring various levels and interacting with many wanderers along the way.

Unlike any typical entities, they are considered as "alien" wanderers and are often welcomed due to their properties when fending off hostile entities and navigating dangerous levels for the wanderers.

It is believed that they are the architects of the Palace and the manufacturers of the Golden Artifacts. Though, those creations are attributed to their ancestors.

They are the enemies of the Marauders.

Entity 70.title: Entity 70
Entity 70.description:
Homo callidus, which roughly means "clever man" or "a clever man" in Latin, are a species of now-extinct primate, categorized within the genus Homo, that were likely introduced into The Backrooms approximately 2 million years ago, right around the end of the Tertiary period of The Frontroom's Cenozoic era, which lasted from 66 million years ago to 2 million years ago.

The nature of this event is unknown, but it is believed that the alignment of an entire N-dimensional boundary along the cross-section of a large island could not have been a mere coincidental occurrence. What entity or entities whom may have initiated this process, and for what purpose is entirely unknown. Based on carvings and simple illustrations dating back to this period, this event caused a species-wide catastrophe of biblical proportions, leading to their civilization, possibly one that utilized bronze and even iron implements, to entirely collapse back into savagery and regressing the technological advancement of the Callidians to that of the early Neolithic. Nevertheless, Homo callidus seemingly managed to successfully colonize a vast portion of The Backrooms, with signs of Homo callidus having been found to have mostly inhabited Level 110, though despite their success in being able to settle into The Backrooms, it seems that the species was living on borrowed time. Judging by the condition of the preserved remains of Homo callidus, a combination of dwindling resources and the increasing numbers of hostile entities eventually led to the extinction of Homo callidus, which seems to have occurred approximately 100,000 to 150,000 years ago.
Entity 70.lead: It seems as if we were not the first, not alone.

Entity PC.title: Entity PC
Entity PC.description:
Entity PC, also known as "The End's Computer", "The Computer", or simply "A Computer", is the computer located on top of the desk of The End. The physical appearance of The Computer is simple, resembling a 1990s computer running randomly between Windows 95, Windows 98, and Windows XP throughout visits. Notably, it is the only computer that runs an actual Frontrooms operating system, a one-to-one copy, although it includes the BACK-NET program accompanying Internet Explorer in the Programs menu. The computer has been known to be able to change the physical appearance, safety, and overall structure of the level, being able to change some of the most recognizable levels into a desert-like landscape where wanderers will be confused as to where to continue. This makes it significantly harder for wanderers who map levels to publicly share information, as it may end up changing because of this. However, level changes are extremely uncommon and rarely happen, and three cases of this have been reported to M.E.G; the levels affected were Level 11, Level 37 and Level 94.

The Computer will have the blue background that is default with Windows 9x installations (or the Windows XP "Bliss" wallpaper if running XP), although, in the Control Panel, a series of backgrounds that switch between levels zero through nine every 15 minutes can be found; one is not able to customize the slideshow, since attempting to do that will cause an error, which reads: "You're not an administrator". Accessing all apps in which computer settings can be changed will be allowed for the most part but not be able to save, except the MS-DOS Prompt, which is also known as COMMAND.COM. Even though it still has some restricted commands, one can read text on a BACK-NET website, even though the connection inside of The End is very slow as it is restricted to dial-up speeds. You can't install or access websites that involve chatting to try to ask for help, and their apps are not blocked from the computer. Any social media that can allow one to call for help will be blocked from the computer with no way of being able to reach out to those in The Frontrooms. This is, but not limited to: comment sections.

The computer has some pre-installed apps, which include: Internet Explorer, BACK-NET, M.E.G File Database (Accessing is only available as user rank), Control Panel, Word 2000, Powerpoint 2000, Paint, Acrobat Reader, Calculator, a few Windows video games from the 1990s, Mail (Accessing with user8327423423@bck.com, one cannot send emails but access them), "Webcam" (One cannot take photos, there is no webcam), Java, Sound Recorder (There is no microphone), and all the other programs bundled with Windows 9x or XP. New programs apart from this list get installed and uninstalled when you enter, so it could include Photoshop and a few other programs typical for the time when you come, but next time you come it would disappear, with Microsoft Plus! and Microsoft Plus! for Kids being installed.

Wanderers have a theory that goes that the computer seemingly has control and/or access to everything and all levels, entities, and objects in the backrooms, with some even believing that it is the long-saught-after core of the Backrooms itself. This leads to them thinking that it's somehow "interconnected" to the Backrooms, and it also appears that The Computer is autonomous, meaning that it can self-govern and place files,

The Computer works similarly to an ordinary computer; it supports lots of music, videos, and extras. Except that it may be somewhat slow due to the ancient specifications, the years the computer has been there, however, it seems that the files and settings a user has persisted through visits, although another user has its own configuration. So if user A saves a word document on the desktop, user B and everyone else will not see it nor have access to any trace of it, or vice versa.

The Computer has files that it may sometimes rearrange around, even when a wanderer is looking for files. These files can range from gibberish text to actual history about The End, making it easier for explorers to find information about The End and determine easier ways to escape. However, the computer has no mentions of a possible exit to the Backrooms, meaning that it hints at the Backrooms not having an escape at all. The Computer has no way to speak to a human with a voice or telepathically. However, it can talk via files, where it sends files almost instantly to a wanderer, almost knowing everything that is happening around it.

H.K. (Count the Stars!).title: H.K. (Count the Stars!)
H.K. (Count the Stars!).description:
H.K. is a digital manifestation that publishes customer reviews, detailing anecdotal accounts and experiences allegedly had in certain levels. Providing commentary on various aspects of the levels such as their quality, hazards, and other features, these reviews are typically posted on forums of websites that are accessible within the Backrooms. However, it has been recorded that H.K. also has the power to modify the online documentation of the levels themselves, thereby inserting its own reviews into these pages for viewers to read.

While H.K.'s intelligence is evident from its abilities, the nonsensical nature of some of its stories contradicts this level of intelligence. Some have posited that a programmed algorithm capable of mimicking human-like speech is responsible for the entity's activities, lacking any true consciousness or autonomy. The writing style of H.K.'s reviews is characterized by a blend of formal and informal language, which is focused on providing objective observations of levels. It occasionally alternates between professional and informal tones with perfect grammar, but handfuls of errors including misplaced punctuations, contradictory statements, and occasional misspellings of specialized vocabulary terms can still be detected.

Eerily, the reviews published by H.K. have shown the ability to prefigure alterations to the mentioned level's environment. Some hypothesize that this remarkable prescience may indicate an ability on predicting the future, or alternatively, that it is actively causing these alterations itself.
H.K. (Count the Stars!).lead:

Entity 10.title: Entity 10
Entity 10.description:
Skin Stealers, bearing the scientific name Homo capgras and classified as Entity 10, are a species of predatory beings with polymorphic capabilities. Common in levels with significant human and/or other sapient presence, they have posed a notable threat to human civilization within the Backrooms.

Skin Stealers are solo hunters, often employing regular use of suggestion and shapeshifting to lure out prey or groups of prey for consumption/assimilation. The population of Skin-Stealers remains unknown, though efforts from the AEA have consistently thinned down the population.
Entity 10.lead:
⇊ click to continue. ⇊

"Alright, time to see if any of you are Skin-Stealers."

A man, around 6 feet tall and visibly malnourished, aimed a gun at the other twenty people in the cramped warehouse room. Pacing throughout the chamber, his hand shook with anxiety even with the powerful firearm he wielded. For many here, the tension was suffocating.

For Micheal, the man holding the gun, it was the weekly check.

Walking slowly towards a young boy, he brandished a makeshift knife made of rusty metal. Throwing him on the floor, Micheal put his boot on the kid's arm to restrain him, inching closer towards his hand with the knife. Raising his hand to make a swing, he slashed at the boy's hand, causing it to bleed out.

Red blood. The child would live today.

Micheal repeated the process with the others. Ten people in, and all were still human so far. The next one up was a woman, around 50 years old. She had arrived at the colony a couple weeks back. Apparently, she'd been part of some big group out there in the Backrooms, until one day she returned to her colony after a resource gathering mission and everyone had disappeared.

Repeating the process once more with the others, Micheal noticed a beige shape moving towards him in a flash, along with a sharp pain in his back. In an instant, he turned around and shot at the attacker-

BANG! BANG! BANG!

Three bodies lay on the floor, along with the corpse of his attacker: another frail man not much unlike him, armed with yet another makeshift knife. The others must've caught a stray bullet. Micheal checked the bullet wound on the person who had assaulted him. Red blood; he was still a human.

"Looks like Skin-Stealers aren't the only traitors around here", Micheal said with a smirk on his face, turning around to the young boy from before. "You were his kid, right? Now all of you remember, these rules are all to keep us safe and happy. You like being safe and happy, right?"

Raising his pistol towards the child, Micheal continued.

"Now let this be an example of what happens when someone gets the idea that they're above my rules."

BANG

One last shot rang through the room.

Red blood.

Entity 8.title: Entity 8
Entity 8.description: Entity 8 (Hounds), are hostile canid entities which inhabit sparsely populated regions of The Backrooms, particularly within damp and derelict levels. Despite exhibiting canid traits, Hounds are genetically similar to humans, sharing multiple biotic attributes, as well as critical thinking and the use of rudimentary tools, among other things. In addition, their unique pelt is sought after by numerous groups due to being one of the only sources of leather in the entire Backrooms.
Entity 8.lead: One of the first recorded images of a Hound, discovered within a heavily damaged Kodak camera in an undisclosed level.

The Wanderer.title: The Wanderer
The Wanderer.description:
The Wanderer, is very hard to explain. It is merely a myth, nothing but superstition. But somehow it became real. It was rumored to be a placeholder of the M.E.G database that somehow became "sentient". But even after countless research, we could not find a single trace of "The Wanderer". The Wanderer doesn't exist. It's unknown, nobody knows why or how this entity exists. The entity is nothing, nonexistent, or is it?

(Update: I think I found out what The Wanderer actually is.)

The Wanderer's physical appearance is unknown. It is completely invisible. However, it is still sentient. It tricks wanderers into believing it doesn't exist and infects their minds with doubt and denial.

WARNING! DATA CORRUPTED!

Restoring Data...

Welcome, .

The Wanderer, also known as "Gary" is an extremely powerful and enigmatic individual who is secretive about all his private information. Not many people know about this entity, as he is only rarely seen wandering around the halls of the Backrooms. He doesn't reveal much about his backstory; only a few people have seen him, although all those people have been reported dead. He is a considerably omnipotent entity, knowing everything about the Backrooms and having many powerful abilities. If you have the chance to talk or interact with him, do not. He will blabber continuously about alternate realities and then he will kill you to hide his secrets. He has a bunch of henchmen whom he calls "Weaklings". Rumors say that he is planning to use his henchmen to take over the Backrooms. However, his intentions remain unknown for now.

The Wanderer resembles a normal adult male in his 20s, and he is approximately 182 centimeters tall. He dons an exceptionally dark red shirt with navy pants and has shoes that contain mysterious symbols on them. Attempts to decode these symbols have all failed. His hair is straight and spiky, and half of his face is completely gone, showing the flesh and bones on the left side of his face. This is considered his "actual" appearance. However, no evidence that can support this statement, as he can shapeshift into any wanderer or entity. He blends into groups of wanderers until he is ready to strike, much like The King of Normality, which he'll then kill the group of wanderers he blends in. Always stay cautious, because he could be blending into your group or colony right now. Soon, you won't even know what hit you.

The Wanderer has only mentioned his backstory to one other person: The King of Normality. Luckily, The King of Normality was willing to share this information with the M.E.G., so we were able to document his backstory. The King of Normality says that he's the first person to have ever entered the Backrooms (or so he claims to be), but he got corrupted by an unknown being, which King of Normality described as "something that is pure evil". He soon somehow glitched out of reality itself. He was tortured, and chained up by his soul until he finally escaped using his remaining power and energy. When he got out, he only had half of his face, and once he saw it, he turned insane. He went on a murderous rampage, killing every human or entity that was in his way, and presumably went to the fourth dimension after he had gained satisfaction from his killing. He continues to stay in the fourth dimension, monitoring the stability of the Backrooms and the people inside them. Although he still has violent tendencies, he rarely kills people nowadays.

The Wanderer has shown amazing power, so most people believe he is one of the most powerful entities in the Backrooms. He has displayed many different unique abilities, such as traveling through different dimensions, killing people with a slight touch, controlling entities to attack wanderers, etc. He frequently brags to his peers about having killed over a thousand people attempting to get to a highly guarded dimension. However, he almost died in the process and had to retreat. This entity is highly dangerous, so if you encounter this entity make sure to stay out of its way, and be silent.

Entity 24.title: Entity 24
Entity 24.displayTitle: R e f u s e
Entity 24.description: Refuse refers to entities within the Backrooms that resemble dark, humanoid figures seemingly frozen in a grieving pose. They do not move at all and, from afar, appear to resemble statues or mannequins. These entities exhibit highly volatile behaviors and can cause deadly bursts of energy capable of disabling all electric devices within a 2-mile radius. As such, it is highly recommended that wanderers stay away from these entities at all costs.

Entity 230.title: Entity 230
Entity 230.description:
Entity 230 appears as a wolf or a large dog. It has two variants: the wolf of darkness and the wolf of light, which are responsible for killing or caring for wanderers. This entity can be found in a large number of levels, except those that block entities. It has been observed to prefer levels in urban areas or open places.

Its senses are highly developed, more than any canine or feline in the frontrooms, except for its vision. It has been seen how its sense of smell, hearing, and touch have been able to detect its surroundings with great precision, which helps it have better control of the environment and choose the best possible option.

The Cadejo possess unique abilities. They can block the entrances and exits of the level they are in, although they are limited to a certain number of people or a specific person. They can block no-clipping within a 50-kilometer radius at will.

It can pass through solid objects at will, which prevents it from receiving or inflicting physical damage while this ability is active. Only the Cadejo's counterpart can harm it in this state, which occurs during battles that have been recorded. They have also been seen to walk at great speed on completely vertical surfaces or ceilings.

The Cadejo can perceive the emotions of all living beings around it, along with their memories. The entity can see the memories of everyone present at that moment, allowing it to understand the intentions and nature of the people it encounters, and thus, it decides whether to hunt or help, depending on the type of Cadejo it is.

The Cadejo has strong limbs that allow it to reach high speeds; when it runs, its paws are extremely silent, making it practically undetectable. Its fur also makes it nearly invisible to various forms of detection used by humans. Its powerful jaw generates extremely high temperatures when it bites, resulting in burns that, in some cases, can be as severe as third or fourth-degree burns. It can regenerate quickly within a few hours, including the regeneration of limbs and organs. The entity can revive once defeated after a few hours.

The dark Cadejo possesses a unique ability that its counterpart does not: the creation of auditory and visual illusions, such as the sounds of children, cries for help, environmental noises, and others, which it often uses to confuse its prey. On the other hand, the light Cadejo can illuminate its fur, and when this occurs, the effects on sanity and hallucinations decrease.

The light Cadejo can make its fur shine, which helps maintain sanity and eliminate hallucinations in the area while it is nearby. This is usually used when the place is dark or when the level has properties that affect sanity.

The entity has two appearances, but both share the characteristic of being a large canine with strong, long limbs, as well as the ability to change its traits. It improves all aspects of conventional dogs, including intelligence. The entity measures 230 cm and has an average weight of 92 kilos, this being the maximum size that both forms can reach. Its skin is immune to weapons and fire.

The dark Cadejo manifests with black fur and red eyes. It has also been noted that it never changes its appearance, unlike its counterpart. This wolf has been observed to act with great aggression, ambushing its prey gradually. When this occurs, its fur becomes camouflage.

The light Cadejo, on the other hand, manifests with white fur and blue eyes. It has been seen to change its appearance to smaller dogs or a less threatening version for the wanderers it usually seeks. It has also been observed that its fur can shine if it wishes, which it usually uses to help wanderers navigate in the dark.

The entity prefers dark and gloomy places when appearing. It has been seen to tend to appear in the colder months.

Maria.title: Maria
Maria.description:
Maria Holdings, often simply referred to as Maria, is a female nomadic faceling child of about seven years old, who has often been observed roaming the Backrooms by many wanderers. She has a single eye, dark hair and an incomplete mouth, and wears monochrome clothing. Apparently, she presents with an anomalous lack of biological ageing; since 1997, witness accounts of Maria have asserted that she does not seem to mature with the years.

Alongside her apparently eternal biological youth, Maria does not seem to have aged mentally beyond the capacity of a seven-year-old either. She expresses frequent curiosity towards humans, other facelings, and glowfolk, and often follows wanderers simply to observe their actions. In addition, she has often been known to play practical jokes on wanderers, including writing on their faces or clothing, or stacking their belongings into tower-like structures. Although she is not known to have exhibited aggression towards humans, caution is nonetheless advised, as child facelings are known to attack out of fear or self-defence.

Maria's voicebox is not fully dysfunctional; she has been known to utter full words and short phrases under some circumstances, which she presumably learned from her father. Personal details which she has revealed through writing include that she hates citrus fruits and counter-entities, loves sweet foods, and has an odd case of globophobia which she puts down to "the sound of [balloons] popping".

Despite her seeming immaturity, Maria appears to be quite experienced in navigating the Backrooms and dealing with its myriad dangers alone. She is known to leave behind childish drawings in dangerous levels to warn wanderers of hazards; while most if not all of these provide a reasonable warning to wanderers, it is important to remember that she may overlook or mislabel certain hazards due to her entitic nature. Provided below is a gallery of Maria's sketches.

Entity 500.title: Entity 500
Entity 500.description:
Entity 500, better known as Blood Marrow, is a deadly yet elusive type of entity that is most commonly found throughout higher-numbered levels of the Backrooms and is more often than not encased within surreal formations made of bone. Not much is known about the entity itself due to the danger it poses to any nearby human life and its tendency to stay within its bone cocoons for long periods.

While much more deadly when out of its bone cocoons, Blood Marrow nonetheless inflicts nearby wanderers with various anomalous effects when in said cocoons. It has been speculated that the bone cocoons themselves give the entity the ability to exert these effects on wanderers, though as no one has thus far survived a confrontation with the Blood Marrow itself, this is unconfirmed.

The Alchemist.title: The Alchemist
The Alchemist.description:
The Alchemist is an entity PoI with strong alchemical abilities that bears a resemblance to a human, albeit set apart by distinct and eccentric attributes. His face is somewhat similar to that of a Partial Faceling, in that unsettlingly smooth skin replaces his eyes beneath his glasses. His ears are distinctly pointed, reminiscent of an elf's rather than a human's. His ghastly white complexion and salt-and-pepper beard contrasts with his toothy grin, beaming along with the slight golden sheen that his skin exhibits in direct light.

His attire resembles that of a scientist's, a peculiar white lab coat. It's made of loose fabric that gathers at the cuffs and is fastened with golden buttons. It is cinched at the waist with a practical brown belt and a gilded buckle.

The Alchemist's coat pocket most commonly includes two items: a pen, and a magnifying glass. These items signify the Alchemist's curiosity and propensity for science, though only the pen is used by him frequently.
